title: Senator Barajas Hotel
address: C. del Galeón, 27, Barajas, 28042 Madrid
descriptions: Casual rooms in a laid-back hotel with a restaurant, a cafe/bar & a seasonal outdoor pool.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Keith Sumner","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJSG9sS74uo6M6hmvqdYN0NOgYhH8kC8kwM-5DhjWRE6Hditg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Rooms are nice. One of the things that I did not like was all the people smoking at the front entrance.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Trip.com Member","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QeCck1CfANpZm9bBk3PHtP97Kh9k25HZfvq9yAWlN3BaIyjSHUPEfWkhAJmuYiJsH-_6g=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Free shuttle bus from the airport. €5 per person to go back to the airport from the hotel with the earliest shuttle at 5am.Good enough to spend over night stay. Pizza restaurant on the ground floor wad so good! True italian styled!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Giorgio Mangeruca","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIfKFyVVLezhHRvyNcclEx8yo6TgzZF9YdJkeWgQXIrSYGynQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Unfortunately, my stay at the hotel was far from satisfactory.
I had to request a room change three times before finding one where it was possible to have a decent stay.
In the first room, there was a strong sewage smell.
Despite that, I tried flushing the toilet and decided to simply close the bathroom door - I managed to sleep fairly well.
However, the next morning I woke up to the same unpleasant smell, only much stronger.
I explained the situation to the reception, where the staff were very kind and professional, and they arranged for a room change upon my return to the hotel.
When I entered the new room, luckily there was no bad smell, but as you can see from the photos, the overall condition of the room — especially the cleanliness of the bathroom — was definitely not up to the standard of a four-star hotel, or even a basic one.
As soon as I noticed these issues, I went back to the reception to request another room change, showing them the photos as well. Once again, they were polite and professional, and they did their best to find an alternative even offering me a complimentary upgrade to an executive room.
Unfortunately, as soon as I entered the third room, the same nauseating sewage smell appeared again. At this point, I believe the hotel has a serious plumbing issue on one side of the building, because although the rooms were on different floors, they were in the same location.
Once again, I informed the reception about this major issue. This time, however, they seemed a bit annoyed by my complaints.
As I told the receptionist:
when someone books a room in a four-star hotel, they expect a four-star experience.
Unfortunately, that was not the case for me.
The fourth and final room was finally livable, and I was able to spend the rest of my stay in a decent way.
I'm honestly very disappointed and disheartened by the overall cleanliness and the state of the infrastructure. I don't think l'Il be returning to this hotel.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD8wg8tnruoZZvULl8MfeF-&fid=0x0:0xc07008176be84901"],"When":"2025-11-9"},{"Name":"kdgAiken","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QdGDJ6WBs5CBoNVcQAwShh25kntoFa0rN3szxhkmmJHnXagNPuSfO1BRpzbnKc5wyCe_g=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"The hotel is very dated, but also very clean.  It is well located, close to the airport.  We enjoyed the bar/restaurant and the bartender was very helpful and friendly.  The furniture in the room is is dated but had good linens and towels.  The shuttle service was efficient and on time (it does cost 5 dollars per person). This hotel is a good choice for an affordable one night stay close to the airport.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Will De Jager","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLhTh5di6s9TdnaNQ4ZqNUkJQiRnqIyvvYD3LlpTXXRjbd_-g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"After staying two out of six nights in the  Senator Barajas Hotel, my impressions. It is close to the airport. If you travel light, you can walk within thirty minutes to terminal 1, 2 or 3 crossing the road over a food bridge. I checked in on line and got the keycard to room 539 quickly. The staff seems professional and speaks decent English, so I didn't have to use the limited Spanish I learned in school many years ago. But if you want to call reception from your room (704) nobody ever bothers to pick up the phone. The room is large and clean enough. It has a balcony (without table or chairs), a safe and a fridge with two bottles of water in it upon arrival. The bottles are not replaced with new ones after using them, but a Mercadona Supermarket is within a five minute walk. The bed is comfortable, but the pillows are very thin and soft. I travel alone and put the two on top of each other. There is daily housekeeping. The bed is made, but the floor is not vacuumed, leaving hairs and dust. The bathroom is small, but adequate with and in bath shower. The hotel is within a ten minute walking distance of a metro station giving access to line 5, fifteen stations and approximately thirty minutes to the centre of Madrid (Gran Via). There are plenty of restaurants and bars in the immediate area. The hotel has a pool outside, so to cold in October and a gym. The restaurants and bar in the hotel are overpriced. But if you walk five minutes, you pay half the price for the same food and drinks. The two main issues are the Wi-Fi and the poor insulation. There is  no Wi-Fi signal in room 539. I have to use roaming and 4G/5G to connect my devices. Not a problem for a citizen from the EU with a large bundle, but very expensive if not covered by the contract in your home country. And even basic and cheap insulation like a door hinges to prevent room doors from slamming shut is missing. The walls are paper thin, so you can constantly hear other guests and the hotel is frequently visited by tour buses with alarms sounding when they back-up in parking in the evening arriving and early morning departing. A lot of groups of tourists use this hotel for one night and they are are not quiet, shouting in the corridor and slamming their doors until well after midnight and again from 6 am onwards. So, even with earplugs you will be lucky to get some sleep. Upon arrival, I requested a quiet room and I got a room facing the steet on a floor with a lot of loud touring people staying for one night and cars and buses pushing their claxon and sounding there alarms. Now, these problems are known to management and addressed in a lot of previous reviews. But if the management only replies to these reviews by apologising and doesn't follow this apology up with even taking the most basic measures, like installing cheap door hinges to prevent doors from slamming shut, these replies are useless and apologies empty. There is no need for a big renovation. With a little investment you can tackle some of the noise issues and guarantee guests a beter nights sleep. Not doing so, shows a lack of interest and leads to bad reviews. For the price paid, more thant €100 a night, guests are allowed to expect more. The price-quality ratio just isn't right. In a hotel being able to get a good nights sleep is the most important thing. A shame really, because with a little effort from the management this really has the potential to be the four star hotel they are claiming to be. For now, three stars is as far as I can go and even then I am being generous.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBk1laWCMFKQ5KYP1ZBBl6n&fid=0x0:0xc07008176be84901"],"When":"2025-10-24"},{"Name":"Ελευθερια Φουστερη","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJOuoHdmmGCuhkO8UrFFdXOWMbqzYx6xrL96dBBkCvCl4niMg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"The hotel is very old, despite being advertised as a four-star property. There is a strong sewer smell upon entering, and the reception service is poor, with rude staff and limited English. The rooms also smell unpleasant, especially when the air conditioning is on.The only positive aspect of the hotel is the breakfast, which is fairly varied. The swimming pool, although advertised, is very small and was closed during our stay.Overall, the hotel offers a poor experience and does not meet four-star standards.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Trip.com Member","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QeCck1CfANpZm9bBk3PHtP97Kh9k25HZfvq9yAWlN3BaIyjSHUPEfWkhAJmuYiJsH-_6g=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"A very decent hotel within a short taxi/uber ride to the airport. There are a supermarket and a few restaurants with 10 minute walk. The neighbourhood is clean and safe. The only negatives are that the check-in procedure is unreasonably long, and there were large tour groups that were making a lot of noise in the corridor, and were leaving all at once with loads of luggage occupying all available lifts. Also, very oddly there weren't any USB plugs in the room. The bar fridge wasn't working, but they promptly sent a technician to fix it.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Eduardo Silveira","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXCUEhNlp6Jy7Mm35BF77pqDYcI66cTqSxqhsainJIIacciBszy=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I've had an initial bad experience with the hotel but after reaching out to the Hotel Group they were really cordial and fast on solutionating our problem.
Now I updated this review to leave my appreciation on their effort and let you know tha you can count on them on any demand.

I can assure the Hotel have worked hard to help me and I recommend this hotel and I hope to stay here again on another occasion.","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: Hotel Avenue Madrid
address: Av. de Aragón, 362, San Blas-Canillejas, 28022 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Mario Garcia Martin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKwMb2w-ziIsNOkdhnQua9U8qP8VVUez9vA5HFCIt-n5T5wEw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Reserve para pasar la nochevieja 12 horas, previamente por whatsap. Al llegar, el numero de reserva no existe pero teniamos la habitacion correctamente. Buena limpieza, suite escultura bonita y decorado como le pedi.
Se oyen ruidos de puertas. Al salir del hotel, hay que llamar a recepcion y estuvimos llamando mas de 30 min para que nos atendieran y poder salir. Un poco caro, aunque buena privacidad.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Ian García Pacheco","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIzf2SSW9xCmeka5tunntb0d6yDN8cmI2S3cW24u4UWGEtabw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Hemos tenido una muy mala experiencia.

Al llegar no funcionaba el código de la reserva, nos hacen esperar en la puerta del parking 30 minutos (la reserva era a las 22:00 y nos abrieron a las 22:30).

Para variar el jacuzzi no funcionaba y no estaba limpio. Estuvimos esperando 1 hora h media al llegar para que se encendiese el jacuzzi , cuando se llenó vimos que no estaba limpio y había pelos por todas partes. Bastante mal.

Al día siguiente estuvimos esperando 3 horas para poder utilizar el jacuzzi de nuevo. Desde las 16:00 hasta las 19:00 no nos activaban el jacuzzi, muy frustrante para el precio que cuesta.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"ainara parriga hijes","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJTzpc1PFNjVEutRMsUtzbBMhC9-lxPBJY27Js0IcSCBlgf1w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Un poco decepcionada tienen mucho q aprender de loob o zouk la verdad, no tienes tarjeta para entrar , no hay una simple neverita, la limpieza bueno teníamos la carta pegada y no me quiero imaginar de que sería, las almohadas malísimas, la zona del jacuzzi olía a humedad q echa para atrás todo el suelo empapado y con cuidadito si no te matabas ahí, el jacuzzi no se vaciaba y tarda mucho en calentarse y escuchabas a los vecinos, y el desayuno pagado? Tostadas con tomate de pan Bimbo en serio? Y un capuchino….. en fin creo q no volveré y el servicio tarda mucho en traer las cosas, para el dinero q es deja mucho q desear la verdad!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jaime Andrés Giraldo Botero","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWVS6vMVh2kJRb-DgLNpPBnuQ_yxDr-R07o268bBAhsb6y_inHY=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Experiencia extremadamente decepcionante y confusa – Publicidad claramente engañosa

Reservé con el # 5.2.3.1.9.5.5.5.2.5 un alojamiento que Booking describe como Hotel Avenue – Lovely Hotel. Mi experiencia en este establecimiento fue profundamente frustrante. Reservé con antelación confiando en la información publicada, donde se presenta como Hotel . Sin embargo, al llegar con mi padre descubrimos que no se trataba de un hotel, sino de un m.o.t.e.l , situación que jamás fue informada de manera clara ni visible en la plataforma.

El nivel de ambigüedad en su identificación es desconcertante: incluso la letra que aparenta ser una “M” en el aviso físico del lugar no corresponde a una “M” tradicional, lo que genera aún más confusión para cualquier huésped que llegue buscando un hotel convencional.

Al expresar nuestra incomodidad, se nos indicó que simplemente canceláramos la reserva. Lo hicimos de inmediato… pero el establecimiento se negó a devolvernos los 800 euros pagados, argumentando que “estaba claro” que eran un motel, pese a que su propia presentación NO lo indica de manera explícita ni transparente.

Como viajero frecuente, jamás había enfrentado una situación en la que la descripción comercial fuera tan contradictoria y en la que el establecimiento desestimara una solicitud legítima de reembolso, sabiendo que la experiencia real no coincidía con lo ofrecido.

Recomiendo a futuros viajeros revisar minuciosamente este alojamiento, ya que la falta de claridad en cómo se promocionan puede llevar a confusiones graves y pérdidas económicas injustificadas.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"angel yañez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKbdBSXS2c2UqRv9hRCe16SwMi3jbjDDiBqLHFBYXny1kAelQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Es la segunda vez que visitamos el """"hotel"""" el marketing engañoso prefiero dejarlo para otro día... El caso es que realizamos una petición de reserva por whatsapp, al no responder decidimos llamar y pedir la reserva por teléfono, nos dan el ok de la suite que pedimos nos indican que hemos tenido suerte y que queda una ultima y que si no llegamos a la hora de la reserva en 15 min nos la quintan y que nos responderán al whatsapp con el numero de reserva, hasta ahí todo bien y normal.

Cuando llegamos nos paramos en la pantalla nos pide el numero de reserva que obviamente jamás mandaron, bueno no pasa nada llamamos por teléfono y le indicamos que esta pasando y de primeras nos dice que no tenemos reserva... le indicamos que hemos hablado por whats app y por teléfono con la compañera y que nos había confirmado dicha reserva y de la nada nos dice ah si si tienen! pero es la que tiene piscina... le indicamos que no que además en los mensajes esta detallado que queríamos una suite X con jacuzzi, la mujer muy mal educada lejos de dar una solución nos dijo que es lo que había que si lo queríamos bien y si no "pues dese la vuelta" con las mismas y sus buenas formas eso hicimos darnos la vuelta, y recomendar a todo el mundo que lea esta reseña que hay mejores suites por Madrid que este """"Hotel"""" y con personas mucho mas educadas que esta gente.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tian W","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVFSa-mS53ukXu_DA1xHnh78IK5pcrMKDGBVzk31JN3yXXRcKxH=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Ha sido una experiencia muy buena en general, le faltan algunas cosas como podría ser caja fuerte y nevera. title: Hostelfly
address: C. de la Caldera, 11, Barajas, 28042 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Louise Mulhern","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWZFPgrT3TS77nxXQiN4NlSbi8e2Xm-K8jMBmP9wNYBZwIeu392LQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"5 min cab from the airport, room was clean bed was very comfy,set up of rooms was great. Locker under your bed for suitcase, had a private shower and toilet in the room so only used by the people in the dorm.  Vending machines,microwave etc in lobby.  Nice patio area outside with a small pool.  Stayed as a stop over due to change of flight would highly recommend for this or if trying to visit on a budget","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Julia Hall","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUCOrigYTcb29-Z-GXp4N9BRoOa-sSF46bhYd0S6eCVVP7pjZGDgA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Clean, secure, and walking distance from Terminal 2/3

24-hour check-in was friendly and spoke English, which is so helpful when you're exhausted in between stages of travel

I took Bolt from Chamartin train station - at midnight on a Wednesday it took less than 20 minutes and costed around €16

They provide sheets/pillow/duvet on the bunk and a large towel for showering.

I walked to Terminal 2 around 6am using walking directions from Google maps. There isn't anyone else on the street at this hour and I didn't feel any sense of concern about the safety of my surroundings. It took me 20 minutes to reach the security checkpoint in Terminal 2. I travel with a backpack so the walk was easy, with rolling luggage it would be a little more annoying but doable if you're determined.

Shower and toilet (separate rooms) are attached to the dormitory so it's possible that other travelers might make noise while you're sleeping. My roommates were very respectful  and I enjoyed a few hours of sleep before my flight.

There is a common area and kitchen on the ground floor, I used this space to make coffee (I brought my own) and reorganize my bags before my flight.

Bed 10/10 clean
Shower 8.5/10 clean
Convenience 9.5/10","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jasmina Orlowska","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVPmRwjaEOpAS9Pgl6rJwdBNc5GxVVmR8GCkkVd35EtbW6hMYQZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very clean hostel, the bathrooms are fine, the beds are comfortable, the towels are new and fresh. Everything one needs to stay for one night between the flights. The area around is also nice, very calm.
There are two things that I think could be improved:
- there should be guides on how to get to the hostel, the name suggests it is 5 min away from the airport - in fact it is 25 min by foot (and the uber is 10€...)
- the light in the bathroom is VERY annoying - it goes down every 30 seconds unless one is in constant movement","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"jainendra sinha","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXw8tTypKiRUwbK_oIOxqb9NwvvqEr_PABxcVi219NNY70uO1Xn=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Decent hostel close to the airport

The hostel is conveniently located near the airport. There are self-service kiosks for coffee, tea, chips, and chocolates, and even some free food options with basic ingredients like pasta and rice — you can cook your own meals in the open kitchen. The Wi-Fi is good, and the overall ambience is pleasant for a hostel stay.

However, walking through the nearby lanes can feel a bit unsettling as the area is usually deserted. One concern worth mentioning — the lady at the reception during the daytime behaves quite rudely, especially toward non-white guests, which was disappointing. The rest of the staff, though, are polite and helpful.

The biggest issue is the sensor-based lighting, particularly in the bathrooms. The lights turn off every 30 seconds to a minute, leaving you in complete darkness — it’s honestly frustrating. Also, they don’t clean or change the bedsheets daily.

Overall, it’s a decent stay if you value proximity to the airport and a cozy hostel vibe, but there’s definitely room for improvement in hospitality and facilities.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Deniz Erkmen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUHTdFzOb8tAnCEKa8Z59vqzQnj8I70MST9KfpMEb_ChjnIHDKnlg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"For one night close to the airport, it's great. Good price, clean. They give you a body towel and provide a locker with a lock. Beds have curtains. Would be nice if they had thicker blankets and provided an additional towel for hands/face.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Anton Inshakov","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWruclxuf14PiiILJrM8cBiJH0nFnSJOYrdURyPbx6dAtzs22U=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It is, indeed, walking distance away from T2/T3. However, the T3 and nearby exits are closed during the night, so i had to walk from the middle of T2. It took around 15 minutes to find the place.

It has some parking space infront for like 10-15 cars. The checkin was fast. The staff speaks many languages and are very welcoming and kind.

The room was amazing. Rooms are card-key locked.

The room had a shower, sink and toilet inside, all separated. Everything is squicky clean. Towel was provided, shower had gel and toilet had paper.

The bed frame was rock solid. It didnt make any sound even when a person climbed to the upper bed. There is a dimmed room light enough to see everything and not bother others. Bed has a socket and a personal bright light. There is also a card locked locker. Room, shower and toilet had decent sound isolation.

Though this is the best hostel i ever stayed, i couldn't really sleep because of the mattres. However, it was much better than sitting in the airport.

It could've been nice to have extra sink in toilet or shower room to not bother sleeping people if you","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"goncalonsilva","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0Qexe1uajF-TSqvbHLzgtUk80BSJEirwlZxdhwIhHVVewMbFHRaNf97WJ3b7Rq47i8M9nA=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Hostel right next to the airport, you can walk 10min to the metro station and then ride the subway for 1 stop and get into all terminal pretty quickly. Very clean and tidy. Nice common spaces and staff. Just be aware that if you are carrying big luggages (like I did) you might have to call a cab to the airport. Apart from that everything was great","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Eff Nym","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX1HUT_CykoiCwq3p2BdXQSgFHGBNRLzjgU924Y2nY4-a3XQ2U=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Great as an in and out place. Super clean and modern. You can walk to/from the airport. There are a lot of restaurants aeound. The only reason I gave a 4 instead of a 5 is because it is really an in and out place which doesn't allow for the same social vibe as other hostels. However, it does what it does the best possible way.","Images":null,"When":""}]

So disrespectful and so unprofessional.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Zahidul Hridoy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXvb4wNiWvSs_2Ikk8mv6WhDgdtxeWCC2Pq4FhhbG69Mb51b9FX=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Expedia or any other booking platform should remove this hostel from the platform. This place does not deserve to be on any reputed booking platform.

Customer Service :

There is an old lady who helped me check in, but she does not speak a word of English. English is my second language, and I believe everyone doesn’t need to learn English, but knowing some essential but fundamental words like Identity documents/ Passport and booking number would help if you are the first point of contact for a hostel where you welcome foreign nationals who don’t speak your language. She took help from another guy to find my booking on the hostel side of the Expedia app. This whole thing happened in a lobby(!) where the lady was sitting on a bench, and I was standing confused. She was making faces; she was upset that I couldn’t understand Spanish. Other guests were standing or sitting in chairs, and they became part of the conversation while she was making remarks about me in Spanish. Everyone laughed at my expense. I would have gotten offended, but I was not expecting much, as I read the Google reviews on my way to the hostel when I tried to commute to the hostel from Madrid's town centre.
I don't think the lady is a bad person, but I think she is not the concerned person to help me to check in, or she is wearing multiple hats as I saw her cleaning the toilets as well.

Infrastructure :
They checked me in by filling out all my information on an A4 page in a row where everyone could see what was written on the other rows—a big GDPR violation. I could see all the other information on that page. The lady took a photo of my passport and visa on the phone. I am not sure whether it was a company phone or a personal phone.

There is only one toilet, shower area, and sink for everyone. I am not sure how many people they can accommodate at a maximum, but in my room, there were 12 beds. They at least have four rooms. Please do the math.

The ventilation of the room I was in was horrible. To make everything worse, the person who was staying in the first bed was stinking very badly, either from the clothes or the bedsheets. I didn't see anyone staying in that bed for the whole night. I am not sure why none of the staff attempted to clean it. I had a 100 ml body spray, and I think I sprayed more than half of it in the room, but it didn't help much.

The beds were not comfortable at all, but after all I had experienced, it didn't bother me much.

Unfortunately, it was late at night when I reached the hostel and experienced everything; it was too late for me to find new accommodation and move, especially when I had an early morning flight to catch.

I would highly recommend Expedia and other booking platforms remove this hostel or make a surprise visit to see its condition and make a decision based on its state.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"RosTeK Vlog","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUv-Pp6RYzir9DIfZLclcQsyJc95DgQUPzdYbqreHotoxfO_NHK=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"The worst Hostel I ever been and I been on many. We chose the hotel for its indoor pool, so we were incredibly disappointed to be told shortly before arrival that the pool was going to be closed for routine maintenance. No compensation offered. They should have advised us at the time of booking or offered compensation if the closure was unforeseen. We complained and they offered us free breakfast, when breakfast was already included in our room rate. So they offered us access to the executive lounge, which was something but with young kids the lounge was of limited use to us.

We had lots of luggage and the kids in arms but they don't help you get bags to your room. Pretty incredible that this simple service isn't available.

We booked adjoining rooms, and one of them wasn't ready when we arrived, despite it being past the advertised check-in time.

The air conditioning system in both rooms was deafening and didn't actually cool. Don't stay in summer.

So I wouldn't say the service was good or the hotel met expectations.

On the plus side, useless air con aside, the suite was very comfortable, breakfast was pretty good, and the airport shuttle service did the job.

I wouldn't stay again. For the price, you would expect much better.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Grahamarb","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QdHDxxyBDO6igPA51sqxYAI1TTeuGvapT1sWSCJfPSgzOZltNlMhWmUDpj2A3v7zAw89g=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"This hotel is excellent. title: Viklo S.L
address: C. Mar Mediterráneo, 20, Hortaleza, 28033 Madrid
user_reviews: [{"Name":"María E. Diaz de Vivar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUb1cQY2eizDUuSwLYKM4Q-dFaHR_OZj3iZMoUnnrk7NhKBd73X=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"La experiencia de alojarnos en este lugar fue tan decepcionante, que no se por donde empezar... lo primero es decir que fue el peor por lejos, de cualquier alquiler temporal al que fui. Ya arranca mal porque la puerta de entrada principal, la que da acceso al edificio, no funciona, esta siempre abierta, con lo cual les aseguro que llegar sola por la noche daba bastante miedo. El piso estaba helado, no había calefacción central, solo aires acondicionados. El living tenía un sillón sucio que daba asco sentarse, el aire acondicionado de ahí hacía un ruido como si fuera una alarma, no lo podíamos prender. El wifi nunca funcionó. En una de las habitaciones no funcionaba el aires acondicionado (que son frío calor), tuvimos días de 2º, pasamos mucho frío, las mantas no eran suficientes, y algunas tenían más de 50 años seguro. La cocina carece de elementos básicos, si jarra eléctrica para calentar agua, nada, de todos modos no podíamos estar ahí del frío que hacía. Las camas de los dormitorios con los colchones vencidos, no te podías mover del ruido que hacían. En uno de los cuartos, las mantas eran como las que te dan en el avión, imposible sentirse abrigado con eso. Y ahora vamos al tema del baño, cuando llegamos la ducha se caía, no te podías duchar, a demás de que había cantidad de hongos. El secador de pelo decide cuando apagarse... El mueble del baño esta podrido abajo por el agua. La ducha es diminuta. La decoración puede ser anecdótica en todo este caos, pero poner cuadros sucios y rotos, para eso mejor no poner nada. En este lugar no cuidan al cliente ni les interesa que pases una buena estancia. A nuestros reclamos a su whatssapp la respuesta fue: \"Entendemos la situación y le pedimos disculpas nuevamente por los inconvenientes ocasionados. En este momento nos encontramos aguardando la respuesta de nuestros superiores para poder brindarle una solución a la brevedad.\" Por suerte ya estoy en mi casa, pero les aseguro que fue la peor experiencia en un viaje.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhArAe_fo705XUGidABjDBBj\u0026fid=0x0:0x433aaa6c84d57839"],"When":"2026-1-29"}]

Empezando por la atención, fue una reserva de última hora y llamé para poder aclarar un par de temas. Eva me atención con un calidez maravillosa, me dio soluciones e incluso me buscó alojamiento para un día que me había quedado sin opciones. También estuve hablando con Rafa sobre el tema del check inn y me comentó que era un negocio familiar que llevaban como unos 40 años, y ahí lo entendí todo. Ese trato que estaba recibiendo es el tipo de trato que recibía como guía cuando hace casi 20 años me alojaba con grupos en hoteles familiares.
A mí llegada me recibieron son sonrisas y sabiendo quien era. ¡De verdad, esto ha hecho mi estancia algo de lo más especial! no puedo estar más agradecida por las sonrisas, las charlas y el cariño que he recibido.
Me he quedado tanto en los apartamentos como en el hostal.
El hostal está muy bien, las habitaciones limpias y actualizadas. Pero los apartamentos son de otro nivel. impolutos, con una decoración cálida, y la cama y la ducha de 10.

Eva, Pedro, Manoli, Rafa y a todas las chicas del equipo de limpieza: ¡sois la caña, y el año que viene vuelvo! guardadme mi apartamento ;)

Por cierto, fue una reserva de última hora a dos días de empezar FITUR (la feria internacional de turismo), para que os hagáis una idea, para esos días he visto un hostal de mucha menor categoría que el Hostal Aeropuerto por 500€ la noche, así que el precio que tienen, tanto del apartamento como del hostal, es coherente para esas fechas, y se nota que no van a abusar, como he visto en otros establecimientos.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"AnabelSP","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU-kFpX70Dej3G82w2lsqgJzU7ULZbqrcp8TrbzYwAVznGoO4ti=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Muy bien. .","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sonia L.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLwdObIMdR1XSDSx2pIW34RVwfUnNf16DpSCx0sQgLMsJ_Zjg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"There is no soundproofing whatsoever, we had the worst night ever there.

The hall looks nice, we haven't tried them but there is a gym, a restaurant and a bar. The hotel is conveniently located next to the airport. The rooms are clean and spacious, however....

Inside of your room, you will hear every conversation in the hallway (and there will be people talking super loud there until 2am at least). You will be startled awake everytime someone will open or close their room door. On the night we were, the people on the room above moved furniture around and dropped stuff in the floor all night long which, again, startled us awake a bunch of times.
The neighborhood appears to be a bit sketchy because we were also awoken several times by firecrackers (or gunshots, impossible to tell) and police sirens (it felt a bit like sleeping in GTA).

Finally, the wall and door to the bathroom are glass. So you will hear everything and see a little bit. No privacy at all is possible. If you are spending the night with you special someone, get ready for the most romantic poops of your life. If you have something important to do on the next day, consider chosing another hotel or you'll have to go about your day without having been able to rest at all...","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"O2533NJpaulp","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QfCm-MBS7Itwd4h-55xcyygsVKq1IZbUTNyg17UKkQsT2C3oSXBlQ_-pGTl8imnrFVtVw=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"A nice clean spacious hotel with friendly staff. The hotel refused to refund one night after my father-in-law was unable to check in due to airline-lost luggage. This was a documented, unavoidable disruption and was communicated in advance. The hotel nevertheless chose to retain the charge.

What is concerning is not the money, but the judgment exercised. In situations like this, reputable hotels apply discretion. This one did not. The response was rigid, indifferent, and inconsistent with what most travelers reasonably expect from a professional hospitality operation.

title: Travelodge Madrid Metropolitano

descriptions: Straightforward budget hotel offering down-to-earth rooms with free Wi-Fi, plus a cafe/bar.

title: Compostela Suites
address: C. Zambrana, 4, San Blas-Canillejas, 28022 Madrid
descriptions: Modern apartments in a relaxed hotel offering a bar, a playground & an outdoor pool.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Daniel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL1R_mG-g13JIIvSrvltHsp5tQiN0vB2zkCWMIceXbCJkq7OcA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I am surprised by the average rating here and booked via trip.com not being aware. We as a family 2+1 (4 yrs old) missed nothing. Good rooms for the price (~105 EUR per night) with a bathtub and a sufficiently equipped kitchen for 1-2 nights. It was indeed noisy in the hallway and you need to close the door of the bedroom if you have one - but after that it was perfectly fine.

I liked the vending machines where you can get coffee, water or a sandwich. If you need more there is a Shell petrol station 5 min away by walk or a huge mall (Plenilunio) with a Mercadona supermarket and many food spots.

Good option for transit passengers travelling via Barajas airport in Madrid, better option than some run-down places in the Barajas district next to the airport IMO, although it would be great to have some kind of airport transfer organized by the hotel for an extra fee. If there is one, I was not informed by any means. There is no real direct connection from/to the airport. From the airport cost us 15 EUR with Uber. To the airport it was 8 EUR.

Protip: if you have a morning flight from Terminal 1, drive to Terminal 2 and take a walk (due to traffic).

Remark/what can be done better: Cafeteria personel did not understand a basic order in English for "black tea". I mean this was absolutely not a sophisticated order.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Steve Cárdenas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL6m4YRMPQDS5hDYHZvUqiY2TmrIqAuKduYp4eFTA_KbKZrlw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I had an incredibly disappointing experience at these 'suites' and would not recommend them to anyone. We traveled as a family of three (two adults and one child), and from the moment we arrived, the service was unacceptable.
The check-in process was a struggle; the receptionist was quite rude and flatly refused to let me briefly use the parking area just to unload our heavy luggage. Once we got to the room, things only got worse. Instead of the double bed we expected, we found two single beds pushed together and a sofa bed. The mattresses were as hard as rocks, and the frames were so noisy that none of us could sleep. We all woke up with significant back pain. To make matters worse, the walls are paper-thin; we could hear every conversation from the neighboring rooms and all the noise from the street.
The 'flat' was also missing basic necessities. We discovered there was no cookware at all. When we asked reception about this, we were rudely told we had to pay a €20 deposit. For that price, we were given one rusty, dirty black pan, one dirty pot, and three basic utensils. After paying a premium price for the room, being charged extra for filthy, substandard equipment felt like a total scam.
The only positive part of the entire stay was the checkout. The lady and gentleman at the desk were amazingly fast and friendly, which was a refreshing change, but it wasn't enough to save the experience. This stay was a complete waste of money and we will definitely not be returning.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Veselin Popov","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIAxQC1lGEsyT8_5_z8xyCU30GS5aZoRMRUY26VYWsiATj3sw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"The size of the room was good and they are not very clean.

There’s a minibar in the room, and guess what? There’s a price sheet above it. While we aren’t expecting a hand out, they could at least provided free water to their customers! Breakfast options weren’t offered even after asking the staff about breakfast times. I had assumed it was included in the reservation, as always on my business trips.
In the morning stuff said they couldn’t ask/remind customers they offered breakfast at extra every time customers checked in? Because that’d be too much to expect from a hotel, I guess?
Having an international bank card means that I may or not pay a fee every time I use it, so if you are charging for the room, you can explain/ remind about breakfast to give clients the option to pay it at check in.

I asked about the shuttle bus, and they said I had to come and “get a ticket”, so at check out I said, I’ll have breakfast (when I happily thought it was included) and come back when is time to leave but apparently I had to be there 15 min before and I couldn’t get a ticket before. The staff wasn’t exactly welcoming nor helpful. The rooms are nice and clean. Next time I’ll look for a better hotel by paying 20€ and save me the extra costs.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"valeria sosa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIakYizJ765F-h1y7bMtH6iiIf1GwD9i-MeV3uf0R98inY-dQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"WORST HOTEL EXPERIENCE EVER STAY FAR AWAY

If I could give zero stars I would. This place completely ruined my entire trip. B&B Hotels in the outskirts of Madrid is hands down the worst dump I have ever stayed in. It is near the airport, yes, but it is in the middle of nowhere surrounded by sketchy and dangerous people. You do not even feel safe walking outside.

Let’s start with the front desk girl with brownish hair and glasses. She is the rudest and most unprofessional person I have ever met. If she hates her job that much no one is forcing her to stay. Her attitude alone should be enough reason for anyone to never book here.

The hotel itself reeks of a disgusting smell that hits you as soon as you walk in. The bathroom has visible mold and the shower feels like you are standing in a swamp. They give you one towel for your entire stay. No extras no amenities nothing. For the price they charge this place should not cost more than 100 euros a night if even that.

It gets worse. Employees entered our room twice without permission while me and my partner were out. When we came back we noticed things missing. So yes they go through your stuff. Absolutely unacceptable and creepy.

They also lie about having AC saying it is only heat. Total scam. And to top it all off the room was infested with mosquitos. I got bitten all over my body to the point that I now need to see a doctor when I get home. The walls and floors are filthy and the so called breakfast they serve looked old and moldy.

Avoid this hotel at all costs. It is disgusting unsafe and a complete scam. I would not stay here again even if it were free. Save yourself the nightmare there are plenty of better hotels in Madrid that actually respect their guests.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Prasad Perera","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXVNFhzj5LDjWBOLVyk2VPWTIhd4jcAo1GtThN5jjCDk98HTV1p=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I stayed at B&B HOTEL Madrid Aeropuerto T1 T2 T3 from in Sep 2025. The location is not very convenient at all. Although it’s called an “airport hotel,” it actually takes about 10–15 minutes by taxi, you cannot walk. And with traffic it can easily turn into 20–30+ minutes — which I experienced on my way back to the airport. Public transport is also not ideal: to catch a bus to the city, you need to walk at least 15–20 minutes to the nearest station, and connections are not straightforward. There’s no metro nearby either.

The room itself was compact and basic — fine for a short stay, but lacking small essentials like bottled water or even a glass in the room. Overall, my experience was not very positive, mainly due to the inconvenient location. There are better hotels closer to the airport if you plan to stay in this area, and if your focus is the city, you’ll find hotels with much easier access there.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"María Cuacos","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIY0i3sPqzD0l6Z6CAIzjgiliJyyVf7Mx3U742YOqBBooTQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Room full of mosquitos!!! On the plus side: underground parking, comfy bed, very helpful and kind receptioninst. We just wanted a hotel to sleep before flying and next time we will try something else.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Andrzej Świercz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI1tcxHRbFcDr1-qHTzqnA317st2DUHpI_7tQQVSZWgKcmI1A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Grate place for short  trip location  very good ,rooms nice and clean  staff very friendly  and helpful  special  ladies  from afternoon shift  breakfast  basic  but always  fresh   available  restaurant after 7pm","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCYCQMgu7braw9159jIn7R4&fid=0x0:0xc410b150111ecfc7"],"When":"2026-2-22"},{"Name":"Sammi Kelly IPHM","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUH3jXcheu3yZFkl4T4Hq_hhpUpXwDtChYfcM8IEGTuLKdhhqre=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Beautiful room, exactly like the photos online.

title: ibis budget Madrid Calle Alcalá
address: Metro Suanzes, C. de Rufino González, 19, San Blas-Canillejas, 28037 Madrid
descriptions: Streamlined rooms featuring complimentary Wi-Fi & flat-screen TVs in a no-nonsense budget hotel.
complete_address: {"borough":"San Blas","street":"Metro Suanzes, C. de Rufino González, 19","city":"Madrid","postal_code":"28037","state":"Madrid","country":"ES"}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Younes BADANI","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXAyRQbbiXuCf6zlHyiMprYJdjfGM36Z8lRAPD_CFDQkB_UlzXx8w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"I recently stayed at this hotel for 3 nights. My overall experience was mixed.
The rooms are very small and cramped. For a single night's stay, this might be manageable, but for anything longer, the size is definitely an issue. Despite the small rooms, the housekeeping staff were exceptionally professional and did a fantastic job.
On the positive side, the breakfast buffet was of good quality and a definite highlight.
However, I found the price to be very high for the standard of the hotel, particularly given the small room size. The hotel also charges a hefty $25 per day for parking, which adds significantly to the cost.
A major inconvenience was related to deliveries. I strongly advise against having any packages delivered to the hotel address (whether for food, clothing, etc.). They do not accept parcels and seem unwilling to communicate with delivery drivers. They should, at the very least, call the guest's room or direct the driver to the room for delivery. This lack of service was very frustrating.
I sincerely hope the hotel addresses the room size/pricing ratio and improves the package delivery service. I would consider staying again if these issues are resolved for my next visit.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAI0x2ipBctnRjADank4181&fid=0x0:0xae3cc0956bdfbc43"],"When":"2025-10-11"},{"Name":"Maksim Smith","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVCim4jGVh4a3Fh-jt6sXp88NRijZs5K_gmJi49KJOHbY4h5DmP=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"There are multiple ongoing construction works around the hotel, that creates a lot of noise and dust during the working days. This information is not disclosed on the hotel’s official website. If you are a light sleeper, I would not recommend staying at this hotel until the construction is finished.

The location otherwise is good, close to the metro Suansez that can take you to the city center. The staff is also nice and helpful, but unfortunately the construction works around and the weak price/quality ratio of the hotel leaves a mixed impression.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"David Boretti","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKWHRquwFce47cHlEoERkPduRIf8LGjGMf6na9WyT93YEJ3yw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"very good value.  smaller rooms but we didn't mind that at all, especially at the price point. service and staff was also great. very happy with our stay here and would stay again!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"lu benavides","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUSQ0IuftkUcCs8UUCFZM3W_-s5LcsVARAldiSXIiyjRhsEHZ7h=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The hotel is clean and well located, about 5 minutes from the metro station and about 10 minutes from the airport. Update: we couldn’t get the deposit back for almost 2 months","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rico","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL8VV_12VImDl2ZHyQHKxeVzgP49VbtM7_KhntYI4y-uci5Jg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"PS: if you don't get your deposit back within a month, please hire a lawyer to send a burofax, it costs between 80 and 150 euros to send it but it works 100%, you will receive your deposit in 24/48 hours.

The staff in the reception is very polite and helpful. The design looks lovely. The rooms and the services included look amazing at first.
However the electricity in the 2nd apartment I have rented for a few months went off dozens of times during my stay.
I have rented two different apartments and for each one they asked for 1 deposit which is not correct. By the time I left each apartment, they had the contractual obligations to return back the deposit within 1 month. user_reviews:

When someone sneeze in the other room/floor it is like it is in your ear. Reception didn’t do anything about it. I want my money back. Don’t come here!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tony Lee","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVyE0_oDGt57wU8WGCstzZcf89HerXyxDSXqDS6Y1aEY2REOP0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Really close to the airport, parking is great, simple basic and clean, good place to spend an overnight","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Daniel Cluxton","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKwoGcOAPlLAbhWRrdO4-xnJrxRLYXIEWFTs9G_hENk-Fm1Cw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Stayed for one night before my flight out of Madrid to be closer to the airport.

Location
While the hotel looks close to the airport on the map, it's actually a fifteen minute drive to the airport and just in general it's pretty out of the way.

There is an airport shuttle to take you to and from the airport, but they charge you 10€ for the right to use it. I understand this is clearly stated on their website, but Booking.com made no mention of this, and in general I find it odd an airport hotel would charge for the shuttle in the first place. Most airport hotels would offer this as a free service to encourage you to stay at their hotel, but I digress.

Additionally, both of the Ubers we used to get to and from the hotel struggled to get there due to the confusing layout. So, if you do end up staying here, I would recommend having a car if you plan on doing anything but spending the night.

Rooms
I am not too nitpicky about rooms in general, but to sum it up the rooms feel more like a dated motel than a proper airport hotel. All the plugs in the room we stayed in were loose, there was no desk, no mini-fridge, and the bare minimum of amenitites. Our bathroom also had a leaky pipe that was constantly dripping water onto the floor, but that might just be bad luck on our part.

Also, while this isn't the fault of the hotel, there is no elevator on the property, so if you have a second floor room you'll have to lift your bags up there yourself or ask the staff for help.

It's nothing too bad if you're just going to spend a night, but anything longer and I would look in to finding somewhere else.

Service
The service is the one thing I will praise the hotel for. Everyone we interacted with, from the airport shuttle driver to the employees who helped us at the front desk were excellent. They made sure we had a shuttle back to the airport in time for our flight and were happy to answer any questions we had. They even offered to help carry our bags up to our second floor room. Super friendly people.

Summary
Given the poor location and poor quality rooms, I can't say I would recommend this hotel for anything other than a cheap, one night stay before an early morning flight. Even then, for the price point I'd honestly consider staying at the nearby Marriott or Hilton instead (which were only 10-20€ per night more expensive at the writing of this review).","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: Hotel NH Madrid Barajas Airport
address: C. Catamarán, 1, Barajas, 28042 Madrid
descriptions: Contemporary hotel & restaurant offering complimentary Wi-Fi & airport shuttle service.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Alex Iuga","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIkDXtiBejBbceDZJkimM1Rf8Use_ks0Dpd5pd_-2JGXvcC0g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great stay, perfect for transit and city visits

The hotel is conveniently located near the airport and offers a free shuttle service until 12:30 AM, which makes it ideal both for transit stays and for exploring the city. The metro station is very close, so getting into Madrid is quick and easy.

The staff was extremely friendly and helpful, and the room was exactly what we needed after a long flight — clean, comfortable, and quiet. Breakfast was good and offered a decent variety.

We really enjoyed our stay and will definitely come back!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Abdelaal Mohammed","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVOigbqiYlo21y_10QObaZexvIg8WUC_7VroFGfqWJ_qCscHt0N=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Unsafe Experience & Poor Response from Staff
I stayed at this hotel recently, and unfortunately the experience was very disappointing. While I was inside the hotel restaurant, I experienced a distressing situation that significantly affected my comfort and enjoyment.

What made the situation even worse was the hotel’s reaction:
– Staff did not review the CCTV footage
– No internal report was filed
– No support or apology was offered

For a hotel that is part of an international chain, this lack of safety and unprofessional handling of incidents is unacceptable.
***************************
Thank you
My review reflects my experience with the NH brand during my stay. Since your hotel is part of the same NH group, the feedback is relevant to the overall guest experience across the brand — not just a single location.
Saying that “this did not happen at our hotel” does not change the fact that it occurred at an NH property. My intention is simply to share what I experienced with the brand, honestly and respectfully.

No one from the hotel contacted me afterwards, and I received no support or apology. The experience impacted how safe I felt and left a lasting effect. I share this feedback because it matters, not to assign blame unnecessarily, but to ensure the brand maintains the level of safety and care guests expect from NH.

**************
Dear General Manager,

I have reviewed your response, and I would like to clarify my position directly, as your message does not fully reflect what we experienced as guests affected by this incident.

First, while you mentioned that an apology was extended to the Embassy of Egypt, this does not help us as the guests who were personally impacted. Unfortunately, no direct or personal apology was offered to me or to any member of our group at the time of the incident or afterward. In such a situation, acknowledgment and empathy toward the affected guests are essential.

Second, I would like to clarify that our personal belongings were not left in a public place outside the hotel. They were inside the hotel restaurant, which is part of your property. As guests, we reasonably expected a basic level of security within a hotel-operated facility, or at the very least, immediate and active assistance from hotel management to respond to the situation and help identify the individual responsible.

What happened was not simply the loss of personal belongings. The incident had a serious emotional and psychological impact on all of us. It caused stress, anxiety, and a strong sense of discomfort, not only for those directly affected but for the entire group traveling together. As a result, the overall experience of our trip was significantly damaged, and the sense of safety and comfort we expected during our stay was lost.

I am writing this message out of genuine disappointment, not escalation. I expected a more compassionate and guest-focused response that reflects the standards and reputation of an international hotel.

I still hope this matter can be reviewed from a more human perspective, and that it can be handled in a way that helps restore some level of trust and goodwill.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"marianellao268","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QftlnsLB8VReEIa8q3x-FUIWL9jAVnw9OiPzFml0xLx7k1DporKgZdMXGALflbNzntF3w=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"I am so happy I stayed in this Hotel for my connection flight. I had to stay 2 nights in Madrid before travelling to South America with my 2 year old. The hotel is so close to the Airport and in a residencial area with markets, restaurants, pharmacies, even pet and small health centers. There is a long promenade to walk and several playgrounds along the way. We didn't miss or needed the city AT ALL. The Hotel has a very nice restaurant where we did breakfast and dinner with lots of nice choices. I received my friends who visited me in the bar of the hotel. The staff was extremely accommodating and friendly. I used the free shuttle to go to the Airport on our departure. It was truly amazing and I'm definitely coming back. 100% recommended.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"granite_meerkat","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0Qexe1uajF-TSqvbHLzgtUk80BSJEirwlZxdhwIhHVVewMbFHRaNf97WJ3b7Rq47i8M9nA=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Had a four night stay here and it was great.  Arrived at lunchtime and was met promptly by the shuttle (it’s recommended to phone more or less once you’ve collected luggage or thereabouts) and from there it was a short drive to the hotel.  Check in was smooth and efficient, with friendly staff who spoke English (ideal as my Spanish is minimal beyond a few phrases!).  The reception is clean and well presented with adequate seating around.||The room itself was clean and although not spacious, it was fine for the few days I was there and I was really only there to sleep.  Storage was fine for a short stay with some clothes hangers, three drawers and a some shelving in the cupboard, along with a safe.  There is also a minibar with a few drinks inside but I don’t know what they charge for them as I only drunk the fine tap water!||The hotel is also equipped with a gym which looked decent, a very small pool and a terrace to sit out on.  There is also a bar and restaurant, although time stopped me from using them.||I picked this hotel based on it being easily accessible from the airport, affordable and very close to the metro, which is a few minutes walk.  Would happily stay here again for my next visit.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"OUFC-fan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QfCm-MBS7Itwd4h-55xcyygsVKq1IZbUTNyg17UKkQsT2C3oSXBlQ_-pGTl8imnrFVtVw=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"The hotel is situated an easy walk from El Capricho metro station for getting into or out of central Madrid, and shuttles take you to and from the airport every 30 to 45 minutes (make sure you book your ride). Best of all is the breakfast, with a great variety of fresh things, eggs, bacon, etc. Loved it.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Luiz Araujo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXliJeMOQx-2Jiv4GuVP3KkoI7NgYBuImLlkUMtlhFbEB0-m5v9=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The overall experience was very good. That was really important to because I was feeling really anxious going into it coming from our previous day which was not very good","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Emma Wright","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWSmUX1l6rwhaPJn0VqlRz3fVL-Bv3ALmLVgxbhTJHHOJNU1hZ_=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We've stayed in Melias before so we chose the hotel based on reliability. I would stay again.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Leo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWuQYfkt7pKxNmjzsMTzNuwf4WWshqxal34F5woKC9vK5Fm5eNI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Great Stay Near Madrid Airport – Clean, Comfortable, and Convenient

We picked this hotel mainly for its location near the airport since it was our last day in Spain. After traveling through Madrid and a couple of other countries, this place turned out to be a really good choice.

✨ What I liked:
•	Comfortable & clean: The room was spotless and very cozy.
•	Facilities: Enjoyed the minibar and overall amenities. Everything felt well-kept and modern.
•	Food: We had dinner at the hotel restaurant and it was excellent. We ordered a lot, and every dish was tasty.
•	Airport shuttle: Super convenient — one of the main reasons we chose this hotel, and it makes departures stress-free.

🛏️ What I didn’t like:
The bed setup. They combined two smaller beds instead of one big one, which isn’t my favorite style. Not a dealbreaker, but definitely something I’d change.

Final thoughts:
✔️ Great location for airport access
✔️ Clean, comfortable, and modern
✔️ Excellent dinner
✔️ Shuttle bus service is a big plus
❌ Only downside: the two-bed setup instead of a larger bed

Overall, it was a very good experience and I’d recommend it, especially for travelers looking for a comfortable last stop before flying out.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB1jJ-_hQsGd0hI-bMKRSXe&fid=0x0:0xc469196792303ec"],"When":"2025-8-17"},{"Name":"Mr. Doe","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXtVgKboBD_IXIWE0crEyuGSaAtJnV2SEMh_Z1WBw8OOHCCYuy7=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️☆
I really enjoyed my stay at Meliá Avenida América. Check-in was fast and smooth, even though I arrived late at night—no problems at all. The bed was super comfortable, though I wasn’t a big fan of the two mattresses pushed together (my wife kept falling into the middle gap 😅).

The room was spacious, the bathroom very nice, and overall the hotel felt modern and clean. The biggest highlight—and main reason I booked—was the airport shuttle, which made everything so much easier and stress-free compared to taking a taxi or Uber.

During my trip I stayed at three hotels between Madrid and Valencia, and this was definitely the second-best. (Valencia was the worst 😬.)
Would recommend this hotel, especially if you want convenience with the airport shuttle.

I also enjoyed walking through the huge park nearby, felt very zen.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCiy6BZjheTxbhI5rDxQaMr&fid=0x0:0xc469196792303ec"],"When":"2025-8-17"},{"Name":"Yuliia Yurchenko","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXd7BAGU70zXuJz5nxAQOQ4CPdWg-nb_dc2182vQZuFLLEjdbc=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great hotel and friendly staff. He was beyond helpful. We stayed at the hotel because our flights got messed up and we turned up pretty tired and stressed out. Carlos checked us in and his laid back self was exactly what we needed in that situation. He even upgraded us to a nicer room (and he's right about the slippers!).

The room was really comfortable, the breakfast was great, and it's really easy for the airport, especially with their free shuttle bus.

Only room for improvement would be with the amount of vegetarian options for dinner but what we did have was tasty.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Oksana Kravchuk","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWQunCoVJ7fd-DbIgScorPmDUEFfVzDvFd2quaFb4WMKmzPSXs9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Our stay at this hotel near Madrid Airport was excellent! The location is very convenient, just a few minutes away from the airport, and the hotel offers a free shuttle service, which is a big plus. However, keep in mind that the transfer doesn’t operate from all terminals, so it’s good to check in advance — that’s the only small downside. Everything else was wonderful: the rooms are clean, modern, and comfortable, and the food is delicious. Overall, a great choice for travelers who want comfort and convenience near the airport!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDDCiDaMTORtyDip4FJPE2J&fid=0x0:0x4277c77199c107c7"],"When":"2025-11-5"},{"Name":"Pete London","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI20FHjCmq2H1nO1x2qDu7iPdc7f4xH7o4iiGorAnanmWc-Sw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Our stay did not start great - there were about 60 people waiting for the hotel shuttle from Terminal 4. Given the minibus fits 20 people and comes every half an hour, it would have taken us at least 1:30hr before getting on. A taxi took 5mins, but overcharged us EUR33 for the 5min journey… The hotel desperately needs a bigger vehicle or to run the shuttles more frequently. There was zero effort made to deal with this issue.

When we arrived to the hotel, there was a huge queue of people waiting to check in. Thankfully I had checked-in online and was able to get a room card from one of the automated machines, but otherwise it would have been another 20-30mins.

The room itself was modern, in great shape and spacious. The bed was comfortable. A lot of light leaked in through the door from the corridor, but other than that the room was dark. The temperature was pleasant. The free minicar is a nice touch.

Apart from the toilet, the bathroom is open and only has a curtain to separate it from the room. Everything worked well, however.

We had some snacks at the hotel’s Yatta street food restaurant. Two baos and two tacos came to EUR28 - I’d recommend going to the Burger King opposite instead. Service was slow, these two basic dishes took 20mins to arrive.

Return journeys to the airport in the shuttle can be pre booked and we made it back easily and quickly in the morning.

Overall, a solid hotel that desperately needs to work on its shuttle service and how to operate when it’s busy.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Heather Milne","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUS726LLDGZhxdA72I4Nmrj94brQzVWWI5EHOq0-YotPAZFO4OH=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Very good airport hotel. There is a free shuttle to and from the airport that will save you money if you don’t mind waiting up to 30 minutes on it arriving at the airport. Rooms are clean and modern, the only thing missing was mugs and spoons for the tea and coffee they provide which is unfortunately instant, no coffee machine. Overall a nice place.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Katarina R.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIl4ybaIGGq-9oRdSXFZLYfjSuNAMNaGWfpWpf8N1nqGpG5-sk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"October 2025 Stay

Pros:
The hotel is beautiful and rooms are spacious and quiet. I liked the area. It was very walkable.

Cons:
Unfortunately I can’t say I received good service.

I called the front desk for water and the gentleman kindly said to come get some downstairs. When I arrived, the woman said that they don’t do that. When I explained, she spoke to him and firmly said it was a one time exception. I felt like I was bothering them. I was happy to buy some, but they weren’t very nice about it.

Secondly, I tried to get shuttle information and it wasn’t really clear that you have to reserve accordingly per time slots. He may have done so on my behalf, but didn’t share that information, so when I arrived there was confusion with the bus driver.","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: ARTIEM Madrid
address: C. de Juan Pérez Zúñiga, 20, Cdad. Lineal, 28027 Madrid
descriptions: Bright rooms in a relaxed wellness hotel with contemporary lounges, a restaurant & a fitness center.
user_reviews: [{"Name":"David Jones","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLUQF_4NVKoLR-Jf9Bqc23CbGEBlGsNOJRETHJ3sd63IskOLw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I loved my stay at the Arteim Madrid! As a regular traveller I like to be as fair and balanced as possible but I must say this is one of the best hotels I have stayed for price to quality ratio. On arrival I was greeted by the friendly check in staff and it was all very quick as I had done the process online beforehand. I had a deluxe room which was big and generally very quiet even though it was at the front towards the road. I could occasionally hear children's footsteps above and other guests until quite late at night but compared to many Spanish accomodation it was quiet. The breakfast choice and quality was very good although a better choice for hot options and especially for vegetarians would have been welcome. The Green Room was fantastic and offering free drinks and snacks 24/7 was amazing! The hotel is quite far way from the centre but I am a keen walker and still managed to do it all on foot although bus and metro options exist. On arrival I took the 200 bus from the airport to a nearby bus stop at a cost of 5 Euros payable on the bus. On the return I got a direct Uber to T1 and it was only 9.5 Euros.
