[{"Name":"Tom De Busscher","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ-cAQEDImJ9213WVMDwLr4p9MIwbp_GetCsksfq4eFMuVYog=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A great free museum that is both informative as interactive. You can easily spend 2 hours here without getting bored. Very friendly staff also to help you on your way or guide you through the do it yourself experiments.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBOdoPX8OHWFKZxbsdmC0TP&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2026-2-10"},{"Name":"Murphy Tassin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVQ6HffsLHHWUMdbUvqs0Oz3J_5_npo-FR-6fL1t4ZwIBvM4Cyo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"🎉 Looking for a Fun Day Out with the Kids? 🧒🦕🔬
Discover the National Museum of Science and Technology – where learning meets adventure!

🐾 Explore hundreds of fascinating stuffed animals
📸 Snap sweet pics of animals that look like they're smiling
🧠 Hands-on exhibits that spark curiosity
🎨 Perfect for curious minds and family fun!

Smaller then I expected.
Bring the whole family for an unforgettable day of science, smiles, and discovery!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBIAqiw6JNcUJ6Byqx-92VI&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2025-7-12"},{"Name":"Toby Vanderbeek","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWlXJDQn2lV2nSAZdrNBVTf3y9WAh7oQy-ckbfCFTLEE5PuSacZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Entrance is free. There’s a small fee for the planetarium and some other experience. There weren’t too many people. It was a Thursday, early afternoon. The ground floor is a static exhibit. The kids didn’t care. We went up to the first floor to play with the interactive exhibits. This was much more fun. The kids played for about 30 minutes before one got bored and wanted to leave. I wanted to go into the planetarium but there was no consensus so we left. If your kids are curious, they will like this place. There’s enough to keep adults interested too.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID-r7ya3QE&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2022-12-12"},{"Name":"Natalia Crea","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXajgzA7hbow_GjIV6kMCEXh_S1w_LjmUhkMcIXCeobjSHj7Jw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Loved it! There's nothing better than an interactive museum where one can learn, play and have fun all in one!
Entrance is free which is also great.

There are some exhibitions and workshops that aren't free and also a planetarium which costs 3€.

I would recommend to get your tickets in advance if you are interested in visiting the planetarium as it does get full pretty quick, specially on the weekends.

If you are adults going by yourself and can avoid the weekends, I would say: avoid them! You are gonna get stuck behind lots of kids trying out the experiments and won't let you play as much as you want. So take care of your inner child and visit the museum during the week.

There are lockers to leave your belongings if you wish to, just be aware some keys don't have number tags so you will have to remember the number. It might sound obvious (we thought so too) but then we saw a few people trying to open every locker as they couldn't remember their number.

All in all: a great museum!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDNuK2DuQE&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2024-1-20"},{"Name":"Sergio Carvalho","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUZNLFi9DRa31g0Stayl7dv4VAPWzZSxjaxhD7M8IDe6Qj8quvsjg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Kids loved it! Some restrictions still apply due to covid which limits the options to interact with all experiments. Super friendly staff. Lockers available. Be there early. Get familiar with all offerings as they vary over time: Interactive tour, planetarium, temporary exhibits,...","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICWwe3UUg&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2022-2-28"},{"Name":"Jonathan Fisher","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXt4LVCEhYpL-x9MmumU7fZ2R1nZnSnimbeFlrdtAWj0nE6toqR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We only had a short time but we could see that this needs about half a day to get a full sense of all the fun demonstrations and exhibits. They've done a great job of making the stories and physical effects of things accessible and interactive. The ramps and walks are fun to move around and give you time to tell your kid(s) more about the things they have seen and touched. If the museum still has the hanging metal curtains on the first floor when you visit though, they don't like if you mess with them ;)","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"vivek sinha","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUaCbHSdt14RuLdfrqca3_Ldhc6qjlErLkVWfIrvlWuX_DjBVBJCw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A must visit place ... plenty of things to do with science experiments

Entry fee - free for museum(though you have to take tickets) ; 3€ for planetarium

Have three floor - ground floor mostly old bike cars movie making and health care equipment’s

First floor is best part - have plenty of interesting things (explaining science concepts),  any kid would love this place

Top floor have planetarium

There is a cafeteria as well on ground floor

Overall, a must visit place and ideal for a half day trip","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE9dqnJw&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2018-3-27"},{"Name":"Anand Gothe","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXqs2VkIj9tXRc5O7ejBKu9o9fYN-4R9uT1UuOyGVxqh6e5bhnEjA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Awesome place, for kids and adults alike. Lot of interesting items and experiments on display. All the labels and descriptions are Spanish. You'll need about an hour to cover the two floors. Entry is free to both these floors. If you speak Spanish, I highly recommend the planetarium on third floor (for EUR 3). There is also a picnic section on ground floor for kids and families to enjoy their meals.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICk883bfw&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2018-5-31"}]

[{"Name":"Eran Gordon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV9TCfglhZAbghI0RPu-m-ZPBi-FZdLJGAKjRajtReHhhnskaMd=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Nice place for kids, very gimicy but I think kids will like it, and The stuff at the tour was really nice.
They give a little gummy candy every room, and a small VR game at the end.
The price is 15€ per person.

I give 1 star cause they offered me a candy if I give them a good review.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCQt1W2fArOblsDLJOasmnP&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-10-2"},{"Name":"Amanda Crook","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJI--3PEU6CVV2n1C01h-yKGKVCbVqSSw_3JMHuND5PXexbxg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Super cute and fun activity for kids. Takes about 30 minutes to complete. Take the slide down!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDTXKFaDAMvt_TxkkmUMJ5T&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-12-29"},{"Name":"Ana Reyes","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWMwtoVpk1i3x_wiRJTSSM-Q4T6tR8QfucPFB3sfTx613SCfoKg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"1/5 stars*

Visited The Sweet Space museum in Madrid with my family, and unfortunately, it was a disappointing experience. We were expecting a fun and engaging time, but what we got was quite the opposite.

Firstly, the duration of the experience was misleading. They claimed it would be around 40-50 minutes long, but in reality, we were done in less than 15 minutes. It felt like a rushed and overpriced experience.

The attractions themselves were also quite boring. My kids and I didn't find them engaging or entertaining, and it seemed like they were just going through the motions.

The only highlights of our visit were the toboggan and the pool full of plastic balls. My kids seemed to enjoy those parts, but it wasn't enough to make up for the rest of the experience. My kids started crying ad they were looking for more things to do but by that time we were already by the exit.

Overall, I wouldn't recommend The Sweet Space museum to others. It felt like a scam, and the lack of transparency about the experience duration was frustrating. Maybe they can work on improving their attractions and being more honest about what visitors can expect.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCYATxwx6h_tORsyiHHhdMY&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-8-17"},{"Name":"Deborah Laks","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI3grBV_SHuXFTJao3Rutge7Qtph1-vhD5tPBYjbJL4SV7esFjt=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A modern museum with a childhood twist. They give you sweets at every stop. When we went the entire once was decorated for Halloween. A nice place to enjoy with your kids.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAPsDf66iV5GVDHHBkIymuV&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-10-28"},{"Name":"Maria Manduca","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLp8c_vw_Rx8IXWepmHa2TGsRjR-x5UtvczrqYHPDzQrUiybA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We spent a different day. My youngest son enjoyed it the most, especially because there were many other children there and he was able to find some company. We had a good time.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Péter Tircsi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVCRIZAxUz_FZObiv4KZppFU21hoNLseK-a-gh8Cb_X1Z9ISo_TyQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Omg, it is such a great place to spend an hour or more. Each room is completely different, not only because of the art, but also the experience just to look at them, touch them, feel them, and have different activities, a lot to see and enjoy. They even decorated the whole place with Halloween decoration in October. We enjoyed the sweets as well which we got in the different rooms. The sour one was very sour! :) Highly recommended, don’t forget to wear clothes which have white writing/art on them because there are a lot of rooms where it is glowing in the dark :)","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICXhYvSCg&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2024-10-18"},{"Name":"Susan Welsh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLI95JpjyBBVNTLcOjn5b433GELt5Zil0HtBBzWebLQRgjvGA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"A few random rooms with lights and painted walls and some decorations is meant to give feeling you are in space. You are given a total of 4 sweets 1 per room. Go down a slide with a small ball pit. Shop at the exit sells a few sweets and cheap toys. VR game is good but in a separate room after you’ve left. Nothing to do with sweets or space or a museum.
Really disappointing don’t waste your money!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alksjndrs F","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKoOWDYxbjIE-rwV5dmL-TzkAaglNsNFj1MjdX8QuGJ54Nfi_E=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Quite a rip-off. Paid 40€ for 2 adults and a three year old. It’s not really a playground more like a wannabe instagram picture place. In everything the kids get sweets. Total tour takes you maybe 20 minutes. We had a group of 20 including kids which was way too many people for some of the rooms.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCeCydmfjMefZ3TaCcEAfVN&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-8-6"}]

[{"Name":"Daniel Alqsous","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKw4rPao_9b4IaexHZnv92NM7flFJ-AguwiAkwBhOkX2SRiRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of the coolest museums i have ever visited. Honestly really cool. It has 2 floors, with the ground floor being more or less basic, but once you go to the first, there are 6 rooms, each with its own story and description, and they are all so unique - it is all quite incredible. Then in the 2nd floor, there is a journey in and of itself. So many rooms, so many cool things to see, it is nothing short of spectacular.

Also, students have free entry!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB-Xb1BgfgezadMSHinMeIK&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2026-1-19"},{"Name":"Roberto Alonso","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJsK6knQRzXtOKSNodgOVqK9_qVAJQVVLWksEZp11iJw85uRg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such an amazing place. Very well preserved, like machine travel, I can really imagine how the people lived there back in time. Super cheap, really worth the visit. The museum workers are very very friendly and kind, they even make a game to keep my kid busy! We went around 12 on a weekday and we entered right away, no line up!!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCmz5DxtyISIVZ0iBvqrDAq&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2026-1-14"},{"Name":"Denisa Boar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUz4j1MccDX5a9w1bCEZ9L9NF0O4fR0gp_RcR6pqDA-cwFMYfY2=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fantastic place, filled with history and culture. Gratiously preserved. Compared to other such establishments from Vienna and Budapest, this offered much more. Only 6 persons at a time, plenty of space to enjoy and breathable even during busy Christmas period. 2.5 euro for the audio guide which was perfect.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCS1jdiN3EOrCUpIVqSZy4D&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2026-1-1"},{"Name":"Cansu M.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJSODTQLbwWbxxIrjR2ZzHKegLDqIqmrjqlh8TQRt2kRdKByg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A beautiful hidden gem in Madrid. Elegant, peaceful, and like stepping into a 19th-century palace. Absolutely worth a visit. We visited at 17:00 on Thursday and the entrance that time was free. We had to wait around 10-15 minutes though to enter.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC9F5wHmHVCRrknC2XVkZuO&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2026-1-27"},{"Name":"Pei C","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjULBN9fst04m1x5gWQB5UkBv0a2ijNs0E6qe-10r07nWai24832=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It’s a hidden gem in Madrid because it’s small and not as famous as other museums or palace! But it’s stunning and beautiful! Absolutely luxurious!

Another good thing is they only allow 40 visitors on time so there’s no crowds. The cons is you have to queue outside a bit longer

They have free entry slot during 6-7pm on Thursdays but since the ticket is only 3€, not worth to queue and squeeze the visit time ( because once you enter, they are about to close)","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAjJq0vn4YjUXExaFJ60FQg&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2025-10-19"},{"Name":"Galina Strekalova","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJLaWdtg_g2DT5-dkryTOlAcyFpNSXySBRsYiKEROF5cPVkXQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Absolutely breathtaking place.
All the enthusiastic words in the world wouldn’t be enough to describe this beauty.
We waited in line for about 40 minutes, but it was absolutely worth it!
Come early so you can enjoy all the masterpieces for longer. And the price is just as delightful — only €3!
A place that deserves a 100% recommendation.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCtbkgeFRjqyR6JOKB6lP9j&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2025-10-16"},{"Name":"Yulia Alexandrovna","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWY5tSODWWVGrpgiTA-avxaEm0Z_IURkK764c6l9it8LWzalL3_=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Absolutely beautiful museum and its collection!
Some practicalities can help enjoying the museum even more - audio guide via headsets so that your hands are free. As otherwise you hold everything in your hands (not even a small bag is allowed inside) which is very inconvenient","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAxn3jfe8g1l_7U_yWQmKWA&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2025-12-13"},{"Name":"eh m","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJDc4S4npu6fVJbaYEi1ib1lumKNzCIEZg_ypimQordOgj_nw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo de Cerralbo is one of those places where you can genuinely imagine how people once lived. The rooms are preserved with such care that it feels natural to step into the daily life of another era. It’s fascinating to walk through the house, from the grand staircase to the quieter corners, and the clear descriptions make it easy to follow the story of the family and the collection. A charming and atmospheric place;  highly recommended.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBThl4J40qEvXdEKoJhUZ1d&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2025-11-29"}]

[{"Name":"Szabó Niki","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV84fSuMdL2CvUMYb8KTIx8o8N_acVCSyv6Jhzs_qi8PGzXEFft=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Too expensive for what it is. There’s only 2 room, and we thought there will be more to see. There was only a few people inside and wasn’t any line at the entrance.
I really liked the horror part though, that was pretty cool.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB_ebNOiQ5DETbdlRgz2VtP&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2025-10-22"},{"Name":"Elona Mendelovich","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXAv8jvh5kGg_s9ACrPeqXHq8uDb5qWUTkl7ZQw69QCYwrK5-qNUw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The place is well-designed and nice with unique themes. A tourist has a little difficulty understanding some of the displays or characters, but I'm sure locals understand everything. Horror movie lovers have an impressive portion of the display.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDEnRRUHgJ1T6epDxqx-1OE&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2025-10-16"},{"Name":"Ray Recce","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVaLUzLvo5cjXZS1ZzCMxys31WGA7W0ybQPm3fN8OEkc5vtYjM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We had a nice visit with family, we specially liked the horror part. A lot of famous people to see and the train was also a nice experience. We bought the tickets online but there was no queue at the museum.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC7jsjMdA&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-8-15"},{"Name":"Mariangela de Gemmis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXdSMyx0obDpTCrQyAZqsYS8HR7RH0Kbw6FCjqH_fUIMVrn6l0OPQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very nice experience, would recommend. In the ticket are included 3 "galleries" to visit, a small fun horror area upstairs and a "train of terror" ride, all inside of that building! I was surprised by how big in reality it is, they planned it very well.

The statues are incredibly detailed but some of them feel cramped and out of place? (Like Leonardo Di Caprio's statue in a modern suit in the Western area). But overall I loved the museum!

They also have an AR experience on their website which you can activate through the QR codes! They give additional information about the characters in display and activities that make you temporarely unlock filters for instagram and tiktok, it's very cute. Only flaw is that the website was very slow and because of that it took a lot of time for me to visit the exposition... almost 3 hrs! But you will take way less if you speed up or ignore the QR codes I guess. Your choice!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDbr93UBQ&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-8-10"},{"Name":"Vivo Vázquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvUyRyXlZBRb-TT4BhrnwZ8T78FNkcDjPnfNdsrNdFZg9teg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo de Cera in Madrid is a fun and entertaining place for people of all ages. The museum showcases a wide variety of wax figures, including both Spanish and international celebrities, famous athletes, and historical personalities. The dedicated horror section adds a unique touch of excitement. Staff are helpful, and the experience is well organized, with easy access and no long queues. If you enjoy exploring cultural attractions or taking creative photos, this museum is an enjoyable stop during your visit to Madrid.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Cherry Blossom","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVzTc_9PiI119Xy0q0bT8F1EkxZ5bBoeD_190WYbA3Wn9pk_BJ_=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Only some of the attendants are helpful, the others would just stare at you instead of showing you the way to go (not marked otherwise).
The museum has got a majority of Spanish personalities/ celebrities. It's got a dedicated horror upstairs, although it all feels a bit creepy. 😄","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chryso Konstantinou","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVak3LGZKu-DY7RPzeR_-fFfSuk4zZ5ICrZySNLg5dSR4y4d1cZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"We visited the Wax Museum and were very disappointed with the top floor experience. It is supposed to be entertaining and fun, not something that scares visitors away. The staff member upstairs yesterday really frightened us — he chased us almost to the very end. I was with my 7-year-old son, and in the end we had to turn back and leave because we were both so terrified that it was no longer enjoyable.

There should be limits to this kind of performance. It’s fine to create a spooky atmosphere, but not to pursue visitors all the way through. That only ruins the experience and achieves the opposite of what it should.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Johnny Rodriguez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWiul4NlavDOHRZ5rs97Ih05Ja0sL6bIWBFTlxJECvl0fKzmRGx=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"During my recent visit to the wax museum in Madrid, I had the pleasure of being guided by Yini, an outstanding tour guide who truly enhanced my experience. Yini's knowledge and enthusiasm brought each wax figure to life, making the tour both educational and entertaining. Her engaging storytelling and passion for the museum's exhibits made the visit truly memorable. I highly recommend taking a tour with Yini for a fantastic and insightful journey through the world of wax figures. Thank you, Yini, for your wonderful guidance and for making my visit to the museum so special!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICr_YfezAE&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-7-10"}]

[{"Name":"CL","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUgDtXnIstkvtpC5PYSNwYYfUq1ihzwRYa2mp6xqbIwT7BTqRg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Love the location of this stadium away from the busy Madrid city life!!! I did the tour before tonight’s champs league match which I’m going too!! Can’t wait!! What amazing stadium and history of this footy club!! Plus I enjoyed this tour to Real Madrid because it was less strict!! The reason why I did not give this stadium tour a 5 star rating is because the toilet situation that is the only flaw of this tour","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBWGgs9aSH_93YK3Gg_dleu&fid=0x0:0x79fe50ba6b4139dc"],"When":"2026-1-28"},{"Name":"Willem Vroegh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ4Nf_23L7sPj_TB4tj-mO26pBwpjcyBt7XuSQIikM__MDivg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The guided tour with Sergio was AMAZING. He shared so much great information and was patient with all my questions. The group wasn’t rushed and he gave plenty of time for pictures. He would suggest to English speakers to take photos while he went over info and Spanish or the other way around d if he started in English. The trophy room
was impressive.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCqiO9WLLM-hhIYh5rHTipW&fid=0x0:0x79fe50ba6b4139dc"],"When":"2025-8-27"},{"Name":"Tom Hsu","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU1flMM5VFFxDSBKboTZO8YX9JL5yC3NVROztkw1LxTJpvnyqOP=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Warning first: no bathrooom on a July weekday afternoon. You have to go out to a restaurant outside to get a bathroom after paying for a drink. Everything else is closed. The staff was not helpful. They could only say go outside from the atleti store and left or right. For that, I removed two stars. I had to endure taking my young ones outside in 95 plus heat for 15 minutes to find one.

Otherwise, I find the museum entertaining. We spent one hour there before the four o’clock tour began. I paid for basic which did not include the audio guide. However I enjoyed seeing the locker room, presidential section, and players’ entrance.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC6YcfXlCuFc0kdBsJL2Zdj&fid=0x0:0x79fe50ba6b4139dc"],"When":"2025-7-3"},{"Name":"Arjun Ghattaura","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVahdn9od80h24TDjAg4zt6Chpuf4BRnKiIjEjs-cehAKeYuuOI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We chose the Atlético Madrid tour and were impressed. The Metropolitano stadium tour gives you incredible access to the stadium, letting you experience what it's like to be a player. We saw the press box, changing rooms, players' tunnel, and even the presidential suite. The museum was also a great addition to the tour.
We visited midweek around noon, which was a great tip as it wasn't crowded at all. The staff were friendly and let us explore at our own pace. The stadium is new and impressive, with excellent facilities. It's easy to get to via the Metro, and the short journey outside the city center is well worth it.

Highly recommend this over any other stadium tour in Madrid due to the access to different areas in the stadium.

Tip - recommend to purchase tickets online before. This will let you skip any queue and go straight into the tour entrance.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAk0FUp61UOr8CyKe3SKE-M&fid=0x0:0x79fe50ba6b4139dc"],"When":"2025-8-5"},{"Name":"Thais Cannell","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLTs5vhy2yVVfQX7euGiI94f4kOXWO3ibC0_tugwcAxKAo22w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Lourdes was an amazing tour guide ! The visit was really pleasant","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB-8DUlL8Wr1ja26trBw5i1&fid=0x0:0x79fe50ba6b4139dc"],"When":"2026-2-15"},{"Name":"Johnny Sutherland","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWGQoj7Rbpy1ttsB_XvW8_hjPwVhF9Bl1AGvUJQ4YhMjVTCjraDJw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We took part in the visit to the museum and the stadium tour. It is well worth a visit. The museum is excellent with numerous activities for all ages. The staff were excellent, very helpful and informative. One can feel the passion for this club. The multilingual tour guide, Andrés, was very knowledgeable and answered our many questions. Thank you tour staff for making this such a memorable time. Much appreciated.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICznLTKFQ&fid=0x0:0x79fe50ba6b4139dc"],"When":"2024-5-31"},{"Name":"David A","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVN8wd7C-7B9UolsIaQQwFGqL7Rgm7FUl1GZDRn54lTJvUqIDAcCQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Just done the guided tour and it was great. Gabriel was very knowledgeable, done the tour in multiple languages and answered any questions. Bonus points to the reception for charging my phone for me as it was nearly out of battery!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Roger Urech","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWCXv2_eotkXkT4FzIedabV5uvwuaPF8zliHLhZ38ZIDNTunAY=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It was a fantastic experience, and I highly recommend the Atleti tour and the museum. A very friendly organization with very friendly staff. A huge thank you, especially to Adrian 😊 I was the only one in the group who didn't understand Spanish, and he went out of his way to explain everything to me in English. It was a truly wonderful experience that Adrian made possible, so thank you again 😊","Images":null,"When":""}]

I would recommend visiting the museum even before visiting the arena itself, but you can only go to the museum with a purchase for a complete visit and tour of the arena itself.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"alex bonick","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXkPghn8i7xhpHglppxQsvurVbfn5dPGMviaRBf6zBGml_SbTTbNQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of the most amazing museums we visited in a city full of amazing museums! The audio tour is well done.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDJ5NvsnwE\u0026fid=0x0:0x44c4b062b0745a08"],"When":"2023-7-11"},{"Name":"Geoff Marshall (Geoff M)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLFsK-oHNx3harVywAojKLdP8J_WuhShUkfvh5MjRtcZyvhonk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Interesting, but not enough for an audio tour around an empty sports/entertainment arena.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB8N4kITiyV-uZXiAq5U28h\u0026fid=0x0:0x44c4b062b0745a08"],"When":"2025-10-22"},{"Name":"Elena Buzády","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUoABMBrKWTk5mTH8JKqU4_OzVGL695fKSLXFj2L6U2CYC93QFp0Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Worth a visit is the free exhibition on the first floor","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCkTHn2MiO4WRvh2pIHHhST\u0026fid=0x0:0x44c4b062b0745a08"],"When":"2026-2-7"},{"Name":"Tim De Buyst","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWQ8zML35cGmHJJCaD_rwqe70a2-zdFYV0O0_FJjB9C3uKUkwe9JQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"You learn a lot about the history and culture of Bull fighting in Spain. Our guide told us everything we needed and wanted to know passionately.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA2pV55M9g3SxGc5tv3ft_D\u0026fid=0x0:0x44c4b062b0745a08"],"When":"2025-6-16"},{"Name":"Luis EValdez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXTKIkfydHmbAtjsxgfW-zpmsu59RiDvgMI41cYbvMLuTiI47Me=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo Taurino de Madrid is a must-visit for anyone interested in the history and culture of bullfighting. Photos are not allowed, so you’ll hardly find any online—this is something you must experience firsthand, whether you support tauromaquia or not. I’d recommend developing your judgment after getting properly informed about its history and significance.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCwiYeK5gE\u0026fid=0x0:0x44c4b062b0745a08"],"When":"2025-3-19"},{"Name":"Jussef Nader","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJN_OTQzRiNquudSvA7aYn8FI_mC5q82zeJ4Z2bIYQ3jK92eA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"What a great experience it was to visit Madrid's main bullfighting ring. It's a Spanish tradition and to understand bullfighting, you need to know Spanish history, that's a lot of history. In any case I truly enjoyed it and it's a short ride on the bus or metro from El Centro de Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDW6fuU8QE\u0026fid=0x0:0x44c4b062b0745a08"],"When":"2022-3-28"},{"Name":"Dima Harba","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW8zU98vEhljHaoAfzLwzkDDijlGADqStNR2IaWMHrbZ-V-1_oU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"This sport or shows or whatever they are called are pure cruel. I don't understand the enjoyment in seeing an animal being stabbed to death. This is so immoral. The museum is currently under renovation. The architecture is nice though.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICko_yiDA\u0026fid=0x0:0x44c4b062b0745a08"],"When":"2018-8-22"}]

[{"Name":"Kelly Sidney","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVuUqfKbkpL015_LwC-awHUYq0K5---lp_AbgGyRQ5gkxchN7hR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such a beautiful collection of Picasso, Salvator Dali, and Joan Miró pieces… amongst several other talented artists ! I preferred this museum significantly over the Prado; it had much better organization and ease to find pieces. The Guernica in person was magnificent. It is a very large museum which you can spend at least 2-3 hours inside. If you are on a time crunch, the temporary contemporary exhibitions were pleasant as well but less a standout compared to the permanent collection.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhALWZzEXq-0QAfRjRAOzQ64&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-2-6"},{"Name":"Alex Veziro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV_prC4j81QgIKfVHsb1zgnDfGKJ6o0QVeve6gytMISddtq28WetA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo Reina Sofía is one of those museums that leaves a strong impression even if you’re not particularly into modern art. The space itself is beautiful, with plenty of natural light and a calm flow that makes the visit feel relaxed rather than tiring, even if you stay for a while. The mix between the historic building and the more contemporary elements works really well and fits the character of the exhibitions.
The main highlight is of course Picasso’s Guernica, which feels completely different in person and has a kind of intensity that makes you stop and take your time with it. Beyond that, the collection as a whole is quite engaging, with some works that make you think and others that you simply enjoy visually, without feeling the need to overanalyze them.
Overall, it’s definitely a museum worth visiting if you’re in Madrid. It doesn’t feel boring or cold, and you leave with the sense that you’ve experienced something meaningful, even if you don’t remember every artist or movement afterward. It’s also great for a more laid-back visit, without the pressure of having to see absolutely everything.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDqj6phukx3ZgfKwfFS17Oq&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-1-4"},{"Name":"Tata Kutaladze","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK0DVJn4bsWYsDSJlAwlQE2Cku3Sdvyo6gUgL7mt6PQUegQAg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My main reason for visiting the Reina Sofía Museum was Picasso and his Guernica, and it was absolutely worth it. Seeing Guernica in person is a powerful and emotional experience — the scale, details, and meaning are far more intense than in any book or photo.
Dali and Miro are  impressive as well.
A practical tip: buy your ticket online. The entrance line is much shorter compared to waiting in front of the ticket office, which saves a lot of time. Overall, a must-visit museum in Madrid, especially for anyone interested in modern art and Picasso’s legacy.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDA4ckpcuwpD0Hvb1bUJ9Ky&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-1-14"},{"Name":"I Berezner","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVpuR88LJbO2mGM0lgRoSSgPom1qNaDTbZc_75JhdnFpfi3w3af7A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Museo Nacional Centro de Arte Reina Sofía stands out as Madrid's premier modern art destination, housing an exceptional collection that spans surrealism to abstract expressionism. Picasso's monumental Guernica commands the spotlight, its raw power amplified by surrounding works that contextualize the Spanish Civil War's devastation. Dalí's melting clocks, Miró's playful abstractions, and international gems like Rothko create a dynamic narrative of 20th-century creativity and turmoil. The renovated hospital building blends airy glass extensions with historic stone, offering light-filled galleries and serene courtyards perfect for reflection. Navigation is intuitive, though the sheer scale rewards a focused itinerary—prioritize floors 2 and 4 for highlights. Temporary exhibits often steal the show, adding fresh energy. Despite occasional crowds around Guernica, the museum's thoughtful spacing ensures an immersive experience. Reina Sofía isn't just art—it's a profound dialogue with history, making it indispensable for cultured travelers","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDB_o3oMjw8-sZEWxSHwF_s&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-12-23"},{"Name":"Asher Mendelsohn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKqRvYW-a_lSUaf5iTtmw6DBfVcf7TfVR491K28IHEfLs9SorY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wonderful museum. Very easy to get in even though it was a crowded Sunday morning. You can pre-buy your art pass (covers 2 other museums as well) or buy a pass at the gate. The queue to buy the pass was longer to enter the museum, so I recommend pre buying the art pass online. The museum is quite big, and there are multiple floors, however when I visited only floors 1 and 2 were open. The world famous Guernica painting by Pablo Picasso is located on the second floor. I like how there’s a cloakroom where you can store all your valuables, and bags. There’s also lots of toilets around the museum. The museum contains a large amount of paintings and other work too, many of them being from Picasso, Dali, and more. But the most popular of all is Guernica, and that can be seen by the sheer amount of people standing around it. Overall museum is great and I enjoy the large collection of art.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAXUzvqZ48DFCyk6ib2H715&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-12-22"},{"Name":"Nicos Stathis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKc-rWmYLiJLBz4mtw01fjprIyd-zvV9OdX_F75kJp_dWx4RvY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I believe one of the most important museums in the world.

It is not just the Guernica, which is astonishing and pivotal indeed, the whole exhibition is a great story telling of how things evolved from late 1800s to mid 1900s, and many artworks and references of some of the most iconic modernism enthusiasts, for example Le Corbusier.

I visited on a Monday, I cannot say it was crowded.

The facade restoration keeps going, not affecting the visitor’s experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBd0vRqhvICgS52QH0HDzpT&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-2-3"},{"Name":"Simona","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXIxl36BDvWwaZaehYUjB_o5qPqD55SL2EAL5iQDj96hxaCIkEj=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An amazing gallery, which concentrates on spanish art, but with some very interesting and valuable international links too.
I went on a sunday in the free hour 12:30-14:15 for which i would recommend being there 30 minute earlier since obviously many people do this🤭. Still despite the long queue, people were getting in pretty quickly.
When in, the permanent paintings, distributed in the halls by style (cubism, post cubism, surrealism, etc.) were on the second floor (3rd and 4th floor were temporarily closed).
On the first floor there was an amazing exhibit by Maruja Mallo (i’ll post photos of some of her paintings).","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCbj5NOy8CEzlG6-hG8v7uT&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-11-3"},{"Name":"Neda Pavlovic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWa59zCZJWVxSL2SdtoKVSif7DYFnayRKCeB7JBshDyK-fQVdrC=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Reina Sofía is a museum you shouldn’t skip in Madrid. While many go straight to the Prado, Reina Sofía offers a modern, refreshing art experience with masterpieces like Picasso’s Guernica. A big plus is that photography is allowed, unlike in the Prado, which makes the visit more relaxed and enjoyable. The museum café also serves great coffee, perfect for a quick break. Overall, a must-visit spot for art lovers.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAPVrVcWtyDWZpO40p6k-l5&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-12-8"}]

[{"Name":"Jaspal Singh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXKoK6lQQKruIpwS2QBDpwAj__9wnNd6RsyiWPI2xdMIy04VT-H=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"February 2026 5*

Visited the museum on Friday evening. I spent 3 hours and could not finish it half of it. This place is very amazing and beautiful collection. I really want to also shout out for staff who are excellent. I bought ticket online from GetYourGuide but did not get it. The staff issued me ticket on the spot.

There is big art collection from different era. There are 3 floors and covering different segments of different centuries. It is so interesting to see the collection. You can get audio tour from the museum.

One of the floor is closed which is covering Arabic art. You can see Hispanic and Roman arts.

The place is fully accessible and will need good time.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAn_Df4pAfgFNOFyHtdqjEX&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-21"},{"Name":"Sophie Macfarland","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKkDSyC73O6U7wn67SRhdMV7tLfHakEHRayagFeeQe5pM9JzA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This was a great stop!

The ticket is very cheap and the museum walks through Spanish history (starting with pre-history). The collection, while good, is not good enough to warrant a long visit. However, the signs and curation are phenomenal (English signage is included). The outline and explanation of history was great and I learned a ton. This was a perfect foundation for the rest of my trip to Spain, bouncing between Phoenician, Roman, Visigoithic, Islamic, and Catholic sites.

In particular the maps were amazing and I loved the late Roman mosaics.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA-lfZOKY30aOAJGmJQApKR&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-9"},{"Name":"Michael Offord","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUCYcnQ9p_fF7bpz5p0dlxJtJ-6yRZkgizWzoZieS9pO2nu0Dw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a quiet but very good museum detailing the early history of Spain, from pre-history to the Romans. There is also an excellent permanent exhibit on Egypt.

Some carvings are top quality. This is not the same size as the British Museum or the Louvre, but is very visitable and a notable collection of Roman emperor busts, though unlike many other former Roman provinces, Spain has not centralised their Roman archaeology, and much of it remains in regional museums.

Very easy to get in with no queue and extremely reasonably priced. Also modern facilities.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBDVxuKkhh1jH8Zit6CxnO-&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-20"},{"Name":"Chiahsun Liao","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV4TDJ6SlyonvuEbjhTra3qvN1QpGzvmehP9hkS97J4t9BvqKFH=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A must-visit museum for history enthusiasts. The collection is highly informative and well presented throughout, covering major human civilizations across the historical timeline with impressive depth. It was surprising to see how lightly visited it was, given the world class quality of the exhibits. It is one of the few museums in Madrid that provides thorough and professional English explanations for every exhibit. The museum also offers very convenient amenities, including spacious restrooms on every floor and a self-help cloakroom that can fit in carry-on sized luggage, which can be a huge bonus for tourists. You can easily lose yourself in the river of history and spend an entire day exploring. A true hidden gem and, in my opinion, the best museum in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD8emR2DIgqvJHvvDaWlXcv&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2025-12-13"},{"Name":"Daria Khenkina","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXNRdxiUGq7jBgczsAL2sQjH8oqhoDG11AET9kNczYnHhWopYPx=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of the best museums I've been to. Rich and diverse collection, very beautiful artifacts. Definitely recommend. Spent here about 2 hours,   but would prefer to spend more","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDsJm2IPD_TBFrx2jpnu8I2&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-14"},{"Name":"Bob Meadow","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJU2pLYy4QH7AEa64d-1yxozoqEo9N-RU-m9HK-wQ0knCCn3g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Located next to the National Library, the National Museum of Archeology offers an amazing journey through the history of Spain from the Paleolithic to the 19th century. The collection includes artifacts dating back to prehistoric times, the Iberian peoples, Roman Hispania, the Visigothic kingdom, Al-Andalus, the Middle Ages, the Modern era and so on. On the other hand, the collection of the ancient Greek and Egyptian civilizations located on the second floor, is really enjoyable to explore thanks to the quality and quantity of the pieces. And all this for just 3€. Don't miss it.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDbzyA8HM2TEYP5Hgye9jbx&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-1-18"},{"Name":"Elaine Huang","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLbBOdrqJ3ChpJY9ADZrIwE3kyztaS4mN7ayUXI_HJtKjf7PA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I participated a guided tour and Sophia gave me a fantastic experience. She shared a lot of information and unknown stories beyond audio guide (the app is really helpful as well). This museum gives me a good reason to come back to Madrid and I would love to stay in the museum whole day by then ~","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBvp-6TJ5VCxKHIGZM1JcP1&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-1-17"},{"Name":"Sandra Gregan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUAzKIkf4GjI17qG_PAe8kJGNxVGaF6ZI_6fNjp_9a6xaxwo508=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of my favorite experiences while in Madrid. Well thought out floor plan and displays. Easy to navigate and amazing exploration of history from the beginning of time. We purchased tickets online prior to entering. Very easy process and only €3/person which was a steal, I would have easily paid more. Highly recommend!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBQCFNJfjVT1IZuzPeu8Ejg&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-1-16"}]

La gestión cultural se hace visible por todos lados. Hay en el museo la exposición de sala permanente sobre el origen del municipio y sus tres mil años de historia,lo cuál es increíble.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Criben Ridro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWbsI4aZVYfPeQ-UjQS3ChrB0AnUUXBy6RZgkJ5KEGbw5qUDe6q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo en el que puedes conocer la historia de la ciudad de Torrejon, en nuestro caso pudimos acudir a una visita teatralizada para conocer su historia a través de emblemáticos personajes famosos en Torrejon de Ardoz. Sin duda una agradable experiencia que nos gustó mucho.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDhtaTIOw&fid=0x0:0xa8481830423fbcd1"],"When":"2023-3-14"},{"Name":"Edward Puelles Valle","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUu_kIT47KJY7y6zDVya4LoTehSBXUNlw2hkViLWYggc2X4L64C-g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un Museo 🖼️ que te brinda acceso libre para poder apreciar parte de la historia de Torrejón de Ardóz, desde muchos años atrás desde sus ancestros del Siglo XVIII en promedio
Además con murales y también restos arqueológicos
Cuenta con pantallas para poder ilustrar al visitante de lo que fue la emancipación y la vida en su evolución en sus diferentes etapas
Con un resúmen de su población desde 1526 con 600 pobladores y 140 viviendas, hasta la última encuesta de población del 2021 de 132,771 personas y una población en viviendas de 51,547 viviendas
Muy bonito y muy ordenado con buena luz y con secuencias muy bien indicadas
Recomendado visitarlo en los días de atención","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIClo8moBw&fid=0x0:0xa8481830423fbcd1"],"When":"2023-11-25"},{"Name":"Jörge","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWXeWEXqdr5t3OlbRokb_Dhsg38mDGwuZo9JWUkx-spF0MuHs0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo inaugurado en el año 2011, dedicado a explicar la historia del municipio de Torrejón de Ardoz. Se encuentra situado en el antiguo edificio de la Herrería, y cuenta con tres plantas: en la primera se encuentra una exposición sobre la arqueología de la localidad, principalmente con los descubrimientos realizados en el yacimiento de Soto de Henares, contando con reproducciones de los hallazgos y piezas originales. En la segunda planta se encuentra la exposición que abarca desde la Edad Media hasta la actualidad, mientras que en la tercera se encuentra un espacio destinado a las exposiciones temporales","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDN1aDM2AE&fid=0x0:0xa8481830423fbcd1"],"When":"2024-1-24"},{"Name":"Luis Garcia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW235KrchEY7jAU2usKj6vTT7QhXWWMu0VoebVS3GjLutwJAk3y=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Lugar de arte y cultura. Exposiciones temporales interesantes. En el sótano tienen una exposición permanente de Gigantes y Cabezudos, que se puede visitar los domingos.","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"G Mac","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfFY2KM7hfNlooonhvV8_u_Q6GiEzn9xdqejVwqehpMdOIKlCRug=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a fantastic building with some wonderful artworks. The ground floor has some student works many of true quality and talent. You descend one floor to see an exhibition of graphic arts from the early 1900s on. You descend to the main exhibition space which has a moving collection of work depiction the trauma of immigration and the victims of this dangerous journey via sea.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAKVHdLqOJeu4cq3_Ew79dC&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2025-9-24"},{"Name":"S Bristol","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI0zVxZqdB273yxQV1VinjzNJsA0sZ28LlFSrl4rEY9BlkzSg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An excellent museum in a lively neighborhood with many independent cafés, bars and restaurants. A history of the early Spanish illustrated magazine  ' White and Black ', showing many covers and early cartoons in chronological order from 1901. Varied artistic illustrations showcasing the ' modern life', politics etc. Other exhibitions on more floors. We visited towards the end of the day,  and could have easily spent 2 hours there, plus visiting the cafe. Recommended!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDzmOedbw&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2024-6-7"},{"Name":"Marc G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK2hJP8h7COFWvZMrwQPcpLDair4JLlEimwAnddDETKQNGIvV5s=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Loved the Museum and the (free!) guided tour. Wonderful to go from 1890ish all the way to 2000 in history through caricature, illustration and art… don’t miss out on visiting this place! I can confirm that very young & upwards will enjoy this colourful and fun exhibition. Most pieces are driven by action & personality of the subjects, or by the relatable situation of the historic moment they were created in.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDTkKuJOQ&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2024-5-21"},{"Name":"Will Finley","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVE9RXbySJxSR7Ize1IBsmiQkteh52ZtMoGsP7LqIwsVeMif9Er=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Not so much a museum as it is a book store. Which by itself is amazing. It is the largest selection of design books I've seen in one place. Very interesting stuff from all over the world with beautiful design. The building is also fantastic. The metal panel facade is striking and the underlit floor modules are really cool looking at night. They gate off the courtyard when it gets dark though. The museum is not exceptionally inspiring, but you should still go up to the second level to walk through the connector.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDE_-TNHg&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2017-6-14"},{"Name":"Elisabetta Borzini","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWW9OaJFHYeOlmWeyc9v25DLabeFhCxN7jRlbMj8RkcjNuLAtMf=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"So, the museum is actually closed but the exhibition on the ground floor is open (and free).
It’s an exhibition about transgender people, trans art and trans artists. I didn’t expect it and I’m so grateful we stumbled open it because it was so heartwarming and marvellous. Thank you for this space!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICFjay90wE&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2023-10-30"},{"Name":"Christian Jimenez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKJschGurXRAeIT0xo2IXQDdEoBMGg9nvZnG8j56pfgoYf7jg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Free Museum featuring Illustration work as well as some digital work. user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Dean Hobson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIF7jS2r5GGbILesYau6uSZnbIOoTXKZy7V-ChylDM7fsDzkLU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Truly, one of the great galleries of the world. Allow a minimum of three hours to visit, and this is a quickly as you could possibly do it, and that's only giving you a brief look. Be there for the opening, it's advisable to buy your tickets in advance, but tickets are available on the day.... DO NOT BUY TICKETS FROM A SECONDARY WEBSITE, YOU DON'T NEED TO.

There are a number of cafes within the museum, so take a break and a deep breath. If you are wondering about a visit, just come, you won't regret it.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBfy4PrrKd78BnlSzqNVrfx&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-2-20"},{"Name":"Sveto Rakovic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXELv1h6KNSxjLBZ4irfEkGKjzrypoUHg3izpRjZZ_yFLu388A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The most famous, the most visited (more than a million people a year) and the largest museum in Madrid. I recommend buying tickets in advance so you don't spend too much on the entrance to the museum. The museum primarily contains paintings by famous Spanish painters (Velázquez, El Greco, Mannings, Goya, etc.) and international painters (such as Rubens). You need 2-3 hours for the tour. What irritates me the most is the forbidden photography (which is not even the case in the Vatican museums) and I will never be able to understand what is achieved by this.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDeMdVw6qZyZhdcvTRHuw7P&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-1-4"},{"Name":"Gabriel Radford","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVwcyb-dRAmO-1NM9nQfZHDz6GtwhkUAPGwtqhfxlDBGuIhY8AwKg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A transformational experience. To see
Such greatness - the best humanity has to offer. Velásquez obviously a highlight. Caravaggio took my breath away. Audio guides very helpful. Staff good. Crowded yes, but still space to see the art. Pre buying tickets definitely a pro move but won’t help avoid a bit of a line.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA4JiJziSda31JjotVGzUNm&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-1-27"},{"Name":"Rae Zyon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUG1QtdDeQPQLETzpCBgyDyGAAEvIdbUpAe1YDVy3QwmQLrwpSA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A magnificent museum, house to famous paintings especially Goya. Absolutely worth the visit, the paintings are beautiful and about three levels of the museum to explore. There's free entry to the museum two hours before closing everyday. Best to be there earlier as the queue gets very long.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDf2hW00MYwX2GfsElbPqd-&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-1-8"},{"Name":"Priya Nagda Omron","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX8HuThTfcNgDC2fnOs9xiSudL-yOJ_wR3gcNtGq2i6x6jtvnQf=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Beautiful museum. We skipped the audio tour, but I’d highly recommend it—there’s so much to take in that it can feel overwhelming without guidance. I also learned that two versions of the Mona Lisa were created at the same time, and the earlier version is housed here (something I had no idea about!). It’s fascinating to see how it differs subtly from the version at the Louvre.

No photos are allowed so I will leave you with a picture of the outside of the museum 🙂","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAm3qp1CmId7FMItVlGChd7&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-1-20"},{"Name":"Gary H","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXVQ8GQfBLsQr8dF9mAzMMkyVfmOldXWUxiSLLn1GUJd55bjyy0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo del Prado is, quite simply, one of the greatest museums in Europe and an essential cultural experience in Madrid. Spread across six expansive floors, it houses an extraordinary collection that feels both monumental and deeply human, offering a powerful journey through the artistic and historical development of Spain.

What makes the Prado truly exceptional is how its collection reflects the evolution of Spanish identity. Through masterpieces spanning centuries, the museum traces Spain’s transformation from an Arab-ruled nation to one developing its own distinct culture, power, and artistic voice. This narrative unfolds seamlessly as you move through the galleries, giving context and meaning to each period rather than presenting art in isolation.

The breadth and quality of the collection are remarkable. Iconic works by Velázquez, Goya, El Greco, Zurbarán, and Ribera sit alongside masterpieces from other European centers, creating a rich dialogue between Spain and the wider artistic world. Each room feels thoughtfully curated, allowing visitors to appreciate both the technical brilliance and emotional depth of the works.

Despite its scale, the Prado never feels overwhelming. The layout is intuitive, the lighting superb, and the atmosphere calm and reverent, inviting visitors to linger, reflect, and truly absorb the art. It is a museum that rewards both first-time visitors and seasoned art lovers.

The Museo del Prado is not simply a collection of paintings; it is a visual history of a nation—its struggles, triumphs, and soul. Profound, moving, and endlessly fascinating, it stands confidently among Europe’s very finest museums.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB8qo90W-d4Te3_RFBIFmFx&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-1-10"},{"Name":"Greg Alford","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVKjj-a8Q9L5aAdj7mt9CvLPiJWdOEMyG8vWgLI0KJjdb0Up4e8MQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Spectacular museum. Probably some little largest paintings and collection of extremely large paintings in the world. If you go after six and I believe this is auto the majority museums in Spain admission is free the lines were pretty long but it went quickly.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBgIJQ5xwinBeF-K95MTusI&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2025-12-21"},{"Name":"bermúdez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUG7HzTtczUBS20m-REqDEHZhqoURjoNr2qW0_ZK8dQNkaGeSU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The “no pictures allowed” policy is quite irrational to me, but alright. Aside from that, it was an extremely pleasant experience—nothing quite as beautiful as pacing three hours around seemingly unending halls covered by breathtaking works of art. Definitely worth the trip.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDqlsy0eMHpoFufpya-bCLd&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-2-6"}]

Entry is free, a beautiful museum preserving history. The museum's got the history of the Spanish. Loads of cool old stuff on display, all laid out really clearly so easy to follow.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBExZULt-3HCI6cxndrKaK2&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2026-1-6"},{"Name":"Naveen Perera","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXo1EOUBZmqNnX9TxAh0YA9pddlpk1dAqohyuijF1dv5BR7eDNB=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very impressive small museum that clearly presents the naval history of Spain. Entry is free, which makes it a great stop even if you have limited time. The museum has multiple detailed models of historic ships, maps, and naval instruments that give a good picture of how Spain became a major maritime power.
There is a nice souvenir shop, but no café, so plan accordingly. The museum is well organised and easy to walk through, and you can comfortably visit it in about 45–60 minutes.
Overall, a worthwhile and informative visit, especially if you are interested in history, exploration, or ship models.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAVglefhROliQ0VvDWRhDkC&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2025-12-24"},{"Name":"Linda Bailey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV13-ZLd5E8gbX7oVINRLkobXoW7aOrzbAdXFra4V8v2e0Q96I=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Naval Museum is located in a gorgeous building with an amazing staircase leading to the museum. Entrance is free with a suggestion donation of 3 euros. There are no lockers and backpacks are not allowed.

The is an audioguide available to use on your phone. Well curated, lovely displays and most placard translated in English describe the incredible history of the Spanish Navy. A must see if interested in all things naval and Spanish  history.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD7vsS9qt10cynH-8O4MnjY&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2025-9-28"},{"Name":"Denisa Boar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUz4j1MccDX5a9w1bCEZ9L9NF0O4fR0gp_RcR6pqDA-cwFMYfY2=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great museum for passionates and noobies. There is something for everyone: from navigation instruments and tools, to boats, accessories, equipments and uniforms, flags, guns, and fabulous paintings.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCH0O4RAFE3SHslWcmA_aCE&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2026-1-1"},{"Name":"Jeremy Lim","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUrc1Ud0khZyXU0C0tbctyuaBLhUoJQiepCDtbzj8M2mEPjfP8i=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Free museum with beautiful ship models and detailed history of the Spanish navy and the journey to the new world. With a visit if you’re interested. Takes about 45min-1hr to walk through.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCU5xl-OCuKupPM3RHkOjbq&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2025-9-8"},{"Name":"Ctgb2","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocItC9iDa9r9g_651odkZll6vFcFOS4APKRO34THBDIzmKIUQQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Naval Museum in Madrid feels like a hidden historical archive rather than a crowded tourist attraction. Located along the Paseo del Prado, it’s easy to overlook — but once inside, you realize it contains centuries of exploration, warfare, and global expansion.

The highlight is the collection of ship models. They’re incredibly detailed, almost architectural in precision, and they help you understand how naval technology evolved over time. You don’t just read history — you see engineering progress in miniature form.

One of the most fascinating pieces is the early world map attributed to Juan de la Cosa. Seeing the Americas represented in one of the first European maps gives a powerful sense of how people once understood the world.

What I appreciated most was the atmosphere. The museum is quiet and organized chronologically, making it easy to follow Spain’s maritime story from exploration to empire to modern navy. It feels educational without being overwhelming.

Overall, the Naval Museum rewards curiosity. It’s not flashy, but it’s deeply informative — a place where history unfolds patiently rather than dramatically.","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"vicetravellers","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqff5QMzzqdnjeGHbPvbc5EDj2433svwF5jcMJTM7qHU5ZLWM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Thyssen-Bornemisza National Museum is an exceptional museum that completes Madrid’s art triangle beautifully. The collection is incredibly well curated, offering a smooth journey through different periods, styles, and artistic movements without feeling overwhelming.

What makes the Thyssen stand out is its balance. From old masters to impressionism and modern works, everything is displayed in a way that feels coherent, calm, and engaging. It is a museum where you can truly take your time and enjoy the art rather than rush through it.

An essential visit in Madrid for anyone interested in painting, culture, and thoughtful curation. Elegant, accessible, and genuinely inspiring.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBdWidapaCnYGeYfb_ZWDy4&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-1-29"},{"Name":"Juliana C M","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXYDWugloZlh79bbKifo16IVCS1CM67BsOBsP__7PPYAZBofac=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing museum that covers almost 700 years of paintings. If you go on Mondays entrance is free from 12:00 till 16:00.  The museum is huge and I would recommend going in different days if you have time. I will add some painting photos :) I used AI to stroll around and experience hand picked paintings and worked really well.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhANYkkmiZmYShE9LLYAzsEs&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-1-12"},{"Name":"KP G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX2Rg9DiQ29ajhUkI-XwqOIh23_chg3Bw9nylChO9JBI-09zvil=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"What a fabulous collection! If you like impressionist and expressionist art, you’d likely enjoy this museum a lot.

Inclusive design, in the sense of having displays for touching, with braille and audio descriptions, for low/no vision visitors. I haven’t seen this attentive detail in any other museum. Bravo to the curators.

Website says Mondays are free. Another great feature of this museum.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAvf1BsJeyyrMDNaQwCaNCt&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-1-21"},{"Name":"Alex Veziro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV_prC4j81QgIKfVHsb1zgnDfGKJ6o0QVeve6gytMISddtq28WetA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Thyssen-Bornemisza Museum offers a very complete and enjoyable experience, both in terms of its collection and its overall atmosphere. The variety of artworks, ranging from classical pieces to modern and contemporary art, keeps the visit engaging without feeling overwhelming. The spaces are calm, well lit and easy to move through, allowing you to take your time and really connect with the works at your own pace.
A major highlight of the museum is the staff, who are genuinely excellent. In contrast to the Prado, the employees here are friendly, approachable and clearly willing to help, and they speak English comfortably, which makes a big difference for international visitors. Whether you need directions, information or simple assistance, the interaction feels relaxed and human rather than rushed or strict.
Another strong advantage is that photography is allowed inside the museum. This adds a lot to the overall experience, as you can capture the artworks that stand out to you and engage with them more personally. Compared to the Prado, where this is not permitted, it makes the visit feel more open and memorable. Overall, the Thyssen-Bornemisza combines quality, comfort and excellent service, making it a museum that feels both impressive and genuinely welcoming.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDSdUxpp7tdukVt8c0Iwv9P&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2025-12-27"},{"Name":"L BM","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVkO6B8IbOnTAt2fcyX0LrY9llf-tZHiPSEaVTMZn9T1FP_WwnS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Beautiful collection! Had a wonderful time admiring the incredible artwork.
An extremely rude tour guide for the museum decided to stand in front of a Caravaggio for nearly 10 minutes as the room filled up with people waiting to see it. She wouldn't move, kept yapping. Even the people in her group realized they were in the way. Eventually someone gently approached her & asked if she could please move aside so that all the rest of the people waiting could view the popular Caravaggio. Her response was that she was with the museum & "there are 800 other works of art you can go look at". I don't care who you work for or how much art there is in the museum, the art is for all of us. Don't be rude.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAqp28PB5JQd5JghpHNSNju&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2025-11-17"},{"Name":"Dana Kay","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVRATWmxn5F6aCV3p5nFeIAGUrPqu7BA-nlAp1x2cUG0B9GB8Bl=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The museum offers an impressive art collection spanning from the early Renaissance to contemporary works. It’s divided into two sections: the permanent exhibition on the first and second floors, and a loaned collection on the ground floor. The galleries are organized by artistic style, so similar works are displayed together, making it easy to appreciate the evolution of each movement. During my visit, I was fortunate to catch two exceptional pairings of exhibitions—Picasso with Klee, and Andy Warhol with Jackson Pollock. It was a rare treat to see these artists presented side by side.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD1xB8M03zFpuGHASrpQF1V&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2025-12-27"},{"Name":"In the pocket Drum studio","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUIXESsegfBQB9BUlhcmYM2BYMAjmIaoxM6W7gpgRocovBrsDw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"totally more than i've expected!
there are not only classical paintings but also lots of pivotal modern painter's master pieces
really impressed by the collections..
stayed 3 hours for whole exibitions💕👍🏼","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBmL4cz-ROreUe_82K3eIJh&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-2-16"},{"Name":"IAN Zhang","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUy_zjCVruENW4GrUB8GggcDbeLevjim5b_QLMkM69oxp_5FS_h=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One ticket costs €14. There are many exhibits, and you’ll need to spend an entire afternoon slowly appreciating them. I also noticed a subtle Republic of China (Taiwan) flag.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAnZ4kJ3TPRfrCAYlixUPmB&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-2-21"}]
