[{"Name":"Dong Cai","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUVDzBsXATZ4chn3Go4FUKsm03lk7jcwsyJ89uTw1eNKA2ebF_c=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"The experience in itself is good.  However, given the small size of the venue and things to do, definitely not worth the 68€ for a family of 5.  Everything could be done and seen in literally 15-20 mins.  Definitely felt like a tourist trap for this venue.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Stefan Nilles","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVcSebPbMGAP-j9hMDMpSkJM2OSZiScSw7Jd2G1MSWm5hmuLuIdjw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Do not visit this place unless your are a Spanish nationalist family with very young children!

The place is catering exclusively to children to teach them about the embroidery on the torrreros' uniform, the tradition of spanish tiles as an art from and other mundane details of Spanish culture that are presented as something Spanish kids should be proud of.

The glacially paced narrative of the projected information is in Spanish (for 3 year olds) and you can read the English translation projected to the wall.

Total waste of money and time.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBxSdMF_EItVZ0NhATVpwun&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Chris Tomkins","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUMdh9K_6GffzOUidLsxnoeKD-7jw1e7YUvNARYkIb04iAytgI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Really good set up. Very engaging immersive experience for the artists. The bit at the end was just novel fun.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCHot09waWMZrBDGpS-w-Z3&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-7-28"},{"Name":"Jonas Montvidas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXykj1DBG41wM8onrJXAQ3usyMsllep8989BjgjWIqriCxo8UQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"It is a hoax. You pay 18 euros to get into two rooms - one bigger and one smaller. The same story about The Last Supper by Davinci goes on a loop in the bigger room, and there are two/three activities for the children in the smaller room. Two short hallways with lights are the main attraction of this place. But even these hallways are just a cheap copy of other museums worldwide that offer better experiences. Not worth 18 euros","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCoqoaZkAE&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-4-16"},{"Name":"Shalom N","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXjmGFJHDuC19RcGGg0lG7mHAePo2CjbG7fd4Pk9sPEsG8DxAUj=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"It was cute for the 10 minutes it lasted for, you can tell that this place was made for influencers to come and take a quick video so others will feel the need to come.

It was one main exhibition room where they just projected some stuff on the wall, then another room where you get to view terrible VR and do some coloring, i got pretty pictures i guess but would i recommend? No because its not giving you value for your money

It is giving very much tourist trap because you leave feeling unsatisfied because it can literally take you 5 minutes to go through it all.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCQ58b0UQ&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-3-7"},{"Name":"Jose Maria Tijerina","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUN2vlCdnJoVVzCMvczORJZNy6fp097DcqnE5mvO5JL7UyEJ4mK=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The immersive experience is very interesting. When I visited, the experience was about the work of Klimt and Van Gogh. I went on a Monday so it was not full. The extra activities besides the immersive experience are more for kids than adults. There are a  virtual painting wall, last for 3 minutes, drawings for kids and virtual reality headsets that were not working when i attended. The narration on the immersive experience is ominous Spanish language, so if you cannot follow it then you would not get  part of the experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCQHraYDrmqxt2VPIpl0y2E&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-7-13"},{"Name":"Ariannaly Pea Martínez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUHlslcBXwCCEYwj-22n12iqaNzJJSfd6xSJOjFHvdkhRKV2mc=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It’s a pretty nice and neat experience. Visuals and music were the best of it, although the interactive room was a bit mid imo. Either way I’d sit hours and hours just to enjoy the Spirit of Japan showcase","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC3vuqCmAE&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2024-11-6"},{"Name":"Doron Privé","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKkdtQAXsQ4Kx4kWjv6_tOt_smbLwFWiIFkEVxSgS1lbwIt1iE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I went there with two friends and it was very disappointing. The tour last maximum 10min and was very expansive. If you are not a Spanish speaker, it’s just a waste of money because they are projecting a kind of movie during the second part of the tour and the it’s 100% in Spanish.
The museum is a first corridor with lights and mirror on 10 meters (it’s the  only cool thing you will see), after that there’s the Spanish movie projected on walls, ground and ceiling and then it’s finished. I told you, it last 10 minutes and cost ~20€/person.","Images":null,"When":""}]

The collections focus on cultural anthropology, presenting materials related to clothing, religion, rituals, daily life, and leisure from Asia, Africa, and the Americas.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDsGnHJ3EcZsrMLJd_JR9gI&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2026-1-10"},{"Name":"Emily L","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIT2mX1D5qRGqic4rzLN5KPD-I-HcTVL6tDeweRX1zvDe3DbcE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Free after 2pm on Saturday and on sundays, lovely quiet museum with interesting exhibits. What a pleasant surprise, very well laid out and filled with great, many from Spain, artefacts. You end up with a great sense of Spain's long history. It normally is € 3 for general admission. A great place to view ancient history that one does not get to see in the USA. It was an honor and treat to see ancient history and artifacts in person. Plus you are allowed to take photos.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDv44rNBg&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2024-12-23"},{"Name":"david howells","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXv00dm90sqOsiG9tuO5orrJOqUEDS-SlSH6CFfk8dmCBLrELi0Jw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Nice museum spread over three floors with friendly staff and just a €3 entry fee. Nicely laid out but the content is quite basic. The exhibits are just informative generally. This is a high school level exhibition really but good enough to pass away an hour or so.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIChmqjJMw&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2023-2-10"},{"Name":"Bret Bowman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVGD8UU6n0pSe3DnnoMF9LV7M8K2xYwXPGsIE1BfmUYZUf70Ir6=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I arrived this morning right at about 11am and was let in free. But the cost of €3 (read in another review below) is will worth your time to see the exhibits on display here.

Wow! Spain has definitely made their historical mark on the world. Ground floor is Asia (mostly Philippines). First floor is Africa. Second floor is Americas. This museum is a great reminder how much influence Spain has had throughout the years of history following their glory years of exploration and colonization.

You can probably get through this museum within an hour if you really rush. Two hours is more appropriate to see and appreciate everything. I went through in about 90 minutes or so and really feel enlightened by everything I saw and read.

I snapped some pictures, but they don't really do justice, so make sure you go if you can carve out time. It's well worth it.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID2mK-_Ug&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2022-5-5"}]

descriptions: Museum relating city's history back to the Paleolithic period via artifacts, maps & paintings.
owner: {"id":"106333832438272038625","name":"Museo de San Isidro. Los Orígenes de Madrid (Owner)","link":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/106333832438272038625"}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"PL Lau","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX_kjCx3N7Oe5EIg03qgHTSqYbHJ9Dr8h6_hZbfJqZYWeYejkv9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ A Hidden Gem of Madrid History – And It’s Free!

If you’re looking for a high-quality cultural experience in the heart of the city, Museo de San Isidro is a fantastic find. It’s a wonderful way to spend an hour or two, especially if you need a break from the Madrid sun in a cool, air-conditioned space.

What to Expect:
The museum is very well-organized and showcases the history of Madrid from prehistoric times to the semi-modern era. It’s filled with fascinating artifacts, including:

Impressive Fossils: Large-scale displays that bring ancient history to life.

Archaeological Finds: Beautifully preserved pottery, tools, and sculptures.

Interactive Exhibits: Plenty of high-quality, interactive elements that make the history engaging for all ages.

Why It’s a Great Visit:
Bilingual Displays: One of the best parts is that all the signage and information are in both Spanish and English, making it very accessible for international visitors.

Quiet & Peaceful: Unlike the major galleries in Madrid, this museum is not crowded, allowing you to slow down and really absorb the details.

Welcoming Atmosphere: The staff is extremely helpful and welcoming from the moment you walk in.

Bonus – Free Entry: Admission is completely FREE, which is incredible given the quality of the exhibits.

Final Thoughts:
Located in the charming Plaza de San Andrés, this museum is a "must" for history buffs or anyone wanting to understand the origins of Madrid. Whether you are admiring the beautiful courtyard or the ancient mammoth fossils, you won't be disappointed.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD4JZ891c15EjgOGHvfwluC&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2026-1-25"},{"Name":"PG G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL-KZn3KzHQeyJSRbIkb3rLdzUVXN_dKo03beVE4Nscw7tU7g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It's free!
Friendly staff.
Lovely courtyard garden.
Fascinating history of Madrid and lots and lots of relics, fossils, ancient tools and artefacts.
We stumbled across this museum after a disappointing attempt to see the nearby Cathedral (closed indefinitely for renovations). We ended up in there for nearly 2 hours!
Highlights:
Mammoth tusk fossil
Stone age tools and flints
Bronze urns
Roman mosaic floor
3-d model of old Madrid walled city
Statues
And it's free!
Please buy a fridge magnet or souvenir to show support!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD_89Y-hArWf1UTpnU6V3iP&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2026-2-3"},{"Name":"Kevin Yeung","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfr8VfoLnrWAatiNzspZuHOsAYPQ0rQ8QcqYSnL_8Cqky7zGB38w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very informative.   The displayed items are well Organized and described.   The staff and security guards are very friendly as well.  The 2nd floor is used for temporary exhibition which is also worth visiting.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCFJh5jJ6aWd5OfSdJnkwty&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2025-11-4"},{"Name":"Nil Alpaslan Kasimoglu","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKlCG1yNzuz1OIRPW8keszyu1bWND86O_CgjpWHOtrPExVwhA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a museum with free entrance that displays the story of Madrid starting from the stone age till today. Easy to see around, good descriptions and some interactive spots.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDXUwoOz43pGxcId1mE60o8&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2026-1-24"},{"Name":"Julio N","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUMN4zqvkcTuhncYG16PdKq-oimRWaSzkXPSI5EJIoQBj46DmE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It is a very interesting museum about the history of Madrid since pre-historic times.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhACO6SQdp6WxTPzbpO-kpvh&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2026-2-22"},{"Name":"Jane C","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWD2fict4yUr0RLT0psUvEs8irrJsa5SXmgjXPupEeil-Og1xK7wQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We were in Madrid for an overnight visit and while waiting on our friend to arrive, we decided to explore La Latina barrio. Having stopped for a drink on the terrace opposite the museum, we then stopped in as we’re interested in prehistoric history. The museum is great, the people welcoming and the exhibits are very well done. Add in that the signs are in both Spanish and English and the admission is FREE, and you’ve got yourself a wonderful way to spend an hour out of the hot sun in the air conditioning!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAl9ZcABAnCAaTrnm8Yc553&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2025-6-27"},{"Name":"Tracey Johnson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXwskhnIlnEeo-n_0YSv-LxRrghFV-m3Br-gt01qKASRTdlV9o=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A charming and insightful museum that delves into the fascinating origins of Madrid! Museo de San Isidro offers a thoughtful journey through the city’s early history - from prehistoric times to the foundation of the modern capital. The exhibits are well-organized, and the building itself, with its lovely courtyard and peaceful atmosphere, adds to the experience.
It’s not a huge museum, but it’s full of interesting artifacts and stories that bring Madrid’s beginnings to life. Entry is free, which makes it even more worthwhile. Some displays could benefit from more English translations, but overall it’s a great cultural stop for anyone interested in history. A quiet gem tucked away in the heart of old Madrid - definitely worth visiting!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"J C","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXXj0TgkTNAuGwMesWnHLHh3gZP_MQOoaZdzbuIwp4tG_MvQdIP7w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A local walking tour guide recommended this quaint, charming museum for a visit. Entry is free. You have nothing to lose and a whole lot of great information to gain in the archeology and living history of Madrid city - less royalty and politics, and more on how locals lived through the centuries.

After or before this visit, go to the local mercado (public market) nearby for a stroll and a bite & drink. Experience where locals eat and shop daily.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICHtdvDRA&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2024-9-7"}]

Mención especial a María Jesús que nos lo explicó todo, con todo lujo de detalles, y esto hizo que fuera una visita muy amena y que reparáramos en pequeños detalles muy interesantes. Si estás leyendo esto, te recomiendo 100% que lo visites, te va a encantar","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBhZ7mSeETtu1GZti1lW5L8&fid=0x0:0xf8174c2f447f60a7"],"When":"2025-12-25"},{"Name":"Carmen Fernández Masero","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJjsun_aslyWuv9y-3CdocIwbzc1rSxsAO9b_EZqzxPM5JK_Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Es una exposición preciosa, a mi madre y a mi nos gusto muchísimo.
Tenéis un auténtico tesoro.
Mª Jesús nos explicó cada uno de los Belenes con muchísimo agrado y cariño.
Os animamos a ir a verlo PRECIOSO","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAujULVPltukp4v5K_UwcTe&fid=0x0:0xf8174c2f447f60a7"],"When":"2025-12-23"},{"Name":"Susi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ-cm_JTkkSRX8Ds3qPEEWyUFhKmiVLIVqDyiVnKyQcSzJlPg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una colección particular de belenés y pesebres de todo el mundo más que interesante. Una maravilla!

Exposición muy bien guiada por su dueña,  María Jesús, que vive su exposición y te transmite su pasión por este arte del belenismo.
Aprendes a mirar los detalles más curiosos de cada nacimiento, las particularidades de los pesebres de todos los continentes y culturas, sus diferentes estilos. Belenés de España, Portugal, Alemania, Mozambique, Madagascar, Perú, México... Belenés hechos de madera, de barro, de porcelana, de tela, de cañas de bambú, de cristal, de latón, de impresiones de 3D, con cuentas de collar, de cerillas!, de virutas de lápices, miniaturas...
Dan ganas de ponerse también a coleccionar!
Recomiendo muy muy mucho esta exposición tan especial.
Ojalá se le dé la visibilidad que merece a este rincón tan mimado!

Gracias! ❤️","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCZ2uA-n4M-Ba5Ud5aaTd3R&fid=0x0:0xf8174c2f447f60a7"],"When":"2025-12-14"},{"Name":"Guillermo rojas friend","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJGuUaRS_0wlglDZi7ufHa-iBE6gdNMbZl1HVSPK9evuFJSBw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Exposición de Belenes muy completa y espectacular, además de ser guiada con todo detalle por sus dueñas. Difícil quedarse con una pieza y no enamorarse de todas ellas. Una experiencia para repetir y cien por cien recomendable.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB4nFXrPdW3cmEmfh4o5wiW&fid=0x0:0xf8174c2f447f60a7"],"When":"2025-12-13"},{"Name":"Pat Vaz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXFvO4vLmOxuPWMqP2mWxG2toMsXRWQhd6j241LmDEt4-lhAhAHKA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"María Jesús nos transmitió su pasión por los belens y el coleccionismo. Se puede ir durante todo el año . De verdad , una maravilla","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCSdJWt41U0rmEaBZF_4ECF&fid=0x0:0xf8174c2f447f60a7"],"When":"2025-12-21"},{"Name":"Mari G.H","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIRbm5FrK-DErDXsE_pm_0ucJwSTpnX_RPuTICjzrlnTkwQPA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Hermosa Exposición de Belenes del mundo, agradecida por toda la información otorgada de la historia de cada Belen y su procedencia...La super recomiendo...no dejen de visitarla..🤗","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhArW5EeNJYIX0vddWas5pSQ&fid=0x0:0xf8174c2f447f60a7"],"When":"2026-1-19"},{"Name":"Maria P.C","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIlWD8pfNdJkrsstY08YhaRQL5gmixgaVejcy6gFu6YEzrCJw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Magnífica exposición de belenes, que su dueña, María Jesús, ha ido reuniendo durante años.

María Jesús es encantadora, y te explica todo muy bien, la verdad es que entiende mucho y gracias a ella vivimos una experiencia increíble.
Muchas gracias por recibirnos con esa amabilidad, que había sentirte como en casa, y enseñarnos ese tesoro de belenes.
La visita es gratuita solo tenéis que poneros en contacto con ellos para poder ir a verla.","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Melissa Abache","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUw0vLeJHVoNMLUgkFmakRU-w4pqdCkX4s1cy5_kSpRWinYVZq2CQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a great activity for families with children of all ages including toddlers. You should book in advance (it is free but they have limited capacity for the guided tour) and it is worth doing the guided tour to learn about very useful fire and safety information. The museum has several antique firetrucks, tools, displays and the guided tour finishes with a short animated movie for kids. It is only available in Spanish but still worth the visit if your toddler is obsessed with firefighters. To get there you should get down at the “Buenos Aires” metro stop on line 10 and then walk for about 7-8 min to the Museum. You will see a tall building with red letters that says MBM and that is it. Very friendly firefighters guide the tour and answer all questions. There is nowhere to have a drink or snack so come prepared but it has restrooms.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICBmcLiHg&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2022-12-29"},{"Name":"Darusik1","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLxCoDECInj0I-kF5XYPvejjQUPtQlQtc_Tl3-esghw6xmt4lQr=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Trying to get there two times and all the time huge dissapointment for the child as its always closed despite the announced hours. Please note that not only you can visit during week days (why by the way? Arent all the kids at school at this time?!🙄)  but even if you finally got a free day with your kiddo you will also need advance booking to get inside....otherwise you will be left behind the door dealing with severe child tantrum. Which is very sad as it looks like a nice musuem on the photoes, ans with the kid who is obsessed with fire trucks and all related things its sad that it is so complicated to get in:(","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"LUCIA DIEGO CARRIL","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXgEgWEPqN0vf01ue8zfJwglf_S0KhKvB-aKrQiWM0cLpxxn8_SLg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Es un museo que merece la pena visitar donde te cuentan la historia de los bomberos, los tipos de coches que han tenido que usar a lo largo de los tiempos y los tipos de maquinarias, además de que te enseñan algún vídeo explicativo. Es una visita de 1h que es gratuita. Pero recomiendo mucho ir, además es un sitio donde suelen ir con niños también. Es muy educativo.
Y al final de la visita te dejan probarte el casco con una chaqueta para poder hacerte una foto con ello puesto.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDu_hWBFi5BIsK5nmQvts8v&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2025-10-2"},{"Name":"Sergi Labernia Pérez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW16RKso-FkghmtROU9m0lvnut95mFjKWdfLrFS0qOHCW5ZO4CJ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Cal reservar. Molt bones explicacions encara que una mica llargues pels mes petits. Pels d'aproximadament 3 anys ens comenten que millor anar a una estació de bombers el dia de portes obertes. Al final de la visita els han deixat posar-se un casc i una jaqueta de bombers. En general el museu té peces molt interresants que expliquen la història dels bombers a Madrid i que són de molt valor. Moltes gràcies per la vostra feina i per la visita guiada.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCcYolnwavH7rjWM9F5W93I&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2026-1-12"},{"Name":"Carolina Da Costa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWYfSgX0Lk0SuEctOO4gjXXttfiuDZHRunajNXXeWOSU0NTrhAj=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una visita muy recomendada, en especial  si vives en Madrid, un lugar lleno de historia, nadie se imaginaría los tesoros que tienen allí, me encantó. Debes reservar con antelación.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDKBFWY4eRUoN00FErjVd3x&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2026-1-28"},{"Name":"Swift Rivera","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVi8Aupr9fkBY6kyosChZP4sFrnrEZNiLqXgIjgzQQbQ9NIWIk4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Si te gustan los bomberos y la historia, este museo es una joya oculta en Madrid. Tienen camiones antiguos, herramientas y equipos históricos que muestran cómo ha evolucionado el servicio a lo largo de los años. Las visitas son guiadas y te explican todo con mucho detalle. Es una experiencia entretenida y educativa, ideal para niños mayores de 8 años y adultos. Eso sí mejor reservar con antelación.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCQtKrLnwE&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2025-3-2"},{"Name":"El Glotón Gourmet","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWxUpfy9hbJ5n9HzXScwRGQinpNKocHoQbrOO8FjvHrl8qXiYCN=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo muy interesante sobre la historia del servicio de bomberos en Madrid. La visita guiada, realizada por bomberos, muy formativa","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBZe9TgpuDK24pRBeHOknqC&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2025-11-16"},{"Name":"Oscar Casas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKt9MiDg_GPchHd8LuExZFUExscCzSWRjIyK1pY-98a4pf2Yw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Visitamos el museo un fin de semana acompañados de nuestros hijos y fue una experiencia fantástica para todos. El museo está muy bien organizado y resulta especialmente interesante para los más pequeños, que pueden ver de cerca antiguos camiones, uniformes y materiales reales utilizados por los bomberos.

La visita es amena y educativa, ya que permite entender mejor la labor tan importante que realizan y la evolución del servicio a lo largo del tiempo. El personal fue muy amable y cercano con los niños, resolviendo dudas y haciendo la visita aún más entretenida. Un plan muy recomendable en Madrid para disfrutar en familia y aprender al mismo tiempo.","Images":null,"When":""}]

Along with pictures of his most famous paintings. It's a short tour and the host is wonderful!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCoFqxGxLjfoKDn60NDsRyv&fid=0x0:0xbfa93202fc6d3d55"],"When":"2026-3-9"},{"Name":"Leslie Sepulveda","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJQtR5gLIfHvnuvtMZCnzpou_RZjVN1ArRA_AVttnZtAjdv1w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rossana Rioperez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKHpteVLxVl7EhTRq6OqI1EdiHGsCRR2PdBv-4zbcSSRr603A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una visita súper interesante a este nuevo museo en Madrid. Me ha gustado cómo está diseñado, la distribución de las salas por temas y las explicaciones de los paneles informativos. No es muy grande pero es muy completo. Ideal para pasar con niños un rato didáctico.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Manuel Gutierrez Hernandez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXBhNgNShCgkXUUDaMsWqDfvucTT-e5pjyDFfDEBzL13Aw_47pu=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Muy interesante, el staff muy amable, pequeño pero merece la pena","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhATsOKMdqiOvYznF-9XpxuY&fid=0x0:0xbfa93202fc6d3d55"],"When":"2026-3-7"},{"Name":"Lucía Fernández","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWo-7rJUvFax_U_UfEomhs-QAw3KDdofD-gwXZ6CPawE0ORnTuGVw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El museo de Leonardo Da Vinci está muy bien, sobre todo cuando te lo explican. Las dos personas que nos hicieron la visita lo hicieron genial y así se disfruta mucho más.

Hay maquetas de máquinas e inventos en sus cuadernos de madera hechas a partir de los dibujos de Leonardo y también reproducciones de la Mona Lisa y La Última Cena.

Echo de menos que haya más pantallas o elementos interactivos para entender mejor cómo funcionan las máquinas. Aun así, la visita merece la pena.

Por otro lado pensábamos con tanto vinilo que estaban en obras, porque parecía que estaba cerrado y al abrir la puerta no vimos la taquilla.
Hay descuentos para niños y mayores.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC6JgZvhlyijm5ZQpihWU4U&fid=0x0:0xbfa93202fc6d3d55"],"When":"2026-3-6"}]

title: Museo Ciencias Naturales
address: Chamartín, 28046 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Maxim BK","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXhjS8BtCwS0iOd3gnZ1TwfD3D2_kseC3i6FY8AXKrHZx2JMIZFUQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Visité el Museo de Ciencias Naturales un fin de semana sin demasiadas expectativas y, en general, la experiencia fue positiva. Es un museo cuidado, con exposiciones claras y bien explicadas, especialmente pensadas para un público familiar. Se nota bastante ese enfoque: muchos paneles didácticos, maquetas grandes y zonas pensadas para niños, lo cual está bien si vas con críos, aunque si buscas algo más profundo o especializado puede quedarse un poco corto. Aun así, hay salas interesantes, como la de biodiversidad y fósiles, donde me entretuve más de lo que esperaba. El edificio es amplio y se recorre con calma, sin sensación de agobio. En mi caso fui entre semana y estaba tranquilo, lo que se agradece. No es un museo que te vuele la cabeza, pero sí un plan agradable para pasar un par de horas aprendiendo algo y desconectar del ruido de la ciudad.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"A O","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXMiIq1Xyp9MqeBs443BWLry63RuqPpQMWYDVUjWQj4PBmqtBND=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Hemos ido al Museo de Ciencias Naturales a ver la exposición de Juan Ramón Martín titulada Kranion, este escultor hace un estudio de los cráneos de diversas especies animales en unas bellas composiciones de hierro. Su obra es impresionante , refleja el alma de las especies con gran sencillez de formas. Os recomiendo que visitéis la exposición que estará hasta finales de Julio.
Contigua hay otra exposición “Dibujando entre leones”, con unos bonitos dibujos de estos felinos .
Aprovechamos para visitar el museo , con piezas de principios del Siglo XX algunas de las cuales podían haberse renovado ya que han perdido el color, si bien es cierto que se pueden contemplar gran variedad de especies de nuestro país","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID47KH4xgE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2019-5-12"},{"Name":"Fernando R.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW3g4SyvCMQRxYY4RhtzYhZwQ0c-BUdBDreKXSNolhkr2uR6xDt=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Muy recomendable. No es quizá demasiado grande pero tiene una exposición muy representativa y es muy didáctico.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKELWirfenibLRhgE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2025-6-8"},{"Name":"Angel Lillo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUdLxuJxr1HCkfLs-WOngjoMyZ2MA5-cnUl3XIBXwIrvtzojynBCg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Museo pequeño y bien conservado. Es bastante molesto coincidir con varios grupos de colegios sobre todo cuando son adolescentes en plena edad del pavo. Hay una pequeña zona azul para aparcar a cada lado pero mejor ir en transporte público","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC4usfb_QE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2018-3-20"},{"Name":"Teresa PGT","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJAdkENhJmyxCNtvBYf5PrBPf3Ywp5vXFRzf_2Qwh5tq6lBZg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"El pintacaras de dinosaurios estuvo bien para acercar el museo a los niños. Sitio interesante","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Dolly Bals","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUaWDFzicOIsNv3JZxewTqNtBAFIqU-FC1QxQE_w8CfbL9AtIM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Fuimos con mucha ilusión al Museo de Ciencias Naturales de Madrid, pero la experiencia fue decepcionante. El museo cierra a las 15:00 y no es imaginable para un turista con niños este horario que no figura en Google, no se puede aprovechar bien la visita si llegas cerca del cierre. Solo pudimos estar 40 minutos.

Además, el museo tiene dos entradas separadas por unos 100 metros, y solo en la más pequeña se pueden sacar entradas, algo confuso si llegas sin saberlo. El personal solo parecía preocupado por que saliéramos cuanto antes. No hubo ni un mínimo de amabilidad o interés en facilitar la visita. Daba la sensación de que estaban deseando terminar su jornada y poco les importaba la experiencia de los visitantes, que hemos pagado una entrada completa como si fuéramos a disfrutar del horario y museo completo.

Una pena, porque el contenido del museo es muy interesante, pero la gestión y el trato al visitante dejan mucho que desear. Deberían revisar sus horarios, su política de entradas y sobre todo, la actitud del personal de cara al público.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"juanjo ovied","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIIyqNL2004eo6S_Bi4f1cyNyPutXmkp11yuhI0xcWRUSAjdw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Parada cercana al Museo de Ciencias Naturales, ETS de ingenieros industriales,que comparten manzana con El Laboratorio de electrotecnia, la Residencia de Estudiantes, el Ramiro de Maeztu, y Educación de la CAM,  también està Nuevos Ministerios, iglesia de San Juan de la Cruz.¡Ahh! Se me olvidaba, el monumento del pueblo de Madrid a la Constitución, habitualmente con estudiantes sentados.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID40uXIDg&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2017-7-9"},{"Name":"Marta FG","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX-00NmE3AnJC7z8tsktVkxGNg6TXDeLdY7eHvO0NFyjRbpxfxI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Hemos hecho la visita con peques y ha estado genial, les llamaba la atención todo y les ha gustado mucho. Es un museo relativamente pequeño dividido en dos edificios, no se hace pesado de visitar. Muy visual e ilustrativo. Buena actividad para hacer con niños!!!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICcipjs6gE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2019-12-1"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Avri Bilovich","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIfpVC1kAslEByD35Lu8fva-ErGe_JVYqyBGCM49loazJ1bcg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fantastic space, offbeat museum which takes some chances on the art it shows. We thoroughly enjoyed our visit and will definitely come back. They also have a cute garden space to have a drink.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBQ7wEVvca6bmbmeqvjZt4F&fid=0x0:0xa9e4000c30a4d6a0"],"When":"2025-9-4"},{"Name":"Stephen Cole","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKL4xnc3v8Hr6yeT3gOYC08TDdjlLK1gwPJdx2r-pMatdJb_g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"What a wonderful art space.
I have visited many museums in Spain over the past 4 months. Some were special, like this one, some awful, especially those that put modern visual art inside the classical room's with their 4 white walls.
Neomudejar is an edgy old factory location ( I am pretty old so I am happy to admit I worked in factories like this) and well, the aging space compliments and brings out the art so much better.
The staff were real, chilled and smiling. A first given the amount of bored, sitting in the corner, flicking their phone attendant's, I have seen in most Spanish art museums.
I am still so delighted to visit honest spaces like La Neomudejar,
but realistic enough to understand that the sad "Instagram" generation will not get their self need hits in such a genuine art space.
By thanks and appreciation to the real artists who still appreciate originality and edge, over mundane copious copying","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCAiuOtJQ&fid=0x0:0xa9e4000c30a4d6a0"],"When":"2025-1-30"},{"Name":"RAVE","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXZkEtNCn047-jM4dPo37rcR69TYzKQQ9RPQ0ETwVOXUhBSVz89Sw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It was excellent, a small museum, with very interesting curating all inside an old factory. They were few exhibition two of them, where out of the ordinary, good statues and exhibition made of pieces of metal in the shape of unique features.

The place itself is very interesting as well.
It is close to the garden and the museum of the carpets .","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDRypTe4AE&fid=0x0:0xa9e4000c30a4d6a0"],"When":"2023-4-22"},{"Name":"Paul King","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWW_jSnIvNucLxIdG6ASaWkwXMdlu5pWKZK9_ymWNbE-C2wrsFe=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Total gem! user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Héctor Sedano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWsOwbcIxSfN5R3erwUEH7988TA-B6DPIEz1n8y5neLGxaWKAQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El nuevo museo está genial. Antes solías estar en el sótano, y daba un aspecto algo abandonado
Ahora mismo se encuentro en una sala muy grande, llena de paneles explicativos y vídeos.
Esta justo antes de entrar a la cafetería
La visita guiada está genial, la guía lo explico todo con mucho detalle y de una forma muy amena.
Torres Quevedo es uno de los grandes inventores del siglo XX, pero que tristemente hoy, es muy poco conocido
En el museo podemos encontrar varios de sus inventos más famosos y hacer un breve recorrido histórico por su vida","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID285m3gQE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2022-5-24"},{"Name":"Alex Soriano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVQDetZwOwRcKNg5bR6pvFZNPkpRe4S5ro24rF87uYDZ9oD4e-9=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo increíble con piezas únicas. Visita muy buena por parte del profesor Romana. Muchas gracias a todos los partícipes por preservar el patrimonio y legado de este genial ingeniero español.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sagrario Sanchez Francisco","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIEswQ6YBfqYqVyaIIUs_egJhgFmpnTQmJTSbH7j-sYcmDwmg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un museo y un inventor Cántabro muy desconocido,es el Da Vicci italiano, pero sin fama. Muy interesante y la persona encargada de hacer la visita guiada nos lo hizo muy ameno. Se tarda unos 70/ 80 minutos con guía, si no,en 30 está visto,es pequeño.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"El Mago 2.0","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUm105mBqAlVdvN-fu1L2cpjUw1hE0LiT2Sl_AUt21zwtSE-JUT=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Pregunta por la cafetería de la facultad y verás el museo justo al lado. Suele estar cerrado por lo que para visitarlo hay que concertar una cita mediante email. Totalmente recomendable y sorprendente. Pequeño pero inolvidable museo donde quedarás impresionado por la inteligencia, trabajo y el desarrollo de la carrera profesional del genial inventor Torres Quevedo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC-3Zbl2gE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2022-11-11"},{"Name":"Castellar zamorano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVZioBhy_uHgfsIlgCCLt87t8-2HMseMFk7FbRebj8FSXHtB2Ms=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Es un pequeño museo en la Universidad de Caminos en Ciudad Universitaria de Madrid.
Inventor destacado por inventar el Transbordador en las Cataratas del Niágara , actualmente en uso desde 1916.
También los dirigibles, utilizados en la 1a Guerra Mundial, máquina Ajedrecista autómata y máquinas de cálculo y el Telequino, el primer mando a distancia a gran escala, entre otros","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDGtOrv9wE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2021-11-19"},{"Name":"Javier Pulido Porcel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXlOGHzkxgej9EEi-M_em4uUxnnYYs_jhuv-mBxuduf4FG7QXY=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Buen lugar que visitar si tienes un rato libre, conocer la historia de Leonardo Torres Quevedo un ingeniero con inventos inimaginables, para la época, muy chula la maqueta del teleférico, que es el mismo que  cruza las cataratas del Niágara. Y otros inventos como máquinas de cálculo, dirigibles, mandos a distancia,etc. El guía, que es ingeniero de caminos nos dio una muy buena explicación de cada uno de los inventos presentes y nos hizo el recorrido a través de la historia de Torres Quevedo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICv3u2_xAE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2024-12-11"},{"Name":"Cristina González","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW4zXb0A_Qr-gplRtewimQsjXPYFCg09HcFz3uvUxizKbuMW42l=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Imprescindible... Se encuentra dentro de la ETSI Caminos, Canales y Puertos en la Ciudad Universitaria.

Es un museo que alberga la colección de máquinas e ingenios diseñados y producidos por el ingeniero Leonardo Torres Quevedo. Se conservan maquetas, aparatos, documentos y fotografías de los inventos.

El gran desconocido para la población y es un español que hizo historia y qué mejor que visitar este museo para poder conocerlo y darlo a conocer. El museo ocupa una sala.

Entrada gratuita y es imprescindible concertar cita previa por correo electrónico. La visita guiada es en días laborales (lunes a viernes) a las 12:00h y de 75-80 minutos de duración. Explicado maravillosamente.

Se puede ir con niños pero recomiendo que a partir de unos 14 años. Para todas las edades en adultos. Grupos de 12 personas para realizar la visita. Al estar dentro de la facultad hay baños y cafetería de los que se puede hacer uso.

Como llegar al Museo Torres Quevedo:
Metro: Ciudad Universitaria (l-6) / EMT: F, G, U, 82, 132

Un poco estrecha la sala pero creo que sí se puede acceder con silla de ruedas sencilla. Es mejor comentarlo al solicitar la cita.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDT7LrBCw&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2024-5-23"},{"Name":"Manuel GD","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXCVXdYApfqmq_XQEcWweualu0tj4DbMma8TLTkZgZveuZWirts=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Acudimos varios alumnos de la Academia de Ajedrez Chamberí y socios del Club de Ajedrez La Didáctica. ¡Una visita estupenda y totalmente recomendable para cualquier aficionado al Ajedrez!
Nos han explicado estupendamente el Museo entero, y muy en particular los 2 autómatas ajedrecistas, primeros de la historia y nos han abierto sus tripas para ver detenidamente el modelo final.
La verdad es que nos dan ganas de repetir.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC2xbXwZw&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2022-4-25"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Alessio Garau","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWXwgwVSUnu7LxBPdgWtjTLnLD__Hy6lgiLJJjnhwE2uZgH1fy7=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently had the pleasure of visiting this fantastic spot just a short journey outside Madrid. Easily accessible from the city center for just a few euros, it's like stepping into a serene oasis. The attention to detail in maintaining the space is remarkable. Everything from the layout to the cleanliness is meticulously cared for, creating a truly tranquil atmosphere.

These aren't just ordinary bonsai; they're exceptional, unique pieces, some even centuries old! It's a rare opportunity to see such an array of timeless beauty.

I recommend visiting early in the morning. Not only is it quieter with fewer visitors, but the light at this time is absolutely magical, casting a serene glow over the gardens. It's the perfect time to fully appreciate the tranquility of the place.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICNkYmHvgE&fid=0x0:0x1a5b05a65963fe4b"],"When":"2024-1-15"},{"Name":"Patrick B.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUoL1DEfK7Bu7_hoNAjXKq5lk_LUu5npisoflWy_A8-MHsmHMY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This is definitely the best bonsai museum that I've seen so far. It's not huge but if you really appreciate bonsais then you'll spend 30-45min just appreciating the beauty of the bonsais. The entrance costs 3€ which is not bad in my opinion. It's also a great place to relax and I'm sure that if you take a book with you or you like to draw natural scenes then it's a very zen place to do so. It's obviously not in a centric area, meaning that is not close to the center or touristic area of Madrid, but if you have the means to go, then it's a good stop. But don't go out of your way just to go there, you can see bonsais at the botanical garden right next to the Prado museum. All in all a great stop to relax and enjoy an afternoon stroll.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICEqeW4jwE&fid=0x0:0x1a5b05a65963fe4b"],"When":"2019-5-21"},{"Name":"Iacopo Breschi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU1MxgP3bvgW9bEUwxoliISDL87kM367xoxfdO2JCa1rsy-yVtU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One amazing place.
For me the highlight of the museum, except from the tree themselves of course, is the arrangement. Each tree is displayed on top of stones or aged wood, against neutral yet rich of fines details backgrounds. White gravel of different sizes and reddish soil on the ground.
The contrast with the green of the plants is superbe.
The staff is very kind and helpful. I will come back for a guided tour.
The shop is small yet super cute with the mini plant compositions.
Thanks to all the people that work there to keep this place in such well maintained conditions.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICOwrfRtwE&fid=0x0:0x1a5b05a65963fe4b"],"When":"2022-6-5"},{"Name":"joshua gorr","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIwaLNGKXvnGdwuOR_qELDMVEuhYaSjIJpzg5WC8aQas1BErw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing collection of beautiful trees. Definitely a great place to check out.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Emaelia Norman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL0jJnJyhaFhUZPzuRonQXKLpLV2kllpoQYAAvj49XtYzU4eUM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It's a nice little quiet place to visit if you're looking for something off the beaten path.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID429qaag&fid=0x0:0x1a5b05a65963fe4b"],"When":"2018-11-9"},{"Name":"Will B","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJlIc2yUz7rxrArXYCudvgc_BgeWKv0h0rIgRObj5bz4XX2qA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Loved, the selection of trees was very impressive","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICFncPxJg&fid=0x0:0x1a5b05a65963fe4b"],"When":"2023-10-31"},{"Name":"Almudena Navarro Sánchez-Mayoral","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUPQYaZCyXNuqF6ENdb6CX5B-lTGWo62v0TIj4mCFUV5_irX3s_=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An incredibly inspiring collection in the most beautifully designed garden. We had the luck of meeting Luis and briefly speaking to him, which made the visit extra memorable.

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"PL Lau","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX_kjCx3N7Oe5EIg03qgHTSqYbHJ9Dr8h6_hZbfJqZYWeYejkv9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A Nostalgic Trip Through Gaming History
⭐⭐⭐⭐

If you have any interest in gaming, OXO Museum is an absolute must-visit in Madrid. It turned into an amazing walk down memory lane for us!

What we loved:

Hands-on Experience: The biggest plus is that you can actually play almost everything. We saw everything from old Commodore computers and classic Sega machines to Pac-Man and a massive range of retro and modern systems.

Childhood Memories: We spent a fantastic couple of hours playing on my childhood console. It was the perfect way to relive old memories while making new ones.

Educational Context: It’s not just an arcade; there are great historical explanations, descriptions, and exhibits that really give you a sense of the evolution of the industry.

A few things to note:
Price: At €21, it is a tad pricey compared to other museums in the city.

Time Limit: Your visit is limited to two hours, which felt somewhat restrictive. Given how much there is to see and—more importantly—play, you have to keep an eye on the clock!

Overall: Despite the price and time limit, it’s a fantastic, well-curated space. Perfect for families or anyone looking to reconnect with their favorite classic games.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBkStWpxMgrPoRNvWDgtfnw&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-2-8"},{"Name":"Diego Schwarz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIqpGpnlnPKQWw4-vgrZrUxVnsOcgERKIZ7Z9T8_P5T6tSZqA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Honestly, we weren’t expecting much — and wow, were we wrong! 😄 It turned into an amazing walk down memory lane. We had a fantastic couple of hours playing on my childhood console, reliving old memories and making new ones at the same time. Such a fun, special experience for both my kid and me. Highly recommended!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAedZjQDHFVCjbXap6SINXI&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-1-4"},{"Name":"Joe Szabo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqlMOLx09MrD42Yu00kBfRybp1LlyD_EY8OUGGMpNkMAlhnys4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"OXO Museo del Videojuego Madrid is a fantastic place for anyone interested in the history of video games. Spread across three floors, the museum showcases every generation of gaming from the 1960s and 70s all the way to the latest PlayStation consoles.

You can try almost everything — from old Commodore computers and classic Sega Machines and Pac-Man games to a wide range of retro and modern systems. There are historical explanations, descriptions, exhibits, and plenty of hands-on gameplay, which is a big plus.

The adult ticket is €21, which I find a bit expensive, and the visit is limited to two hours, which feels somewhat restrictive considering how much there is to see and play.

Overall, I’d give it 4 stars — but for anyone who loves video games, it’s definitely worth a visit.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDMjCWyiWGSXjNSHlW9acs-&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Siv Carina Molvik","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWfHfJ-c2ljwqULxk8LJK81CWRBkqoj4OUL8Uduvdie9XnbnCI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Whether you are a younger gamer who loves dropping Tilted Towers and playing LEGO versions of games or an older gamer who remembers PONG fondly like it was yesterday, this place is going to amaze you! Spend a couple of hours walking down memory lane of games and play ALL of them! Interactive museum indeed! Highly recommend!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBCro9zn2JwMcS7TJhlB_dG&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-1-26"},{"Name":"Kam Loqueman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUxC4clvyzC3x-fNmSBYU0Vrhvk05LFQnTDSuN-p7D6-yD4FCzj-Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One could argue a tad pricey at 21 Euros but for me well worth it. Brought back so much nostalgia and the fact you could touch and play with 40+ year old tech was brilliant. The arcade was great fun playing games of my youth..for free! The Lego exhibition was a bonus.

I visited on a Saturday and it wasn't too busy at all.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBSR2u9NhpzjJrowg7093ry&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-11-16"},{"Name":"Vivian Martinez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVxe-YsjP5k6BiGnY8-7zA7gI6OHs8j_MTCaODEYXkYCZxE__0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"If you’re a video game fan, this one’s for you! Have never seen anything like it. 7@ years worth of video game history, you get to play in all (and I mean ALL) the consoles you can think of from 70 years back. Truly a blast from the past! Make sure to make a reservation.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAsPWVPbdWKhFNzLTKmrwIA&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-10-7"},{"Name":"Lawana “Mitchi” Walker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKyssC3nkPp_pZqwmcbuNrVmcnOaoycZTYhTvAsDH-Ec2q8UARRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Went on a weekday afternoon and there were only a couple other families there.

It is a little expensive BUT they do have a reduced rate for families, & an annual pass that I personally think is worth it. I've seen some reviews that said some of the games didn't work, but I only saw maybe 3 or 4 games/systems that weren't working. The history of videogames part is super cool, & they have a huge selection of gaming systems on display & available to play. The arcade is also super cool, the Japanese games were the most fun for my son. The PlayStation floor was pretty cool, too. The entire place is themed very well, it's clean, in good condition & a nice place to spend a couple of hours as a family.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBmtrAubrN2mFcA5v0O8vhQ&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-7-3"},{"Name":"Diogo Morais","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLLo3-eNubWf7buJzmV4e2jyPV9FE1UJME0WrCQxukl-eqvWQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Very good museum with a lot of games and arcades to try out!

Needs more control on that part a lot of machines had the same kids there for more than 10 minutes....","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAshlO6DoTDkUTtdUNcLoLA&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-9-13"}]

La selección de temas es variada, abarcando diferentes periodos y artistas, lo que permite tener una visión general de distintas corrientes.

La organización de las salas es funcional y el recorrido está estructurado de una manera lógica. Sin embargo, en ocasiones, la cantidad de visitantes puede dificultar la observación detallada de las obras, especialmente en las muestras más populares, por lo que se recomienda comprar la entrada con antelación para asegurar el acceso en la hora deseada.

La información que acompaña a las obras es adecuada, aunque a veces podría ser más extensa para proporcionar un contexto más profundo. En general, es una opción a considerar para complementar la visita a la colección permanente.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marga Corchero","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVlWyxEwOZiFHq03iR3sWkk2iGO95iBcTWB8VMHtbu7Y-PoexzTYg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Maravillosa exposición de Gabrielle Münter. Very magical. Used to be the cloister of the cathedral and was used in former times for wedding cocktails and such events, now it is still used to host events but within the context of the Prado Miseum","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Carmen mendez ayala","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL-78cNA2vUKujKoiZ2z7MVlQTCbrf4eZalnfSS868UlhzbVA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Esoectacular","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Gogara Arkitektura","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWY6jDT2RpjbdhXi4TeWiLeDVxwVKV-mYRRzgTqNYRnmupeTKwk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El claustro del Museo Nacional Centro de Arte Reina Sofía se percibe como un espacio especialmente agradable dentro del recorrido del museo, pensado casi como una pausa necesaria cuando el cansancio empieza a notarse tras varias salas.

Arquitectónicamente, la solución es muy sugerente. El claustro original aparece suspendido, “volando” sobre una estructura de hormigón visto, que se muestra sin intentar mimetizarse. La estructura no pesa visualmente, sino que enmarca y realza el valor del claustro original.

El patio central actúa como verdadero corazón del espacio. La proporción, la luz y la ausencia de ruido lo convierten en un lugar tranquilo, donde el tiempo parece desacelerarse.

La experiencia de calma la percibimos ya que al ser lunes, el claustro estaba prácticamente vacío, lo que acentuó esa sensación de refugio dentro del museo.

Lejos de ser un espacio residual, funciona como un lugar de descanso real, tanto físico como mental, que equilibra muy bien la intensidad del recorrido expositivo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDik19UFagP_sYEXD5emUej&fid=0x0:0xfd3c86f95e23fa1a"],"When":"2026-2-20"},{"Name":"Francisco Lucena","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXXAnbPEgO38IAgd5mIrYHf4QXKfnMypv1751f6z9U8cL9E_HQHDQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"La ampliación del claustro de los Jerónimos es una espectacular obra de Rafael moneo en 2007 incluyen la recuperación y rehabilitación del claustro de los Jerónimos que perteneció al antiguo monasterio de Jerónimo el real, utilizando gran parte de su estructura original.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAaCF038YEMtiB28iO2eG6_&fid=0x0:0xfd3c86f95e23fa1a"],"When":"2025-9-14"},{"Name":"Fernando Anayet","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUioCJNuamwUKIFiD6_N5CG67YAYQLWgVY6ygBcezFzyMDDJnk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Es parte de la última ampliación del Prado en manos de Moneo.

Es interesante como edificio y alberga las colecciones temporales del Prado, dándole más espacio.

La puerta principal simula la corteza de un árbol.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCYxdCkTg&fid=0x0:0xfd3c86f95e23fa1a"],"When":"2025-5-5"},{"Name":"Etsel Roberto Eschoyez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKCliESF1ORBDFthhaKlTcq5JX4y_bcC2V-L4G98rx9WNp4Iw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Me pareció cara la entrada por lo que se puede ver en su interior. title: Museo Sorolla
address: Chamberí, 28010 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Jose Garcia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVZ3giLPz2ziBWCFCbpRI3YaT88dz_l2MtIJzKAAnNPitSP6oCwaA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This beautiful place is run with an absolute ignorance to the customer needing special assistance.
When we asked if wheelchair accessible they excused this necessity by "It is stated in our website we don't "
The elderly and immobile have to walk long distances climb steps and are barred from the second floor.
If you are a costumer needing special assistance this museum discriminates against you.
On top of that the staff is rude to begin with.
Be careful  Museo Sorolla the world is reading this in this part of the web.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Nuan Masa Eros","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLqeUSl3WORL5a3CMEboXX3uWCC__p1bBDzJqnXL5DMr8s_kg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El Museo Sorolla es un tesoro escondido en el barrio de Chamberí que conserva la esencia del gran pintor valenciano Joaquín Sorolla. Este museo-casa permite conocer tanto la vida como la obra del artista en un entorno íntimo y auténtico. El edificio mantiene la distribución original con el taller del pintor, sus salones y un precioso jardín andaluz diseñado por el propio Sorolla, lleno de fuentes, azulejos y vegetación mediterránea. La colección permanente incluye numerosos óleos que muestran su dominio magistral de la luz y el color, especialmente en sus famosas escenas de playa. El museo no está masificado, lo que permite disfrutar de las obras con tranquilidad. La entrada tiene un precio muy accesible. Es una visita imprescindible para los amantes del impresionismo español y el arte decimonónico.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Francisco Javier Toribio Dominguez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIQOaCV23hOJZyXCwvLI2zjGRUGwGI6p2RNfRO2lHV6TycI8A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Pequeño, pero gran museo con gran multitud de obras del genial artista, y en estos momentos y por poco tiempo mas con una colección de trajes y complementos repartidos entre este museo y el thyssen muy interesantes y con un patio en pleno centro de Madrid que nos lleva a tiempos pasados.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE7Y-bEg&fid=0x0:0x493b2c9b15e9fd5e"],"When":"2018-5-21"},{"Name":"Ulricus Angelus","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW-TXBol6ahJS4nIPaNJPPjeTbb1l9alGTE43IXPPHSeqc8qPvS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wunderschönes Museum in eines kleinen schattigen Park umgeben von vielen höheren Häusern. Wohnhaus, Arbeitsplatz und Galerie des großen Malers. Unbedingt empfehlenswert.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC4j7nHcQ&fid=0x0:0x493b2c9b15e9fd5e"],"When":"2017-6-4"},{"Name":"Gustavo Rodríguez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIYJpJyxyNyb2zy1jw4SUD82YE9x52J5hNWgrbkpnZ58B2R9tQB=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un museo muy agradable, un pequeño paraíso para tomarse un respiro. address: Paseo de las Delicias, 61, Arganzuela, 28045 Madrid
open_hours: {"Friday":["9:30 am–3 pm"],"Monday":["9:30 am–3 pm"],"Saturday":["10 am–7 pm"],"Sunday":["10 am–3 pm"],"Thursday":["9:30 am–3 pm"],"Tuesday":["9:30 am–3 pm"],"Wednesday":["9:30 am–3 pm"]}
descriptions: Museum of railway history in grand former station, with carriages & locomotives, library & archives.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Lucas Kovács","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUAilLNzd-8YrLSM-6EX9NLiSIkwgxKq6129nEafcsyV9U-HPsV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The visit was overall nice and interesting, but I left wanting a bit more.

When I went, there were not too many people — just a couple of groups at first, and once they left, it felt quite calm with only a few visitors around. The ticket price is accessible (€7), and there’s an audio guide available (needs mobile data/wifi), which helps give context to the collection. The total visit took me about one hour, though if you’re really into trains, you could easily spend two hours or more.

Inside, there are toilets, a small shop, and vending machines for coffee, which makes it convenient. The collection includes many RENFE trains that operated in Spain, mostly steam and electric locomotives, and they are very well maintained and preserved. It’s definitely impressive to see them up close.

That said, there were a couple of things I didn’t love. The miniature train section on the second floor felt a bit rushed — it lacks landscape and detailed scenery; it’s mostly just trains running. Also, this area is not accessible, which is worth noting.

More importantly, I felt the museum could be more interactive. In a museum, you expect some level of interaction, but here the opportunities are quite limited. You can’t enter the locomotives or carriages, and in some cases you can’t even properly look inside. That’s a bit of a shame. This isn’t a train storage facility — it’s a museum — and opening up more spaces would really help spark visitors’ interest.

I’m not saying it wasn’t impressive — it absolutely is — but I was left wanting more. Under the current conditions, I probably wouldn’t visit again. If they allowed access inside some of the locomotives and carriages, I would definitely reconsider and recommend it even more strongly.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDSNuPK7DTyAp6qtZjL7L39&fid=0x0:0xb46b68a535be221e"],"When":"2026-2-8"},{"Name":"JR Suarez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV125tV-n_qcVxm3oiGFaOYghpi_ecYeHDOTmo-qTf2pPzlJmkr=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I went during the Motores Market that they have generally the second weekend of each month: Mercado de motores. I had a great time. Don’t miss the futuristic Talgo II and the Isabel II, Alfonso XII and Alfonso XIII carriages showing what elite travel was like in the late 19th and early 20th. The museum is housed in the former 1880s Madrid-Delicias station, and it has been feature in many films.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhArU1jqVlZa-GqKAwJ_1EkT&fid=0x0:0xb46b68a535be221e"],"When":"2025-12-20"},{"Name":"Rishi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLEoe_r1zog52g3UxzujFKvAvLMCO8ZKCsIrV7bDodgkrH3M00=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very well built and maintained museum showing the history of this railway station and the different trains, couches used since the 1900s. Very fascinating to see the rich history of trains and railways.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBGpe_7CxmCFxLLjJFbeMkW&fid=0x0:0xb46b68a535be221e"],"When":"2025-8-30"},{"Name":"luis quinones","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJIoYUyFzrpRfuKjOFU7fwchh2BL4dTCbZAi9e_PazgPB5OQg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very good if you’re into trains. More than 20 exhibits. But the small model exhibits are awesome!!! Really cool and lots of history & info.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCVGyYhjRE2hy1smNC2IX_J&fid=0x0:0xb46b68a535be221e"],"When":"2025-11-30"},{"Name":"Peter Aba","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWmDSnfYqLdtS7DkhvNrshz-rA0e66W-XElxLUG7nvKhbY_rM9v=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Steam locos are overrepresented. Although rather small, due to bad management by historical institutions in the past that allowed the destruction of a big part after the discovery, the explanation and guided tour from the team in charge is outstanding.
Free entrance (Just need to pay the regular subway entrance to access the location), but you need to book a visit in the museum web page","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Melinda Merola","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVFngu06ulrW8u0ebmP25yp3ZtA_2hDyVGjpV0tNJZcO5Eqrw4O=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I wasn't sure what to expect from this museum that I'd passed so many times in the Opera metro station. user_reviews: [{"Name":"Lorenzo Berardinelli","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLQYZfs7aGmPhWV6GArevvBQrV3lL4dmCAQU_1872nRZD-NgA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I visited the Museum of Illusions in Madrid with my girlfriend, and it was a fun experience overall. However, I think it’s more enjoyable if you go in a group of more than two people, since many of the photo spots are designed for pairs or groups. Inside, there aren’t that many interactive activities, but the main issue is definitely the number of children running around — though of course, you can’t really ask them not to come! Other than that, we had a good time. I’d recommend going with friends or people you know you can have fun with anywhere.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDmml8-lSdI7MJDcz6dCc1F\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-10-27"},{"Name":"Darren Beck","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLRfTQ5lzSivZGEcb4EWCur2uNoffJUe10rBNwzOdNobY-mUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A little small but worth an hour or so if you want a break from the heat. Note that it definitely skews more toward a younger crowd, and there were a lot of families with little kids there. As a group of two adults though, it was still a fun experience. I’m sure the kids had a great time as well. Staff were great also. Give it a try if you need a break from the sightseeing and just want some fun relaxation and air conditioning!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCDZBKUvmAhb_LOAn8QK7LQ\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-13"},{"Name":"rated by maryam • food critic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV2rFU889_uyIosz_7bpffSFpmpz0-gKcaVKqZvHb7h1qD_qhXV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Absolutely entertaining from start to finish! The Museum of Illusions in Madrid kept us all fully engaged — we could’ve easily spent two hours exploring. The installations are clever, interactive, and truly high quality. My kids had a blast, and honestly, so did I. Every room is picture perfect, and the staff made sure the flow was easy and fun. Whether you’re going with kids or just want to play with perspective and be wowed, this spot delivers.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCvIn2cbOwD1xlT2Bju8D5l\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-7-1"},{"Name":"Alex S","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWuqMxZHWW4Q3oWg1MozJ5EDSP48AY-vf9ImsmDzcUsLKEKjbGa=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wow! What a great museum! If you want to find where the magic is at in Madrid, look no further! Offering many illusions for you to try out and capture, this museum is incredibly fun for everyone of all ages except people who otherwise have vertigo, as some of the illusions you will have to walk through may not be good for people with vertigo.\nIf you are wanting a fun activity to do with your kids, it’s a great place! Depending on your pace, you could be at this museum for a while. My favourite illusion was the vortex, as it is a really cool experience and it really makes you feel like you are spinning in an actual vortex. If that’s your thing, it is the last illusion downstairs at the very end of the room. This museum is also not far from the train line, so you can travel here easily.\nIn sum, if you’re wanting something to do while you’re in Madrid, come here!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAiCzHJZBmXN2Y3VDQxPYol\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-7-7"},{"Name":"Michael Stockdale","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIIrWLzicQpgC3hAFUv5MqBQNE3eX1_sKxt7Pa3MR8iF60jyQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"It was okay but l was hoping to see more than l did for the entry price.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCoNl4gi2TxojBRMWk7xy-N\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-12-15"},{"Name":"Rebecca Sirois","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUVZzU3H_Gmj---yW_cc8WSqFd5G5oCZ4-hT-20m9bPEiVF3i3R=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such a fun experience with the kids. Definitely buy tickets in advance, they don't let you in early so show up right on time. Staff were helpful for picture taking otherwise a fun place to wander through at your leisure.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCS7-Y2SbFgXGvCL3e_1CBg\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-29"},{"Name":"Joel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWl5821tyzJrwoJ9WtZ4ckHFtm77vetb8svyUaCTvs_yjbDKrzH=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We had such a great time at the Museum of Illusions in Madrid! I went with my two kids and they absolutely loved it — from the mind-bending exhibits to all the fun photo ops, there was never a dull moment. A big shoutout to Roberto, who went above and beyond. He was incredibly kind and patient, explaining how the illusions worked in a way my kids could understand, and even helped us take some amazing photos. He showed us the best angles and made sure we got the most out of each exhibit. Definitely recommend for a fun, family-friendly outing. So much fun and lots of laughs!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA41UAbda0q4GHtxLgczpgq\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-29"},{"Name":"David Sargent","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXNd7Vx1pxVZ2hd91K_Xk_NBWo4ZUDgz8hEItPBONm_A7r1mRme=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I love finding unusual museums, and I read about this one on WikiTravel when I had some spare time on a recwnt business trip. This museum was an absolute delight! My only regret was that I was alone, as a lot of the displays are interactive and the couples and groups were having so much fun watching and photographing each other and srading places and such that I decided that someday I need to go back there with at least one other person who hasn't seen it before. Fingers crossed for another trip to Madrid one of these days!!","Images":null,"When":""}]
