user_reviews:
[{"Name":"PL Lau","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX_kjCx3N7Oe5EIg03qgHTSqYbHJ9Dr8h6_hZbfJqZYWeYejkv9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A Nostalgic Trip Through Gaming History
⭐⭐⭐⭐

If you have any interest in gaming, OXO Museum is an absolute must-visit in Madrid. It turned into an amazing walk down memory lane for us!

What we loved:

Hands-on Experience: The biggest plus is that you can actually play almost everything. We saw everything from old Commodore computers and classic Sega machines to Pac-Man and a massive range of retro and modern systems.

Childhood Memories: We spent a fantastic couple of hours playing on my childhood console. It was the perfect way to relive old memories while making new ones.

Educational Context: It’s not just an arcade; there are great historical explanations, descriptions, and exhibits that really give you a sense of the evolution of the industry.

A few things to note:
Price: At €21, it is a tad pricey compared to other museums in the city.

Time Limit: Your visit is limited to two hours, which felt somewhat restrictive. Given how much there is to see and—more importantly—play, you have to keep an eye on the clock!

Overall: Despite the price and time limit, it’s a fantastic, well-curated space. Perfect for families or anyone looking to reconnect with their favorite classic games.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBkStWpxMgrPoRNvWDgtfnw&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-2-8"},{"Name":"Diego Schwarz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIqpGpnlnPKQWw4-vgrZrUxVnsOcgERKIZ7Z9T8_P5T6tSZqA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Honestly, we weren’t expecting much — and wow, were we wrong! 😄 It turned into an amazing walk down memory lane. We had a fantastic couple of hours playing on my childhood console, reliving old memories and making new ones at the same time. Such a fun, special experience for both my kid and me. Highly recommended!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAedZjQDHFVCjbXap6SINXI&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-1-4"},{"Name":"Joe Szabo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqlMOLx09MrD42Yu00kBfRybp1LlyD_EY8OUGGMpNkMAlhnys4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"OXO Museo del Videojuego Madrid is a fantastic place for anyone interested in the history of video games. Spread across three floors, the museum showcases every generation of gaming from the 1960s and 70s all the way to the latest PlayStation consoles.

You can try almost everything — from old Commodore computers and classic Sega Machines and Pac-Man games to a wide range of retro and modern systems. There are historical explanations, descriptions, exhibits, and plenty of hands-on gameplay, which is a big plus.

The adult ticket is €21, which I find a bit expensive, and the visit is limited to two hours, which feels somewhat restrictive considering how much there is to see and play.

Overall, I’d give it 4 stars — but for anyone who loves video games, it’s definitely worth a visit.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDMjCWyiWGSXjNSHlW9acs-&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Siv Carina Molvik","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWfHfJ-c2ljwqULxk8LJK81CWRBkqoj4OUL8Uduvdie9XnbnCI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Whether you are a younger gamer who loves dropping Tilted Towers and playing LEGO versions of games or an older gamer who remembers PONG fondly like it was yesterday, this place is going to amaze you! Spend a couple of hours walking down memory lane of games and play ALL of them! Interactive museum indeed! Highly recommend!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBCro9zn2JwMcS7TJhlB_dG&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-1-26"},{"Name":"Kam Loqueman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUxC4clvyzC3x-fNmSBYU0Vrhvk05LFQnTDSuN-p7D6-yD4FCzj-Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One could argue a tad pricey at 21 Euros but for me well worth it. Brought back so much nostalgia and the fact you could touch and play with 40+ year old tech was brilliant. The arcade was great fun playing games of my youth..for free! The Lego exhibition was a bonus.

I visited on a Saturday and it wasn't too busy at all.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBSR2u9NhpzjJrowg7093ry&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-11-16"},{"Name":"Vivian Martinez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVxe-YsjP5k6BiGnY8-7zA7gI6OHs8j_MTCaODEYXkYCZxE__0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"If you’re a video game fan, this one’s for you! Have never seen anything like it. 7@ years worth of video game history, you get to play in all (and I mean ALL) the consoles you can think of from 70 years back. Truly a blast from the past! Make sure to make a reservation.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAsPWVPbdWKhFNzLTKmrwIA&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-10-7"},{"Name":"Lawana “Mitchi” Walker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKyssC3nkPp_pZqwmcbuNrVmcnOaoycZTYhTvAsDH-Ec2q8UARRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Went on a weekday afternoon and there were only a couple other families there.

It is a little expensive BUT they do have a reduced rate for families, & an annual pass that I personally think is worth it. I've seen some reviews that said some of the games didn't work, but I only saw maybe 3 or 4 games/systems that weren't working. The history of videogames part is super cool, & they have a huge selection of gaming systems on display & available to play. The arcade is also super cool, the Japanese games were the most fun for my son. The PlayStation floor was pretty cool, too. The entire place is themed very well, it's clean, in good condition & a nice place to spend a couple of hours as a family.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBmtrAubrN2mFcA5v0O8vhQ&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-7-3"},{"Name":"Diogo Morais","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLLo3-eNubWf7buJzmV4e2jyPV9FE1UJME0WrCQxukl-eqvWQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Very good museum with a lot of games and arcades to try out!
My biggest critic is that the parents don't understand their kids can't be on the same game for long time... Needs more control on that part a lot of machines had the same kids there for more than 10 minutes....","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAshlO6DoTDkUTtdUNcLoLA&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-9-13"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Helen Stasa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXkfbRD5S6JkCLjo4woGkC6XUh2i9JY7Y96xNg9wsDsS9wFsiE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"It’s an interesting idea, but there’s definitely some improvements that could be made.
1) In booking the tour, you’re obliged to have a back-and-forward WhatsApp exchange with an employee about the time. Surely this could be automated? Similarly, the tone of the WhatsApp messages is very patronising- they treat you like you lack basic comprehension skills e.g. “Be advised that the museum is in MADRID.” Yes. We know.
2) The staff member was clearly bored and uninterested and going through the motions. The tour started 10 minutes late (the bossy WhatsApp messages insisted we MUST be early), and, upon noticing that I had an accent, the staff member told me “The tour is in Spanish. It’s not in English.” I told her (in Spanish) that understood that, and that I can understand Spanish. I considered the conversation closed, but at the end of the tour, she again insisted on addressing me in English in front of everyone. I found this impolite, and bad customer service. In addition, she had a bored expression on her face the whole time, which was hardly fun for the kids.
3) The rooms are full of interesting things, but you don’t get given enough time to look at them. Instead, you’re rushed from room to room.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICn09fydA&fid=0x0:0x7180acca61fb61bb"],"When":"2024-9-29"},{"Name":"Carol Matiz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ1TvbhuEQcxzksgRIkjdPZG2rSXSfB66q3q_pi3jXKZDwTMJY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Our visit was great, we love it! The storyteller was excellent just a little fast telling the story, but we still had a very good experience our little one enjoyed it. And they don't let you take pictures or videos.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Amnet Pantaleon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVdOrQg7I6VMdSmP1_34UfqqjOYsmOKaVqCJOzPiGHpUBuQY9oI=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Beautiful place and loving staff.very magical for the little ones and the entire family. Highly recommended.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sarah B","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWJbDlKPDDo4d5sMWGfEHsjEEpymmi8Jw8jqCw_HBuUDm8pw8T_qg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We visited with our three children (ages 10, 9 and 7) after a visit to the Royal Palace.

Initially told the 5pm tour full so we were going to book for 6pm but it became clear we were English so we were allowed to go on our own as likely we don't speak enough Spanish to benefit from guided tour.

The museum is located in a sort of mini shopping arcade on the main road between Sol and Opera. It's easy to miss. We went up to the museum and one of the staff members spoke good English so showed us into the cartoon presentation (in Spanish but understood enough that we got the story) she also gave us a book with the English translation in to read too. We are then given a map (in English) to spot the key items as you move to the three rooms.

It's a very quirky museum full of legend about the tooth mouse and the children enjoyed clambering through the little tunnels and seeing if they started getting an itchy nose. We liked the biscuit tin room and the special post box and the tunnel where they found the special coins left for them.

I would say that at 5 euro per person it didn't feel like amazing value for a family of five BUT that said the children loved it and the member of staff engaged with them beautifully and helped us to find the mouse door out on the main road.

Great little museum if you have children similar ages to ours but not the best value thing you will do during a day in Madrid.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"BasicReviews","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUm35sAVzX2zZyFrASlJrXPsSi8kGFv4Qx_OGrBZHL2J2GI-HxlQA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Awesome place. Nice staff. Good story.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEI7xkqi1_Zz5Bg&fid=0x0:0x7180acca61fb61bb"],"When":"2025-5-23"},{"Name":"Lole Ramirez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvEBMqQIRYo0vU_HXUyb6ZBsN_Bl8-JeCVOFMX-lOykIJc7g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It was ok, but not very interactive and our 5 year old did not engage, which is odd as she is every curious.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Monica Lidia Rodriguez Estrada","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVxxKYl6aehcsVQdmZchgXc-dHiaqbW_yDPd0kkbQNCOqZAaDUK=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The staff was nice although the museum was quite small not something I was expecting. I went with my 17 month old and 6 year old.  I was advised by the guide to please step out of the explanation in  case  the baby would start crying, it made sense since it was crowded. and hardly had any room to walk. I would not recommend it for babies unless an adult can stay with them outside.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Cian Donohoe","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUpR7dgPhfhDsUehrwXOBt8f4Z9NWaR-YlOEIb9iyx_rYXfHSAF=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fantastic food and atmosphere. The waiter Juan was incredible, very nice, very funny, and made sure we were enjoying ourselves. Would definitely recommend for an evening meal and some drinks","Images":null,"When":""}]

Si pregano quindi i curatori del museo di apportare la dicitura “chiuso” sul sito Google anziché “aperto il mercoledì “","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDopZKEQExBPGJRwjwLyG8N&fid=0x0:0x26395748a589a3e5"],"When":"2025-11-19"},{"Name":"Jose Andrés Elizondo Fdez-Cuervo /Eli","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVTzSJ45JY2HxmeixiDtuHFMsl7-60fJKPVbBsK__jj-keyD7mtcQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una pasada y un pasado oculto que conviene recordar.
Es impresionante la de objetos auténticos que tienen.
Y si tienes la suerte de una visita guiada es genial.
Es pura historia sin polarizaciones de un pasado que forma parte de nuestra historia de España 👌💯👍","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Nuan Masa Eros","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLqeUSl3WORL5a3CMEboXX3uWCC__p1bBDzJqnXL5DMr8s_kg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Este museo dedicado a la historia de la División Azul es un lugar único en Madrid. Ofrece una visión histórica detallada sobre este episodio de la historia española. Las exposiciones son muy completas, con uniformes, documentos y objetos personales de los soldados. Es un espacio educativo que permite entender mejor este capítulo histórico. La visita resulta muy interesante para los amantes de la historia militar. Muy bien conservado y organizado.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Gus Tavo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWuxoqR3ojExsmsTr9ZJQVldKB5wCwjEjMFC1oZPQktN0nCgUx9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Tenía muchas ganas de visitarlo y al final lo he conseguido gracias a la invitación de uno de sus miembros, pues no es un museo al uso. La colección de material es increíble, las maquetas Y recreaciones muy buenas, y el guía explica muy bien la historia de esos españoles que fueron a luchar a Rusia. Llamad y concertar la visita antes de ir o no podréis entrar.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDJ3ImcUg&fid=0x0:0x26395748a589a3e5"],"When":"2023-7-12"},{"Name":"Ciber Hispania (Edu)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVMNOqbr6iKDl6ErhbaIOJQrZPmY52HQXHv6syBmZIa2h6yXGku=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Increíble la acumulación de objetos militares originales.
Muy meritorio el Trabajo de Recreación de zonas del Frente, o el hospital, o la Cantina.
Excelente colección de uniformes, medallas.
Excelente explicación por los voluntarios de la Asociación.
Zona de Bar a precios muy bajos.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC44s7CKQ&fid=0x0:0x26395748a589a3e5"],"When":"2017-6-9"}]

You have to make an appointment. Would have been great to know this before putting all the time into finding the damn place and making the long trek out here","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jack Johnson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI8xo438JDNLHjwwNF4DTVtNEV7x7thUCLsqvcF0-oR2mOPJA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Hard to find and opening hours on herr were incorrect apparently","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Martin Hilvers","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV3oalmaDxws8BwlgqwgAjq5il5cmZctPauaw_-EUHo9g80p9k=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Impossible to find.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"chris gabriel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXBMSQ_V9l6h5e8HVDkbkUpsWb7HpW7VtGYnBpq-GgL9jvn4kfR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Ubicado en la Facultad de Medicina de la Universidad Complutense de Madrid, el Museo de Anatomía Javier Puerta es una joya poco conocida pero fascinante para quienes se interesan por el cuerpo humano y la historia de la medicina. Al entrar, uno tiene la sensación de estar viajando en el tiempo: vitrinas de madera, iluminación tenue y una cuidada disposición de las piezas crean una atmósfera que combina lo académico con lo casi museístico-artístico.

El recorrido comienza con modelos anatómicos de cera del siglo XIX, que impresionan tanto por su realismo como por el detalle minucioso con el que están elaborados. Estas figuras, muchas de ellas creadas con fines didácticos, muestran órganos, sistemas musculares y estructuras óseas con una precisión que todavía hoy resulta impactante. También hay esqueletos humanos, preparaciones en formol y una colección destacada de cráneos y fetos que permite entender la evolución de los estudios anatómicos en España.

Uno de los aspectos que más me llamó la atención fue la combinación de ciencia y arte. Algunas piezas, especialmente los modelos más antiguos, no solo buscan enseñar sino también provocar una reflexión estética sobre el cuerpo humano. Además, el personal del museo —en su mayoría estudiantes o profesionales de la medicina— está muy bien informado y dispuesto a responder preguntas, lo que enriquece mucho la visita.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCvhHDugJq2jC6CbaKS7s3z&fid=0x0:0x5e4509e9f7642276"],"When":"2025-8-29"},{"Name":"Mases Vinyard","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV29zDXlwWsdYYeFCr5CoLkafJ_J4G5h7UjAIk82-0UqbQYcuQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Estupena visita durante el jueves 06 de febrero de 2025.

El profesor Fermín recibió al grupo con entusiasmo, compartiendo con nosotros la historia y secretos del Museo y las espectaculares piezas que alberga.

Una visita para recordar siempre. Mis felicitaciones al profesor por su amabilidad y su gran labor didáctica.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Maite Leon-Sotelo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXmODoiCNxu0jg_-IlvyUX-QC9SnouE30EI88Di02L21Ei4ZvI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Es otra de las joyas que alberga la UCM, esperemos que no se pierda","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Aleksei Nasedkin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIDGogfOsA5W-1zde8PriymSPslyyAJGSnLC5og36t4XGCahQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Este Museo es espectacular. Especialmente estara interesante para personas quien quiere saber mas sibre historia de desarolllo de medicina, autopcia, patalogias de enfermedades. El doctor Fermin quien guia la excurcion es un gran especialista, cuenta muy interesante y siempre esta preparado a responder a cualquier pregunta. La exposición tambien es vale la pena a ver. La mayoria de los exponates no son de verdad, no son partes de cadaveres. Pero esas maquetes estan hecho de cera y tallados con mucha finidad. Admas estan exibidos mesa de autipsia de siglo 18, aparato de exibicion de cadaver de siglo 19, esquletos con patologias. Recomiendado 100% para visita.","Images":null,"When":""}]

Usually we would get a pitcher of cold water with ice so everyone could drink, but this guy told us we had to buy the bottled water and that the boss told him to sell everything he could, no freebies. That day we cut our meal short. My daughter got upset and left! I spoke to the manager that was in charge that evening and he excused himself for the waiter. He informed me that he would speak to the waiter in question. The young waiter (Estevan) that was working with Andreas was also apologetic and I informed him that we didn’t want the other waiter to come to our table anymore. [{"Name":"Daniel Lobo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWP7EtNH8nWsJ_7-rcmcycS2S2SFrJbzMn-Q7X9OwsbXNcNwMlS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fun historic visit to the decommissioned Chamberí Metro station. Wonderful care has been taken to offer the opportunity to access the old station. The free visit is somewhat brief but insightful. The first part is guided by knowledgeable staff, which adds robust historic context. And it ends with some time to linger and photograph.

Lifetime iconic as it can be seen from the passing trains of Metro Line 1, it was fun to visit after so many years and enjoy how well preserved it is. Reservations are best to ensure timed access with one of the small tour groups. But folks without a ticket can also wait in an open queue at the street entrance. And if the group is not full they are invited to join the slot.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDZnC13iSo3BOd_38wbjPSR&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-10-7"},{"Name":"Raluca Chirita","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI520GJb2nosTLbyFObR6VpPrEVUChv4XKd7CDwcMIdrGFZkw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The station is in really great shape and is really interesting to see how metro stations looked in the beginning. The museum is free of charge and there is a free guide, but it is only in spanish. You cannot visit the station on your own and you have to wait for the tour guide. If you don't speak Spanish is quite boring to wait for the tour guide to speak. There is a qr code at the beginning with a brief information guide in English, but I feel like it did not contain everything that tour guide explained in person.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD44tZlXYHq7jGJf4sjj9Z4&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-11-29"},{"Name":"Rinor Zejna","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUvvIekDoiakC0lcbOFvJQGN4mjdoSd_J6yW7G_q8jDxJDZekMP=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This was one of the best places to visit in Madrid, especially that is free and with a guide that tells you the history of the Metro (you need to speak and understand Spanish tho). But it is an amazing experience to try, make sure to reserve the spot in advance as there are only a small number of groups that can enter, although you may enter without a reservation but it is risky and the waiting time can be a lot.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDQb2_vTQ-T1qoP2HUrxwr9&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-11-6"},{"Name":"TravelingDave","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVPCrljDYhlOATfCbyYZ9A7VQbXiacTlndePDddXXJdRhME57CL=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Old station preserved. Highly recommend a visit. Only complaint is I wish it was opened more days.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD2cQnA3b5ms8af-bEwihpt&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2026-2-20"},{"Name":"Ruben Perfetto","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU2uggQ_5xKUD5-hkwA03izN3qimvpvvIWnQlkHkl1U6fgnf4Kcxg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Excellent guide tour, full of history and incredible work for the 1900th.
Free admission, last week of the month you can reserve your spot, what it is highly recommended. They reserved 25 spot and tree for walk ins.
Tour run from 4:00pm every 1/2 and hour.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEKnSrJLHyLSo7wE&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-5-24"},{"Name":"Ivo Driesser","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIciPniOzcbLK9IFEiJhdmfNrwnHpZemYdTRmQ68X9Aq62_JQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Nice opportunity to travel in time.
It’s a pity that there wasn’t an English tour. Nevertheless they offer information in PDF format in English.
Check online to reserve your tickets.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBVyiiYrgDC88WX2WxyF4Qi&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-10-20"},{"Name":"Navnish N","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXefl5E1EmAwE-upSkIAStxun_gBvfSrsyE0-DGSA75Wn6aViCC=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very unique experience. The guide speaks in Spanish but he does ask if anyone needs information in English. It was a good experience to see something which is preserved well. Book your tickets in advance we booked ours 2 weeks prior. A must visit if you are in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICZtuuH_QE&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2023-9-10"},{"Name":"Nina Liu","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV9hmUHjjBS1nrNNkzP024qy_M5YiDBJIbKFV4VaqoNOes6O0Fo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I made a reservation online beforehand (Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays) but I think you can also go there and wait in line to see if there are free spots for the upcoming tour. The whole tour - only in Spanish, lasted around 40 mins or so and you can have some time afterwards for photo taking. I liked it as it’s like going back in times and you can see still metros passing by the station!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAil-SnGuBoU4fZfyoT8t00&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-6-25"}]

Está justo donde el ascensor, pudiéndose acceder desde la calle directamente. Se trata de una exposición con unos cuantos paneles en la pared de la historia del metro y de la estación de gran vía, con carteles informativos y objetos encontrados durante la construcción, así como algunas cosas históricas del metro. Está bastante chulo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCAkcP-Rg&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2025-2-1"},{"Name":"Aran A.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfH4gTdkqeLJoNdPTUdr4gPj6MGWSVPQd-CSuAwybErQs_eJQxFQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"No hace falta pagar billete para verlo son unas tres o cuatro vitrinas donde se pueden ver cosas encontradas y antiguas del metro. Depende de lo interesado que estés pero se ve en menos de 5 minutos. Lo más interesante para mí es el emblema- escudo de Madrid","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDRy8uJKA&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2023-5-3"},{"Name":"Jordi Fajula","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJbvMkNWXEHot-zzw0mI3KEF1Ch8NJprx_I6RSrSHOzsdoFvA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Me ha parecido una forma interesante de conocer cómo fue una parte de la reforma de lo que es hoy Gran Vía. Puedes ver un emblema de madrid","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Raúl Fernández López","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX607swNaQjjzE_mhMfXHDElPsOGK5mKK6UpWfARpRFuleEVsKylg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una parte importante de la historia de Madrid es la historia del metro de Madrid.

Metro a través de este pequeñísimo museo muestra todo lo que se encontraron tras la remodelación de la estación de metro de Gran Vía. De los restos a destacar fue el emblema de Madrid.

Museo de entrada gratuita ya que se sitúa fuera de los tornos del metro y se halla en la segunda planta.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDW8p7fHA&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2022-3-20"},{"Name":"Jörge","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWXeWEXqdr5t3OlbRokb_Dhsg38mDGwuZo9JWUkx-spF0MuHs0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo en el que se exponen diferentes piezas procedentes de las excavaciones arqueológicas realizadas en el año 2019 durante las obras de ampliación y remodelación de la estación. user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Raz Maoz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU_JjPTGQF7fDVY4j9TjcAXUbfEf0FTyO37LPVvC3DZ87QKsidCeA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We visited this museum with different expectations and both left absolutely fascinated. For my wife, who has studied the history of fashion and haute couture, this place fit like a glove. She found a professional and deep analysis of silhouettes, textiles, and craftsmanship at the highest level.
But the real surprise was for me. As a history buff, I found a comprehensive and gripping chronological journey. The clothes are just the medium: through them, you see the Industrial Revolution, the wars, the political shifts in Spain, and the social transformations of Europe.

It’s far more than a 'costume exhibit' – it’s tangible history, phenomenally organized, from 18th-century corsets to the groundbreaking designs of Balenciaga.
A true hidden gem in Madrid, highly recommended even if you don't consider yourself a 'fashion person'.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lawana “Mitchi” Walker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKyssC3nkPp_pZqwmcbuNrVmcnOaoycZTYhTvAsDH-Ec2q8UARRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I enjoyed this museum quite a lot. The descriptions were good for the pieces, and they were arranged in chronological order with historical context for each section. Some of the exhibits are dark -- which is by design. Light is extremely damaging to textiles, and the low light is necessary to preserve the items. The low light of the rooms is to prevent a harsh glare on the glass of the exhibits, making it easier to see the items in the low lighting that is required. The only thing I personally wish was included was more information about the people who made the garments, as well as the processes of making the materials & garments. It was a nice relaxing way to spend a Saturday afternoon.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCwoNnLZA&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2025-3-15"},{"Name":"yael fayna","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJG-0a9FbqzdCVfD7oKkmOCuuvZztmzAR4nBVqvWImted6o3w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It is not at the city center and this is the benefit, very interesting museum I loved the curatorial process in the sexhibition","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBOvLgHqXlu-cq6TWkzZ-q3&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2026-1-27"},{"Name":"Jakub M Ochab","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXWeOsgMckABLzCjQG-D7kanzxb_tm5gLRx84U6XGHKPXlPU4Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We visited the Museo del Traje again over the weekend, and it continues to be one of Madrid’s hidden gems. Despite its location on the edge of the city, it wasn’t very busy—which actually made for a peaceful and enjoyable experience. It’s a perfect option if you’re planning to spend time in nearby parks or looking for a quieter cultural stop away from the city center.

This time, we went specifically for the seasonal exhibitions, and admission was free. The exhibitions were well-curated and offered a fresh perspective on fashion and design. The museum staff were all very friendly and attentive, which added to the overall positive experience.

There’s also a nice café on site where you can relax with some food, coffee, or a drink—ideal for a break during your visit. Don’t miss the gift shop downstairs either. It has a great selection of fashion-related books and unique items that are definitely worth browsing.

All in all, the Museo del Traje is a wonderful blend of fashion, history, and calm atmosphere—definitely worth a visit whether you’re a fashion enthusiast or just looking for a quieter cultural outing in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCeMzq71dIhXAPbZQjIYB1X&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2025-7-20"},{"Name":"Alejandro Perez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKHk2nbyV1HYyUXx8bXnKtGbRG7n3r_5fqwa7ozq4iCVkJWQw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Small and amazing museum. Great place to bring your family!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB8aNK2UHUlAiv9nPBuWhWl&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Jelen Sita","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLahMROOG8JNp7RJvZFjIqI8kI5SuUrnqmMJd-MtC6n4zfMvQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The day was one of those frigid windy winter days in Madrid that chill you to the bone, especially if you walk there from the metro station- a good 10 mins away on wide avenues which only act as wind tunnels....When I arrived I was greeted by an impressive über-modern "container- looking" building - the only drawback was the equally impressive set of cement stairs that lead up to the main entrance(*).  I am sure they had a wheelchair accessible elevator from the ground up but I didn't have time nor the inclination to look for it!!
Now as a veritable human popsicle I was greeted, -after trudging up the stairs- by a large but well heated foyer (which was appreciated instantly). It was sweetly staffed by welcoming people who chuckled commiseratingly at my thankful antics.
It is an easy museum to navigate (all on one floor) and the collection here was far fuller (in regards to the male clothing) than the one in Barcelona. It is amazing how, as a society, the man has been pressured from wearing intricate and delicate embroidery with beautiful floral patterns to a stark simple dark clothing with no embellishment excepting, perhaps, the cut. The only nod to a more changeling offer for the guys was the modern and future clothing, which was interesting.
The female showcase was very prevalent throughout the ages and explains pretty well what the intricacy of the layers were. The most amazing is to consider the fact that the apparatus to enhance the "derrier" was ever worn...
Most of the clothing was representative of the high society so I really enjoyed the great collection of what the less "economically proud" wore- which shows far more useful logic in its design.
There is also a modest lineup (13?) of  clothing designed for the blind to be able to touch and get a feeling of the changes in clothing (mostly female, one male) during the ages. I found this wonderfully inclusive. They could offer a taped explanation (or on tablets in Braille?) of each piece (in the "sighted" part) for those that are recently blind but remember colors and textures?
I would have liked a bit more info of each: the materials used, the name of the designer (if known), the price it might have cost (translated to nowadays euros).  One thing that was commendable is that all the explanations, that were there, were translated into English.
I would suggest they look into using  l.e.d. lighting for those windows where light was very dim so not to damage the cloth...what is the use if you can't see it?!

Overall a great museum! Doable in one hour or so (since it has few placards), with a couple of didactic screens to explain some of the displays...

(*)Oh there is also a lovely looking cafe and a temporary collection (they were announcing "Jeans") both of which are at ground level but I didn't have the time to visit.
I highly recommend it (but dress -ha!- well if coming from the metro in winter time!)
P.S. I asked if they ever staged a real "runway"(pasarela) of modern designs (they could be students' creations?!) but they didn't understand my question and seemed eager to close...","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID1gYWo2QE&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2024-1-7"},{"Name":"Seokjin Ham","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIhtf6A7X2UDP--Xi1vhwXxfhLJk0GUh_pTYJvgqrBOTATbUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This is one of the national museums where you can explore the history of Spain's traditional clothing and fashion. The building was built in 1973, but the current museum opened in 2004. It houses more than 160,000 items of clothing, accessories, and textiles. Because it is relatively quiet, it is easy to obtain an annual membership to the national museums. There is a beautiful garden nearby, so you can enjoy a leisurely walk.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDP7JTNRQ&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2024-11-29"},{"Name":"Kathy Patton","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUI5xWFJ7mPNsJDqWmP_hMM6aI9XBZmZV1AQIcLLMEYEq0_zx4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such an interesting museum learning about Spanish history through clothing across the years. We really enjoyed our visit, especially on a hot day in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDrhYapjgE&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2024-7-18"}]

Un monumento emotivo y cargado de simbolismo. La estatua del padre con su hijo muerto en brazos es muy cruda pero miy apropiada. Creo que es un buen sitio para visitar en el parque. Además es muy tranquilo y los atardeceres le dan una iluminación interesante. Desde luego, hay que verlo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDE2OXPpgE\u0026fid=0x0:0xbe1d847df899d403"],"When":"2019-1-10"},{"Name":"Alejandro de Ildefonso (Sirone)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX9dRyiWORQfwWE0wQmm-HAwwCaToAX7TKYdFvS8d74UJgJV0XxyQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Monumento extraño y curioso, el parque entero esta muy cuidado y da gusto asistir. title: Monumento a la Paz
address: Barajas, 28042 Madrid
open_hours: {"Friday":["Open 24 hours"],"Monday":["Open 24 hours"],"Saturday":["Open 24 hours"],"Sunday":["Open 24 hours"],"Thursday":["Open 24 hours"],"Tuesday":["Open 24 hours"],"Wednesday":["Open 24 hours"]}
user_reviews: [{"Name":"Francisco Lucena","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXXAnbPEgO38IAgd5mIrYHf4QXKfnMypv1751f6z9U8cL9E_HQHDQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"El monumento a la paz situado en el parque Juan Carlos 1 de Madrid es una obra abstracta de acero, bronce y hormigón de la artista brasileña Yolanda  d'augsbur creada en 1992 para comer la conferencia de paz de Madrid de 1991","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAoZqwsxZ7X_NTuCJLtphHB\u0026fid=0x0:0xfc996347687c236e"],"When":"2025-10-13"},{"Name":"Tito Rubio","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUQxmo6mQaJ-jpXRWak2CdDTM5wjRUdpYxV0TDYJj1nb75_p6dZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Obra de la escultora y pintora brasileña Yolanda D'Augsburg, para conmemorar la Conferencia de Paz entre israelíes y palestinos celebrada en Madrid en noviembre de 1991. Fue instalada en octubre de 1992 e inaugurada el 6 de noviembre del mismo año en un acto presidio por los reyes de España. Está ubicada en el acceso al parque desde el aparcamiento principal y es una de las piezas (concretamente la nº 18) que integra la llamada Senda de las Esculturas.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICJwOvadg\u0026fid=0x0:0xfc996347687c236e"],"When":"2023-6-18"},{"Name":"Fernando Vicente Perez Garcia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX6wGdM99zZLynhF8i2zFY---iX3UA_Bk_aEGSGhuxaRdZiE4w3zg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Una escultura dedicada a la Paz, a la conferencia de Paz entre Israelis y Palestinos de Madrid en 1992, es triste escribir esta reseña en un momento que el conflicto entre Israelis y árabes esta en un punto álgido, haciendo de esta conferencia y este escultura un sueño roto.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Doncelador","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXnewNEbYYaXdHD0epgVByIWzW2jKqBZMPTzU18-HYzM_sBuOGCqw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"El conjunto escultórico data de noviembre de 1.992 siendo obra de Yolanda D'Ausburg, se creó para conmemorar la conferencia de paz entre palestinos e israelíes celebrada en Madrid un año antes. Está formado por tres elementos, el cuerpo más grande de acero llamado Las Raíces de España, otros dos elementos de acero inoxidable llamados Los Lazos y por último una columna de acero llamada La Raíz de la Paz. Todo el conjunto descansa sobre un gran bloque de hormigón en cual una placa de bronce nos cuenta la historia del monumento. Se patrocinó por el Corte Inglés.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Meeth56","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXP6S8BKpPOY2pIU4eiz0sTCVOBYiUbDy6hpje2Np5U7BqfROXWtg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Enorme parque - en general con buen mantenimiento. Cuidado con los perros sueltos.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Julian Garcia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKgfrc2k_THsVVVf6Vp3X5MSBIuUrTlOtsT6hweMBwrKGgT3Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Buen parque","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Carla Alvarez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJzBejR-_eJfyzGlt5LmLsFFoeqchdRj_69Hfg5yR7bWCqJPA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Iván Fernández Olavarrieta","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLjgZxkbSX51s-lkimA1DnqJhoEYG6L6SV-0lHHQtzox9zlOQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Ferhat Yildiz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWV6W2gfwUqAFIDgffx1HATO6JsIPotrLYKf7X6lAbflWpp-7tw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A charming tribute to one of Spain’s literary greats! The Statue of Jacinto Benavente, located in the peaceful Parque del Retiro in Madrid, is a simple yet elegant monument that beautifully honors the Nobel Prize-winning playwright.

The statue blends seamlessly into the park’s serene atmosphere, making it a lovely spot to pause, reflect, and appreciate Spanish culture.

Whether you’re a fan of literature or just strolling through Retiro, this statue is worth a quick visit for its historical significance and picturesque surroundings.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCom53nWQ&fid=0x0:0xdfb79e16d3b4103c"],"When":"2025-4-20"},{"Name":"Manuel Urquidi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVb3NqeqLMbUnMDHCCGqfKIrXOgRZeGLl7N3nZOPBiM7Lx4le2p=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This statue of the 1922 Nobel Prize in Literature winner, Jacinto Benavente, was funded through a public appeal that raised over one million pesetas. The statue, created by sculptor Victorio Macho, aims to immortalize the soul of the artist, as mentioned by Macho himself. It was inaugurated in April 1962. The area is also home to some oddly shaped trees like yew trees and horse chestnuts.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID_0_SrGQ&fid=0x0:0xdfb79e16d3b4103c"],"When":"2025-1-26"},{"Name":"Jim De Mauro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVYTq70wrnbeJxNH8_FvCSQjFCURTl0uZJE-ekM3hY8Wvk5v9dy=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"One of the most exotic trees we have been able to find was near this statue.  The shapes are exotic and intriguing.  We were not able to determine if they grew this way or they were pruned to look so unreal.  We spent a half an hour just sitting and taking photographs.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE0r-Kaw&fid=0x0:0xdfb79e16d3b4103c"],"When":"2017-12-31"},{"Name":"david howells","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXv00dm90sqOsiG9tuO5orrJOqUEDS-SlSH6CFfk8dmCBLrELi0Jw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Nice area of Retiró park, very pretty with some lovely quirky trees and a cool monument.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIChsorPDA&fid=0x0:0xdfb79e16d3b4103c"],"When":"2023-2-9"},{"Name":"Geovanny","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWpba9fybFOGKARgiQWJJKLXixzY6BzcQ8Id_v0GFsgkaMSRfk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Jacinto Benavente, a highly influential Spanish playwright born in Madrid in 1866 and who died there in 1954, significantly revitalized Spanish theater with his realistic comedies and social satires, earning him the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1922; his prolific output of plays, which explored the moral complexities of Spanish society, cemented his legacy as a key figure in 20th-century Spanish drama","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDIzOWHWA&fid=0x0:0xdfb79e16d3b4103c"],"When":"2025-4-7"},{"Name":"macedonboy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL7obIFVFelZpGZFWErr47Wzzrz8xLtHVDOYNGdpIt459EbUg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This monument stands in tribute to Jacinto Benavente, considered to be the foremost Spanish writer of the 20th century and was awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature in 1922.

The monument features a bronze classical sculpture featuring a woman (presumably an actress), hold a theatre mask as if about to place it onto her face. The sculpture stands atop a plinth, which a rectangular stone block bearing the name of Jacinto Benavente and a bronze relief sculpture of the writer.

A beautiful classical statue and all the more so for it's easy to understand reference to a Nobel winning writer.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICc_L-c-gE&fid=0x0:0xdfb79e16d3b4103c"],"When":"2020-2-17"},{"Name":"A V","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJZBqTmmYT3Q0x4e4TLC7rAKSnv94GL00nBaa9xzXt_6PhbxQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very well maintained and gorgeous area surrounding statue to famous Spanish dramatist.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID44KroBg&fid=0x0:0xdfb79e16d3b4103c"],"When":"2017-11-10"},{"Name":"Miguel P.C.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVPDy8QrAcFWEOFTkH4s_QD0aosAntu-Z4gpRrjANDg-25LaA6qXg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Beautiful garden, well kept and nice to walk around.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICO_oKtngE&fid=0x0:0xdfb79e16d3b4103c"],"When":"2022-6-11"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Doncelador","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXnewNEbYYaXdHD0epgVByIWzW2jKqBZMPTzU18-HYzM_sBuOGCqw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Es una donación de la Asociación de  descendientes del Exilio Español, obra del escultor Julián Martínez en 1.983, representa a Lázaro Cárdenas de cuerpo entero y vestido de traje de chaqueta y con sus brazos cruzados. Está sobre un pedestal en el cual podemos leer su nombre y ubicada en una plazoleta por la que se accede por cuatro escalinatas.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICqwvr1tgE&fid=0x0:0x888abd6f8b72f544"],"When":"2021-5-31"},{"Name":"Marcos Cabrera García","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWyOBiJvQHy8TN38eB5aq8FnksAylW07cNTllWUTeQp5Q6m66Sb=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"La estatua de Lázaro Cárdenas es un monumento conmemorativo ubicado en Madrid. Erigida en el Parque Norte, sirve como homenaje al presidente mexicano Lázaro Cárdenas, destacado por acoger a miles de exiliados republicanos españoles llegados a México escapando de la Guerra Civil española y la posterior dictadura franquista.

La aprobación de un homenaje en Madrid a Lázaro Cárdenas, el presidente mexicano que acogió a aproximadamente 30 000 exiliados republicanos españoles que huyeron de la Guerra Civil española y la posterior dictadura franquista, fue una de las primeras decisiones tomadas por la corporación democrática resultante de las elecciones municipales de 1979 Madrid, aprobada en la primera sesión plenaria del Ayuntamiento de Madrid. Los costes de la estructura fueron financiados por el consistorio,[1] mientras la estatua fue financiada mediante suscripción popular entre el colectivo republicano español en México. Aunque la pretensión inicial era simplemente erigir un busto, recabaron tanto dinero que se pudo encargar una estatua de tamaño completo.

El diseño del proyecto fue adjudicado al escultor mexicano Julián Martínez, un exiliado español que llegó a México de joven. Con una altura de 3,5 metros, la estatua de cuerpo completo resultó demasiado grande para su ubicación tentativa en la plaza del Presidente Cárdenas (dedicada en honor de Cárdenas en 1982), por lo que el Ayuntamiento acabó decantándose por una nueva ubicación en el Parque Norte. Fundida en bronce en México, los costes de cara al transporte del monumento a Madrid fueron también financiados por el colectivo republicano español en México. Joaquín Roldán y Santiago Cubero desempeñaron un papel en la erección del monumento como arquitectos.

El monumento fue inaugurado el 16 de noviembre de 1983 durante una ceremonia a la que asistieron Enrique Tierno Galván (alcalde de Madrid), Ramón Aguirre Velázquez (jefe de departamento de México DF), Amalia Solórzano (viuda de Cárdenas), Cuauhtémoc Cárdenas (hijo de Cárdenas), Enrique Barón, Joaquín Leguina y José Federico de Carvajal, entre otros.

Las dos placas que se añadieron inicialmente rezaban «el pueblo de madrid / a lázaro cárdenas / presidente de méxico (de 1934 a 1940)» y «el exilio republicano / español / con honda gratitud / y reconocimiento / a lázaro cárdenas / presidente de méxico».

En octubre de 2005, a raíz de una iniciativa de la Asociación de Descendientes del Exilio Español, se añadió una nueva placa que rezaba «padre de los españoles sin patria y sin derechos, perseguidos por la tiranía y desheredados por el odio», un fragmento de una cita dedicada a Cárdenas por parte de Álvaro de Albornoz en la época en la que este llegó a México como exiliado.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDRx-XK2AE&fid=0x0:0x888abd6f8b72f544"],"When":"2023-5-5"},{"Name":"ANTONIO JESÚS MARTIN","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXKub6pNKeczKmOp0H0-jV6Losp_GxbKHrk-bLGNbe_wHDuI52wrw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Es una de las esculturas que más me ha gustado de este parque, y está ubicada en la zona más alta del mismo.

Representa a Lázaro Cárdenas del Río, que fue presidente de México entre los años 1934 y 1940. Este presidente acogió desde el año 1937 a diversos grupos de exiliados españoles, siendo los primeros un grupo de 500 niños denominados "los niños de Morelia", en alusión a la ciudad de México que los acogió.

Este monumento tiene su origen en una donación de la Asociación de Descendientes del Exilio Español, en agradecimiento a la cálida acogida que recibieron los exiliados españoles del pueblo mexicano.

Por último, comentar que el autor de esta escultura es Julián Martínez, quien fue uno de los niños exiliados españoles, y se inauguró en el año 1983.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Raúl Peñalver","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK20oIYuFwpwExn31O8Sz2BRCewwVlPbnB3ko3xbUJ_UHEVLQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Por ahí anda paseando la soberanía popular de España aún hoy.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Matteo Allegri","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUXbUaK-QYAR-ngKNRctF8GsNrx2fntAqNxYB3WYWq3Rrq1tcwAAw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Grande estatua que se encuentra en el punto más alto del parque","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Norlan Orozco","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUYcALmck_BYFTDzIO3qfFF3gCj4GEiXcQE5dWE6KMI1EBwE_sO=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Pi Dalpe","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIiuRQ6Ru5HAjK-9HIgbd6TBwavZ6rz8K1fYwcIXkyvMAe-=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Silent","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVMfd87lI2A2TJw5KirO1a-PCbTs-BBDwVaJXfk-t14WNzFJig=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""}]

address: P.º de la Castellana, 39, Salamanca, 28046 Madrid
open_hours: {"Friday":["Open 24 hours"],"Monday":["Open 24 hours"],"Saturday":["Open 24 hours"],"Sunday":["Open 24 hours"],"Thursday":["Open 24 hours"],"Tuesday":["Open 24 hours"],"Wednesday":["Open 24 hours"]}

[{"Name":"macedonboy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL7obIFVFelZpGZFWErr47Wzzrz8xLtHVDOYNGdpIt459EbUg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This monument stands in tribute to Emilio Castelar, a Spanish politician, and president of the First Spanish Republic. Not only was he a national leader, he was a noted writer and journalist and left a legacy in print of political science such as a history of the Republican Movement in Europe amongst other works.

The monument features a stone base with figures on all four sides. On the main side, facing southwards, is the bronze figure of Emilio Castelar, and at his feet lies a marble Venus, an allegory to the truth. Flanking him on either side are two classical public speakers, Cicero and Demosthenes who observe and listening to Castelar.

Like many great monuments in Madrid, this sits in the middle of a roundabout in a very busy road. I didn't even try to get closer. The monument is really out of the way of the main tourist areas. While it's definitely worth a look, only do so if you have a bit of time in Madrid","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE1Iabaw&fid=0x0:0x234303e313ffeb9d"],"When":"2019-4-22"},{"Name":"Kelloggs Frosties","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW9AE8vHKv8U3ElHYJOz7qZMynWG1jbYGPapp07kCS_RDg75Ys=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Located in the middle of a roundabout on the Paseo de la Castellana, this monument is dedicated to the historian, journalist, academic, public speaker and politician from Cadiz, Emilio Castelar Ripoll.

It is a complex work by Mariano Benlliure. It is a stone base that allows figures to be placed on each of its sides. On the main side, facing southwards, we can find the bronze figure of Emilio Castelar, and at his feet lies a marble Venus, an allegory to the truth, towards which the bronze figures of a worker, a soldier and a student rise, representing Work, Effort and Study. On the opposite side, there are two classical public speakers: Cicero and Demosthenes who observe and listen to Castelar. Finally, at the rear, there is a bronze relief dedicated to Castelar’s speeches in favour of the abolition of slavery in the former Spanish colonies.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Francisco Arteaga","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXNEsluXXj7svw9Md2msUadIeb26Fz6d4eNqB3ac_6CM9FnE81BOw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A must see. title: Monumento al Marqués del Duero
address: P.º de la Castellana, 70, Chamberí, 28046 Madrid

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"macedonboy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL7obIFVFelZpGZFWErr47Wzzrz8xLtHVDOYNGdpIt459EbUg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"This statue stands in tribute to Manuel Gutiérrez, the Marquess del Duero and a Spanish general during the Carlist War of succession to the Spanish throne. His is honoured for his victories that eventually lead to the defeat of the rebellion and restoration of the Bourbons to the Spanish throne.

This monument is an equestrian statue and depicts Marquess del Duero on horseback (obviously). That's about all I can say about it since the statue sits in the middle of a busy roundabout and I just didn't feel like getting run over for a better view, especially for someone who is likely unknown outside of Spain. Nothing wrong with the statue, but I wouldn't go out of the way.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE1Ib-rQE&fid=0x0:0x34b59cbdb431c889"],"When":"2019-4-22"},{"Name":"Cristian Carrero Roca","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUjruhZF37hpNlAGlHA2lb53Nh4Ou54GL3vwMRClIc0F5LEdFk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un monumento elegante y con mucho encanto, perfecto para descubrirlo paseando sin prisas. La estatua y el conjunto transmiten un aire clásico y solemne, con buenos detalles que se aprecian al acercarse. Me gustó especialmente cómo encaja en el entorno: es de esos puntos que dan carácter al lugar y que, aunque no seas un apasionado de la historia, te invitan a parar, mirar y hacer una foto.

Es una visita rápida pero agradable, ideal para completar una ruta por la zona y sumar un toque histórico al paseo. Si vas con buena luz, se disfruta aún más, porque la composición y el entorno hacen que el monumento luzca mucho.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Julio","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIWBBpBXlKk7PFYHuZWo-TDvrSDTT2o-LEU5Z_BZdCLGammIA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El Monumento al Marqués del Duero en Madrid es una escultura majestuosa que homenajea al militar Manuel Gutiérrez de la Concha. El caballo y el realismo de la figura destacan por su detalle, y la base muestra sus victorias más importantes. Ideal para amantes de la historia y la fotografía.

Dato curioso: Fue inaugurado en 1885 y es obra del escultor Andrés Aleu.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBI68t15q0eIylhq3jDXBhR&fid=0x0:0x34b59cbdb431c889"],"When":"2025-9-1"},{"Name":"Vivo Vázquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvUyRyXlZBRb-TT4BhrnwZ8T78FNkcDjPnfNdsrNdFZg9teg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un monumento ecuestre majestuoso dedicado a Manuel Gutiérrez de la Concha, Marqués del Duero, distinguido militar español del siglo XIX. La escultura representa con gran detalle y elegancia al general montado a caballo, transmitiendo la dignidad y el carácter de este importante personaje histórico. Ubicado en el Paseo de la Castellana, el monumento se integra perfectamente en uno de los ejes principales de Madrid, siendo un testimonio pétreo de la historia militar española. La calidad de la obra escultórica es notable, con una excelente ejecución de los detalles tanto del jinete como del caballo. Es un lugar ideal para los amantes de la historia y la fotografía, ofreciendo una oportunidad para conocer más sobre las figuras que marcaron el devenir de España. Un monumento que merece reconocimiento y visita dentro del patrimonio madrileño.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Luis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJx8P9QtFBqT70yfr20sndE3yc6uVKBLBLsJKBg7XJPrGKrGQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Estatua ecuestre clásica dedicada al general  Manuel Gutiérrez de la Concha e Irigoyen, general destacado en la lucha contra el carlismo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAeuNL39kHoukZCkfD4aXxO&fid=0x0:0x34b59cbdb431c889"],"When":"2025-11-22"},{"Name":"Doncelador","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXnewNEbYYaXdHD0epgVByIWzW2jKqBZMPTzU18-HYzM_sBuOGCqw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Obra del escultor Andrés Aleu en 1885, fué sufragada por suscripción nacional con un coste de 143.000 pesetas, la estatua se fundió en Sevilla con el bronce procedente de cañones donados por el cuerpo de artillería. El Marqués está vestido con traje de campaña y espada tirando de las tiendas con la mano izquierda y levantando la derecha indicando un objetivo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICktbeUNQ&fid=0x0:0x34b59cbdb431c889"],"When":"2018-11-21"},{"Name":"Victoria Senaty","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXDqI6YcQt-mtmIGFFLGaGy-ebJ3tEwmyjjrN0PDXKEpEamT1h0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"En homenaje a este ilustre, que fue también político 5 veces presidente del senado; como luchador en las guerras carlistas, y por lo tanto isabelino. Haciendo esta defensa llegó a ser capitán general.
La Reina Isabel II le otorgó este título de marquesado. Era también buen empresario, tuvo muchos cultivos de caña de azúcar en Málaga, San Pedro Alcantara.
Por esta época no solo el don de la palabra valía, que hizo buen uso de ello, sino que hacia falta destreza en el campo de batalla, hablamos de finales del 1800, que había que lidiar con cercana Portugal, para conquistar terrenos. En el monumento ecuestre señala con el brazo a la Castellana?","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICKgM3cuAE&fid=0x0:0x34b59cbdb431c889"],"When":"2021-3-24"},{"Name":"Domingo González Gálvez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKO2KxJdpJVDbrQSvbdw4QaNKZSuSbFt8HzF9XA7rpbrUBWGHw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Bonita escultura en el paseo de la castellana
Foto y reseña en agosto del 2025","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBUEkwk7du3fd4bOM8IIi5m&fid=0x0:0x34b59cbdb431c889"],"When":"2025-8-6"}]

user_reviews: [{"Name":"Don B","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVGcsUF_MoUvMreMVUqweJtinMVrtfSxmQTM9avT1cQfFth5jsZvg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very beautiful monument.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICZjeOkAg\u0026fid=0x0:0x8d5c1885193d2623"],"When":"2023-9-15"},{"Name":"Endika A","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjURoXt1jCauAIFLaHHF4awkzC9ssYuTj1Gr6oYudPMKSxYxqSCHrg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Awesome place","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Santos Muñoz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUfD-h44RfDhLF_p8-4LKi19Q5yl05Z4oqG-BLPwnygLxbfnZ4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El Monumento a los Hermanos Álvarez Quintero, situado en el Parque del Retiro, es un homenaje elegante y lleno de simbolismo a los célebres dramaturgos sevillanos. La escultura transmite ese aire teatral y costumbrista que definió su obra, con un conjunto artístico muy cuidado y lleno de matices.\n\nMe gustó especialmente porque no es solo una estatua aislada: el entorno verde del parque le da un marco perfecto, convirtiéndolo en una parada tranquila y cultural en medio del paseo. Es de esos lugares donde te detienes, lees la placa, y conectas con una parte importante de la historia literaria española.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Francisco Lucena","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXXAnbPEgO38IAgd5mIrYHf4QXKfnMypv1751f6z9U8cL9E_HQHDQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El monumento a los hermanos Álvarez Quintero fue inaugurado en 1934 es una obra de piedra,mármol y bronce ubicada en la avenida de Colombia,fue iniciada su construcción por el escultor Lorenzo coullaut Valera y finalizado por su hijo Federico coullaut tras la muerte del primero e incluye una figura femenina y un jinete andaluz.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBoAbKD_gn4dXsthNottCbI\u0026fid=0x0:0x8d5c1885193d2623"],"When":"2025-10-28"},{"Name":"Vivo Vázquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvUyRyXlZBRb-TT4BhrnwZ8T78FNkcDjPnfNdsrNdFZg9teg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un hermoso homenaje a Serafín y Joaquín Álvarez Quintero, los célebres dramaturgos andaluces que enriquecieron el teatro español con sus obras costumbristas. El monumento se encuentra en una ubicación privilegiada dentro del Parque del Retiro, rodeado de naturaleza y tranquilidad. La escultura captura la esencia de estos dos hermanos inseparables que trabajaron siempre en colaboración, creando comedias que reflejaban la vida andaluza con humor y sensibilidad. El entorno arbolado del Retiro hace que la visita sea especialmente placentera, ideal para hacer una pausa reflexiva durante un paseo por el parque. Un reconocimiento merecido a estos maestros del género chico español.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Luchi Dorian","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLcKqWzESzn-2VaRilfxcCKlTM5pk91sY9DvaLVLhEh96Z5Qw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El Monumento a los Hermanos Alvarez Quintero es una bella obra escultorica dedicada a los famosos dramaturgos sevillanos Serafin y Joaquin Alvarez Quintero. Inaugurado en 1934, este monumento de piedra, marmol y bronce fue creado por el escultor Lorenzo Coullaut Valera. La composicion incluye figuras alegoricas que representan el espiritu del teatro andaluz y la gracia de sus obras costumbristas. Ubicado en un tranquilo rincon del Parque del Retiro, es un lugar encantador para descansar y disfrutar del arte al aire libre. Un homenaje merecido a dos figuras fundamentales del teatro espanol.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tito Rubio","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUQxmo6mQaJ-jpXRWak2CdDTM5wjRUdpYxV0TDYJj1nb75_p6dZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Inaugurado en 1934, fue iniciado por el escultor Lorenzo Coullaut Valera y finalizado por su hijo Federico tras su muerte, en 1932. La composición configura una plaza circular, contando con un banco corrido adaptado a esta geometría, que se interrumpe en su centro para levantar un triple hueco. En los vanos laterales se sitúan jarrones decorativos y en el central una elegante e idealizada figura femenina de cuerpo entero y vestida a la andaluza. Fuera de esta composición se levanta la escultura de un hombre a caballo sobre un pedestal macizo y prismático, también vestido a la andaluza. Fuera de esta escena, en el centro de la simulada plaza, se encuentra una fuente de doble vaso y un surtidor central.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC1ktusswE\u0026fid=0x0:0x8d5c1885193d2623"],"When":"2023-12-26"},{"Name":"Jörge","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWXeWEXqdr5t3OlbRokb_Dhsg38mDGwuZo9JWUkx-spF0MuHs0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Monumento inaugurado en el año 1934 y realizado por el escultor Lorenzo Coullaut Valera y tras su muerte por su hijo Federico Coullaut en honor a los hermanos Álvarez Quintero. El conjunto está formado por una balconada en la que se asoma una mujer con traje andaluz, y en la parte posterior un jinete, sirviendo de reconocimiento especial a Sevilla, escenario escogido por los comediógrafos para muchas de sus obras","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDtqoOjbg\u0026fid=0x0:0x8d5c1885193d2623"],"When":"2024-2-3"}]

address: C. Alcalá, 559, San Blas-Canillejas, 28027 Madrid

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Marcos Cabrera García","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWyOBiJvQHy8TN38eB5aq8FnksAylW07cNTllWUTeQp5Q6m66Sb=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Monumento al torero "Manolete"

En la calle de Alcalá, junto a las tapias de la finca Torre Arias, y ante un jardinillo municipal, se emplaza la estatua en bronce del célebre torero cordobés Manuel Rodríguez Sánchez "Manolete" (1917-1947), levantada en 1997 en conmemoración del cincuentenario de su muerte en Linares (Jaén) a causa de la cornada de un toro de Miura.
La estatua es del escultor Luis Sanguino, artista discípulo de Federico Coullaut-Valera, que obtuvo un temprano triunfo en 1952 al ganar junto a Antonio Martín el concurso nacional para los guerreros dolientes del Valle de los Caídos. Tras residir largas temporadas en Estados Unidos y México, volvió definitivamente a España en 1986, habiendo realizado numerosas piezas escultóricas repartidas por todo Madrid, como el bajorrelieve del Encierro y los monumentos al Yiyo y a Antonio Bienvenida junto a la plaza de toros de Las Ventas, las puertas y los doce Apóstoles que rodean el tambor de la catedral de la Almudena, y la fuente del Homenaje a la Mujer y los monumentos a Cervantes, al siglo XXI y de nuevo al Yiyo en el propio distrito de San Blas.

Autores
SANGUINO, Luis (Escultor)

Fecha
1997","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICxgryRHw&fid=0x0:0x8f0023bda354ced9"],"When":"2023-5-13"},{"Name":"Doncelador","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXnewNEbYYaXdHD0epgVByIWzW2jKqBZMPTzU18-HYzM_sBuOGCqw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Obra del escultor Luis Sanguino en. 1.997, está situada en la calle Alcalá junto a la Quinta de Torre Arias. La escultura es de cuerpo entero realizada en bronce, representa al torero #Manolete vestido de luces con los brazos en alto portando en ambas manos trofeos taurinos y en actitud desafiante.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICktYeh_wE&fid=0x0:0x8f0023bda354ced9"],"When":"2019-5-19"},{"Name":"Adoracion Rodriguez Garcia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXdzl2HDBAVthkL0YEZePFrLiQMBCASTOfXdvWSSDbf2izV_6wu=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Monumento al famoso torero Manolete en Madrid. Manuel Laureano Rodríguez Sánchez, más conocido como Manolete (Córdoba, 4 de julio de 1917-Linares, Jaén, 29 de agosto de 1947), fue uno de los grandes toreros de la historia de la tauromaquia de España en la década de 1940. Murió en el Hospital de San José y San Raimundo de Linares. Tras la profunda cornada mortal que le asestó el Miura Islero en la plaza de toros de Linares se convirtió en un mito de la España de la posguerra.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDh5c2WdQ&fid=0x0:0x8f0023bda354ced9"],"When":"2023-3-13"},{"Name":"JUAN GARCÍA","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXeFoy7VWXizC9nnTwPRgL6XIZE0miK2VXhMizX2UI4vMoCnR8J=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Espectaculares estatua en homenaje al fantástico torero Manolete. user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Marcos Cabrera García","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWyOBiJvQHy8TN38eB5aq8FnksAylW07cNTllWUTeQp5Q6m66Sb=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Monumento al Almirante Miguel Grau

El busto fue erigido el 27 de julio de 1984 por el Ayuntamiento de Madrid para conmemorar el 150 aniversario del nacimiento del almirante peruano Miguel Grau, quien luchó en la Guerra del Pacífico conocida como de los Nitratos y librada en el año 1879 entre tropas peruanas y chilenas. La victoria fue de estas últimas, superiores en número y medios, si bien encontraron una gran resistencia en el buque peruano Huascar, liderado por el almirante Grau, finalmente vencido en octubre de 1879 con el hundimiento de su nave frente a las costas de Punta Angamos y su consiguiente fallecimiento, hecho al que hace referencia la placa que acompaña a la escultura. Ésta se encuentra ubicada en una zona baja del Parque Norte, en el que se localizan varios monumentos dedicados a diversas personalidades hispanoamericanas.

ROLDÁN PASCUAL, Joaquín (Arquitecto)

En la base emerge del suelo una punta piramidal como símbolo de resistencia. La ejecución de la escultura y del monumento tuvo un coste de 10 millones de pesetas, de los cuales ocho fueron invertidos por el Ayuntamiento y dos por la Comunidad de Madrid.
El monumento se inaugura en marzo de 1985.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDE5KrcpwE&fid=0x0:0x3597519d46babcb3"],"When":"2017-9-2"},{"Name":"Rosas en Almíbar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUfUFWIA5U-cA6nDO86JQTULSIPZDT--I2zgfbxJTK8QTObvFY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"No está mal. Un poco soso y feo. La iniciativa es bonita, pero a Miguel Hernández casi ni se ve.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDeu9bOgwE&fid=0x0:0x3597519d46babcb3"],"When":"2022-10-20"},{"Name":"José Julio Melgares","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVPlI3_WxZ-JbiFDSG6A1n_aKR_BFRpvuYb0hLgejNuccGQun5Z=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Monumento en homenaje al poeta alicantino Miguel Hernández obra del arquitecto Enrique Dominguez Uceta. Destaca un medallón en bronce, realizado por el escultor Miguel Ángel López Calleja, con la cara del poeta que es una reproducción del retrato que le hizo Antonio Buero Vallejo en 1940, cuando estuvieron encarcelados juntos.

"MADRID... esta ciudad no se aplaca con fuego,
este laurel con rencor no se tala.
Este rosal sin ventura, este espliego...
júbilo exhala"
Miguel Hernández

Miguel Hernández escribió un precioso poema dedicado a Federico García Lorca, tras la muerte del poeta granadino, con el que había tenido una lucha de egos:

"Qué sencilla es la muerte qué sencilla
pero qué injustamente arrebatada.
No sabe andar despacio y acuchilla
cuando menos se espera su turbia cuchillada"

Miguel Hernández","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE84Pq5AE&fid=0x0:0x3597519d46babcb3"],"When":"2017-12-22"},{"Name":"Arturo Villacañas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJFDq7wmxmLUqgAL1EUzdv1pu_WF5qpBvDo_51XwQ27UIEKo5Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Monumento difícil de evitar si se viene caminando desde la Casa de Campo. Se levanta en honor a Miguel Hernández, poeta de la Generación del 36, quien fue separado de su familia e impedido de ver crecer a sus hijos después de ser arrestado por el franquismo con 28 años, debido a sus "simpatías con ideas anti-fascistas".

Amigo de sus amigos (nos conmociona especialmente la "Elegía" que dedicó a la muerte de su íntimo amigo Ramón Sijé); pastor de ovejas de profesión; autodidacta que nunca necesitó de grandes credenciales para aprender a leer por su cuenta y firmar una de las mejores producciones poéticas en castellano.

Moriría en la cárcel de tuberculosis con apenas 32 años. Esto, unido a las penosas condiciones de tortura física y psicológica a la que sus captores lo sometieron durante meses, lo convirtieron en una importante figura de la libertad de expresión y las garantías procesales en España, ya bastante antes de la caída del Régimen (cuando España todavía era una dictadura, hace escasos 46 años). Hernández recuerda a lo peor de Franco; la insensibilidad vulgar del nacionalismo, la inexcusable entrega a sus ideales totalitarios, y su desprecio al significado real y concreto de las causas de "compasión" y "protección de la familia" con las que originalmente justificó su pronunciamiento militar.

El monumento, obra del arquitecto Enrique Domínguez y realizado en piedra pulida, se compone de cuatro columnatas de piedra en forma de V, y un pilar central con medallón que refleja el grabado facial realizado por Antonio Buero Vallejo durante sus visitas a la cárcel. Además, en su parte inferior este pilar se abre para adoptar la forma de un triángulo invertido 🔻, que es el símbolo con el que se "marcaba" a los prisioneros comunistas en los campos de concentración del nazismo (que ahora ha quedado resignificado a símbolo de los perseguidos por ideas políticas).

Todo el complejo escultórico se conserva relativamente bien y se integra perfectamente en un escenario, la Casa de Campo, que todavía alberga fosas comunes de "desaparecidos" y muertos sin nombre, asesinados durante el asalto a Madrid en la Guerra Civil española.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"JPB Auteur","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVv0_EgnAIWUQHeX31_sSm5IZQi5QrXWitM_fwfM8xy5NbB1xWg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Monument dédié au poète Miguel Hernández. 0 voir aussi le parc qui est magnifique","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC41-7TNA&fid=0x0:0x3597519d46babcb3"],"When":"2017-11-25"},{"Name":"Lucy Isabel De Los Reyes Tovar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW7XRhTemAolYyAV4u1hspfARbNwnggRBmZb0EEKNuXgQVNxdK1dw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Los monumento se levantan como recordatorio, de aquellas personas que han pasado y han dejado huella.
