title: Monument to Christopher Columbus
address: Pl. de Colón, Centro, 28001 Madrid
open_hours: {"Friday":["Open 24 hours"],"Monday":["Open 24 hours"],"Saturday":["Open 24 hours"],"Sunday":["Open 24 hours"],"Thursday":["Open 24 hours"],"Tuesday":["Open 24 hours"],"Wednesday":["Open 24 hours"]}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Mark M","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUS9xWFdqeoIGM48SlGzqRbIUYckO5GoiKaVUECu7qOA5BWOej9UQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Monumento a Cristóbal Colón stands as a majestic sentinel in the heart of Madrid, anchoring the vibrant Plaza de Colón with its neogothic elegance. Designed by Arturo Mélida and sculpted by Jerónimo Suñol, this 17-meter-tall landmark is a masterpiece of late 19th-century artistry. The intricate white marble statue of Columbus, draped in a cloak and holding the flag of Castile, gazes eastward, evoking a sense of timeless adventure and maritime legacy.
What makes a visit truly special is the seamless blend of history and modern urban beauty. The monument is situated within the Jardines del Descubrimiento, where the roar of the massive cascading fountain provides a soothing soundtrack to the city’s bustle. Standing at its base, you can appreciate the intricate carvings on the pedestal that detail the famous voyages.
The surrounding area is a cultural treasure trove; the square also hosts the world’s largest Spanish flag and the fascinating "Julia" sculpture by Jaume Plensa. Whether you are a history enthusiast or simply looking for a picturesque spot to relax between visits to the nearby National Archaeological Museum and the upscale shops of Serrano, the Columbus Monument is an essential, uplifting stop on any Madrid itinerary.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCp8sd7f9KzUU38ZsT7oiCW&fid=0x0:0x33d1b8829355e4a"],"When":"2025-12-23"},{"Name":"snarkyguy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUpLEkMbqQ117vEXR5NomvRhhZgkwVvVtKfHyjAnrI4JKwbnhs2=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Super detailed monument in a cool part of town. It sits opposite the Bronze statue of a Fat Lady by Fernando Botero and is really cool to view at sundown. Every angle of this square looks good and is well-displayed. The monument itself is on a tall dais with outstretched arms near Centro Colon.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICdjtqvMg&fid=0x0:0x33d1b8829355e4a"],"When":"2024-2-15"},{"Name":"macedonboy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL7obIFVFelZpGZFWErr47Wzzrz8xLtHVDOYNGdpIt459EbUg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"This monument stands in tribute to Christopher Columbus, the explorer who discovered the Americas for Spain. The monument consists of a statue of columbus atop a 17m high plinth which is built in the Gothic style. It's located in the centre of a roundabout in a very busy road and because it's so high up and so far from the pedestrian pavement, it's pretty difficult to get a good view of it.

One good thing about it's location is that the Torre Colon provides an interesting backdrop if taking photos from Plaza Colon. It's good to see the statue for it's historical value, but thumbs down for the difficulty in getting good views of the monument.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE1IaT5AE&fid=0x0:0x33d1b8829355e4a"],"When":"2019-4-22"},{"Name":"George Messinia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLCxA8R_mO15QqKHTo8jPP0_YjV-KgANNdlMxoMmbA5sKi-qQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Columbus Square was originally called 'Plaza de Santiago', but was renamed in 1893 in honor of the famous explorer who discovered America. The square is dominated by the statue of Columbus, Monumento a Cristóbal Colón, standing on a tall column, looking in the direction of the new world. The square is also home to the Jardines del Descubrimiento, a park first opened in 1970 where you can admire various sculptures as well as a monument dedicated to the discovery of America. Just in front of Columbus Square is the National Archaeological Museum of Spain and the National Library.
On the north side of the square are the twin skyscrapers Torres de Colón which are two 117m high office skyscrapers with 23 floors. They were built between 1967 and 1976 by architect Antonio Lamela. Today the towers are being renovated.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICJudW1Ng&fid=0x0:0x33d1b8829355e4a"],"When":"2023-6-30"},{"Name":"Seokjin Ham","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIhtf6A7X2UDP--Xi1vhwXxfhLJk0GUh_pTYJvgqrBOTATbUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It is a neo-Gothic monument built between 1881 and 1885. [{"Name":"Jai Arr","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW6oGFmfWzsps2Ux3EaJ9qeSYQGJp6-IDoJdjmuF679ew0iuukA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"As an Pinoy I had to check it out. It’s a great monument for Dr Rizal.  Commemorating the diplomatic relations between the Spanish Kingdom and the Republic of the Philippines.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCg0pHJlQE&fid=0x0:0xc5da4a1a6d075063"],"When":"2025-2-15"},{"Name":"Bon Salinas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjViqqrKvJioc0TQjAxptdC16_YoIS5bL7tUuTDNTs7DuGFRKzhUIg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"**A Monument of Reconciliation: Spain Honors José Rizal in Madrid**

In a powerful gesture of reconciliation and historical reflection, the Spanish government has unveiled a monument dedicated to José Protasio Rizal, the Philippines' national hero, in the heart of Madrid. This act of recognition comes over a century after Rizal's execution by Spanish colonial authorities in 1896, a moment that marked a turning point in the Philippines' struggle for independence.

The monument, a striking bronze sculpture of Rizal, stands tall in a prominent Madrid plaza, symbolizing a bridge between two nations with a shared, albeit complex, history. Rizal, a polymath, writer, and revolutionary, advocated for reform and peaceful resistance against colonial oppression. His writings, particularly *Noli Me Tangere* and *El Filibusterismo*, exposed the injustices of Spanish rule and inspired a movement for freedom.

The Spanish government's decision to honor Rizal reflects a commitment to acknowledging the past while fostering a spirit of mutual respect and understanding. Spanish officials emphasized that the monument is not just a tribute to Rizal's legacy but also a recognition of the enduring ties between Spain and the Philippines. "This monument is a symbol of our shared history and a step toward healing," said a spokesperson during the unveiling ceremony.

For Filipinos, the monument is a poignant reminder of Rizal's enduring influence and a testament to the power of forgiveness and progress. It also serves as a reminder that history, though often painful, can be a foundation for building stronger, more respectful relationships.

As the sun sets over Madrid, the monument to José Rizal stands as a beacon of hope, reminding us that even the most divided histories can find common ground in the pursuit of truth and reconciliation.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDAlNj81QE&fid=0x0:0xc5da4a1a6d075063"],"When":"2025-2-9"},{"Name":"Chelsea Janes","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVi5RYgdJVjosZ1bfo8yRHkVR5BmsCP-H6gForcoflp1a0oA-8B=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very interesting to see Rizal in Spain, especially since it was the Spanish colonists who murdered him. Obviously, though, there is no mention of that anywhere on the monument... ugh. Still, glad this Filipino hero is finally being recognized by the Spanish, even if it was nearly 100 years after they assassinated him...","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICfp_jazwE&fid=0x0:0xc5da4a1a6d075063"],"When":"2025-1-2"},{"Name":"Mocmoc P","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUzTfPVUkeKNOWiuxPr3HITdaAOuQcK7LhUSFGEwliKKM8XWGfG=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It was a sunny Sunday afternoon in September 2023 when I finally visited the Jose Rizal Monument in Madrid. I had been wanting to see it for a long time, as I am a Filipino and Jose Rizal is our national hero. I highly admire him for his courage, his intellect, and his commitment to fighting for freedom and justice.

The monument is located in the Parque de Santander, a small park in the Chamberí district of Madrid. It is a replica of the Rizal Monument in Manila.

I left the monument feeling inspired and uplifted. I knew that I would never forget my visit to this place.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICZzrTSZQ&fid=0x0:0xc5da4a1a6d075063"],"When":"2023-9-10"},{"Name":"Tristan Dwyte","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWNU-MmGLiN_irpQ8N6sJKS4R7F3MdDG2ibBmdnaiG0IEIZzdY0=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This is a must-see for every Filipino who visits Madrid. It’s are reminder how our National Hero and his legacy for independence have come a long way from the bitter events of history.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Londoner R","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXi_4XSDCHP3WzP-Y1dw4U6wOZ78VcsWT9ebtFENvxONLFho0C0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"National hero of the Philippines. He sacrificed a lot, even his life, to better the lives of his countrymen.
This monument is open to the public 24 hours a day. It is a replica of the monument in Luneta Park in Manila.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDhpuOYDA&fid=0x0:0xc5da4a1a6d075063"],"When":"2023-3-7"},{"Name":"alexander valera","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK-nzGxVAVwlxNwSNOT_l-7YiLO-H30yjuaGYuC6cxJsV0Y=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The place is properly maintained even though he was a foreign dignitaries , the place also is in a great location.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEMvzuo3Dm5rnWg&fid=0x0:0xc5da4a1a6d075063"],"When":"2025-6-5"},{"Name":"Jonas Paolo Corpuz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUuysJsvIT0DW9ifKZXv_YK_83dknaFp680GAZc6V0bkkAIsf0e=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A must visit for every Filipino. While not atypical, the statue is slightly unusual for having the general sitting on a prancing horse. The monument is a bit out of the way and not that close to the tourist circuit in Madrid. I wouldn't recommended going out of the way to see it unless already planning to visit the Parque del Oeste.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICM2oHf0wE\u0026fid=0x0:0x5dc97e96e814d6ca"],"When":"2019-8-12"},{"Name":"Angie Amboni","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWNCvvOhPikXDHhGSqxeHBil2Xgekh13uvEvt_b3pneofjTvQXdfw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a beautiful  place","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Vivo Vázquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvUyRyXlZBRb-TT4BhrnwZ8T78FNkcDjPnfNdsrNdFZg9teg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un monumento imponente que honra al General José de San Martín, el gran libertador de Argentina, Chile y Perú. La estatua ecuestre captura la figura heroica de este prócer sudamericano que jugó un papel fundamental en la emancipación de América del Sur. Su ubicación en el Parque del Oeste proporciona un marco natural apropiado para reflexionar sobre los lazos históricos y culturales entre España y las naciones hermanas de América Latina. El monumento está muy bien conservado y la figura del general se presenta con dignidad y autoridad. Es un punto de encuentro importante para la comunidad argentina en Madrid y un símbolo de los fuertes vínculos que nos unen. Muestra al general vestido con uniforme militar y en postura de saludar con la mano derecha. El óvalo de la glorieta, rodeado de grandes plátanos, enmarca adecuadamente al monumento y crea un remate interesante a los tres paseos y acceso frontal que confluyen en él.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC1yr7Afw\u0026fid=0x0:0x5dc97e96e814d6ca"],"When":"2023-12-27"},{"Name":"Hernán Gutierrez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXChmrOJID08gziXu5fImCUtbdd7d7axyCFKm8AUjfy59_CjAjN=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Hermosa la estatua del Padre de la Patria Argentina, disfruté mucho la visita. Socialista que luchó por la verdad, lo bueno y la justicia. Enseñó realmente la gran brecha que había entre ricos y pobles, intentando paliar esa diferencia.
La guerra civil y la dictadura de Franco se encargo de dinamitar los valores humanos de muchos españoles, los herederos de los ganadores añoran los años horribles en los que el país era solo de ellos y lo pasan muy mal por no tener ahora esos privilegios.

Decano de los Abogados del Estado, llegó a ser Fiscal General de la República, cargo para el que había sido nombrado por Niceto Alcalá Zamora el 16 de noviembre de 1935 a propuesta del Ministerio de Justicia, tanto por su prestigio como jurista, como por el hecho de merecer toda la confianza del Gobierno de la República. Pues no debemos olvidar que Don Niceto Alcalá Zamora había sido el primer presidente del Gobierno Provisional y finalmente llegó también a ser presidente de la Segunda República Española.

Marcelino Valentín Gamazo estaba casado con Narcisa Fernández Navarro de los Paños, con la que tuvo nueve hijos. Su esposa poseía fincas en Rubielos Altos, en la provincia de Cuenca, aunque la familia vivía en Madrid donde él ejercía su profesión. Su actuación profesional más notable, en el ejercicio del cometido que le correspondía como Fiscal General de la República, fue en la causa emprendida por el Tribunal Supremo contra el socialista Francisco Largo Caballero (el llamado Lenín Español) como principal responsable -instigador y organizador- del golpe de estado contra la “república burguesa” que la historiografía denomina “La Revolución de Octubre de 1934”.
En dicha causa, Valentín Gamazo había pedido para Largo Caballero -cumpliendo el cometido que le correspondía como Fiscal General de la República- una pena de 30 años de reclusión, que era la que le correspondía al ser acusado de rebelión militar. Pues la “Revolución de Octubre” había sido un alzamiento en armas contra la República que causó más de mil muertos, siendo necesario el empleo del Ejército para sofocarla. Absuelto Largo Caballero merced a las presiones ejercidas sobre el Tribunal Supremo, y muy especialmente ante el temor a la reacción de las milicias armadas del PSOE, si resultaba condenado. Por ello Valentín Gamazo presentó su dimisión, al haber quedado probado en el juicio la participación y responsabilidad de Largo Caballero como instigador y organizador de la Revolución de Octubre contra la República.
En el mes de julio, dando por hecho como toda persona bien informada que el enfrentamiento era inevitable, bien por una sublevación militar o por una insurrección revolucionaria, pensó que estaría más seguro en Rubielos Altos en la casa y finca de su esposa Narcisa Fernández Navarro de los Paños a donde se trasladó con toda su familia.
El 5 de agosto de 1936, cuando se encontraban en el interior de la vivienda los tres hijos mayores del matrimonio, José Antonio, Francisco Javier y Luís Gonzaga Valentín Fernández, jugando al frontón, se presentó un grupo de diez o doce milicianos en una camioneta requisada. Tras penetrar en el patio de la vivienda, manifestaron que habían venido para llevarlos a Albacete a declarar. En principio los hermanos se resistían a hacerlo, pero el padre, Marcelino Valentín Gamazo, como correspondía a un notable jurista que había sido nada menos que fiscal general de la República, les ordenó que obedecieran. Pues al no tener ningún cargo contra ellos, tras tomarles declaración quedarían en libertad.
Pero en cuanto se alejaron del pueblo los ataron, y tras vejarlos y torturarlos durante todo el día, los asesinaron al día siguiente en un olivar situado en el margen derecho de la carretera que va de Tébar al Picazo. Y fueron tan canallas que los asesinaron de menor a mayor. Primero al pequeño Luis Gonzaga, de 17 años. Luego a Francisco Javier de 20. Después al mayor, José Antonio de 21 años… y finalmente al padre, al que obligaron a presenciar la muerte de sus tres hijos.
